text: api.add_route() accepted *args: API.add_route() previously accepted *args but now no longer does.

min_value, max_value: get_param_as_int keyword arguments were renamed: min → min_value, max → max_value to avoid shadowing built-in names.

text: Request.if_match and Request.if_none_match now return a list of falcon.ETag objects instead of the raw header string value.
Setting Response.etag now wraps the value with double-quotes if not already present, to comply with RFC 7232.

included charset parameter in content-type: The default error serializer no longer sets the charset parameter in the Content-Type header, which may break test assertions that check the exact Content-Type value.
falcon.DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE, falcon.MEDIA_JSON, the default media_type for falcon.API, and default_media_type options no longer include the charset parameter.
Request.context_type now defaults to a bare class; the dict-style interface (req.context['key']) is deprecated in favor of attribute access (req.context.key).
Response.context_type now defaults to a bare class instead of dict; use resp.context.cache_strategy = 'lru' instead of resp.context['cache_strategy'] = 'lru'.
def handle_error(ex, req, resp, params): Custom error handlers now receive (req, resp, ex, params) instead of (ex, req, resp, params).
response.content_length property: Stop using stream_len on Response; use content_length or set_stream instead.

deserialize(raw) and serialize() without content_type: Media handler deserialize() now takes stream, content_type, content_length instead of a single raw argument; serialize() now receives a content_type argument.
Request.if_match and Request.if_none_match now return a list of falcon.ETag objects instead of the raw header string.
content-type header no longer includes charset parameter: The default error serializer no longer sets the charset parameter in the Content-Type header for error responses.

meta.symbol: content-type header no longer includes charset parameter

text: falcon.DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE, falcon.MEDIA_JSON, and the default media_type in API, RequestOptions, and ResponseOptions no longer include the charset parameter.

On Python 3, CaseInsensitiveDict now inherits from collections.abc.MutableMapping instead of collections.MutableMapping; code that relies on the old MRO or isinstance checks against collections.MutableMapping may break.
Setting Response.etag now wraps the value in double-quotes (if not already present) to comply with RFC 7232; code that manually adds quotes or compares etag values may break.

header values forced to str on python 2: On Python 2, simulate_request now forces header values to str as required by PEP-3333; passing non-str header values that were previously accepted will now be coerced or may behave differently.

meta.symbol: header values forced to str on python 2

text: Custom error serializers are now called for all HTTPError instances, including those where has_representation is False (e.g. subclasses of NoRepresentation); previously they were skipped.
Stop using Python 3.4; Falcon 3.0 requires Python 3.5+ (WSGI) or 3.6+ (ASGI).
Stop using CPython 2.7 or PyPy2.7; Falcon 3.0 removed Python 2 support entirely.
Stop using OptionalRepresentation; the default error serializer now always generates a representation for every HTTPError subclass.
Stop using has_representation; all HTTPError subclasses now always have a representation generated by the default serializer.
Stop using NoRepresentation; all HTTPError subclasses now have a media type representation.
falcon now clears any previously set response body before handling any raised exception: Any response body set before an exception is raised will now be cleared before the error handler runs.

meta.symbol: falcon now clears any previously set response body before handling any raised exception

text: default user-agent header was 'curl/7.24.0 (x86_64-apple-darwin12.0)': Stop relying on the default User-Agent being 'curl/7.24.0...'; it is now 'falcon-client/{version}' unless you override falcon.testing.helpers.DEFAULT_UA.
http_version keyword argument (value is just version number, no 'http/' prefix): Stop using protocol=; use http_version= and pass only the version number without 'HTTP/' prefix.

meta.symbol: http_version keyword argument (value is just version number, no 'http/' prefix)

text: root_path keyword argument: Stop using app=; use root_path= instead.
writeable property: Stop using .writeable; use .writable instead.

remaining middleware process_response methods will no longer execute before bubbling up: If an error handler raises an exception that is not HTTPStatus or HTTPError, remaining middleware process_response methods are skipped before the exception reaches the web server.

meta.symbol: remaining middleware process_response methods will no longer execute before bubbling up

text: no longer accepts floats (method deprecated): Stop passing floats to falcon.get_http_status; it no longer accepts them and the function itself is deprecated.
accepted a single middleware object: Stop passing a single middleware object to prepare_middleware; the argument must be an iterable.
now includes remote_addr as last element if not already present in checked headers: access_route now appends remote_addr as the last element if it isn't already in the route from headers.

meta.symbol: now includes remote_addr as last element if not already present in checked headers

text: default error handler generates http 500, middleware process_response still called: Unhandled exceptions now generate an HTTP 500 response via a default error handler instead of being raised to the web server, and middleware process_response methods are still called.

meta.symbol: default error handler generates http 500, middleware process_response still called

text: handlers matched by most specific exception class via mro: Exception handlers are now resolved by the most specific matching exception class (MRO) instead of reverse registration order.

meta.symbol: handlers matched by most specific exception class via mro

text: trailing slash only removed when option is enabled, routes should be added without trailing slash: When strip_url_path_trailing_slash is enabled, routes must now be added without a trailing slash; the old bug that always stripped it is fixed.

meta.symbol: trailing slash only removed when option is enabled, routes should be added without trailing slash

text: exceptions wrapped as mediamalformederror rendered as 400: Exceptions raised by media handlers are now wrapped as MediaMalformedError and rendered as a 400 Bad Request response.

meta.symbol: exceptions wrapped as mediamalformederror rendered as 400

text: re-raises the same exception on subsequent calls (unless medianotfounderror with default): Subsequent calls to get_media or media after an error re-raise the same exception instead of returning None.

meta.symbol: re-raises the same exception on subsequent calls (unless medianotfounderror with default)

text: Stop relying on falcon.Response.data to serialize media; use falcon.Response.render_body() instead to get the HTTP response body that accounts for text, data, and media.

meta.symbol: falcon.response.data used to also check and serialize falcon.response.media

text: from falcon.util import json: Stop importing json from falcon.util; it is deprecated.
Stop referencing old private attribute names on falcon.media.JSONHandler; they have been renamed.
Stop referencing old private attributes on falcon.media.MessagePackHandler; they have been replaced.
Stop using falcon.media.Handlers.find_by_media_type(); it is deprecated and may be removed in a future release.

When no route matches, the framework now raises HTTPRouteNotFound instead of plain HTTPNotFound, so custom error handlers that catch HTTPNotFound may not catch the new subclass unless they catch the parent.
Stop using RequestOptions.auto_parse_form_urlencoded; use URLEncodedFormHandler instead. The old option is deprecated.

compiledrouter stripped trailing slashes from uri templates, making `/path` and `/path/` map to the same route: Stop relying on CompiledRouter stripping trailing slashes; routes with and without trailing slashes are now distinct.

meta.symbol: compiledrouter stripped trailing slashes from uri templates, making `/path` and `/path/` map to the same route

text: when auto_parse_qs_csv was enabled, `t=1,2&t=3,4` produced `['1', '2', '3,4']` (only split first occurrence): Stop relying on auto_parse_qs_csv only splitting the first occurrence of a repeated query parameter; all occurrences are now split on commas.

meta.symbol: when auto_parse_qs_csv was enabled, `t=1,2&t=3,4` produced `['1', '2', '3,4']` (only split first occurrence)

text: falcon.uri.encode and encode_value did not re-escape already-escaped percent characters: Stop relying on falcon.uri.encode and encode_value preserving already-escaped percent characters; they now double-escape them by default. Use encode_check_escaped or encode_value_check_escaped to retain old behavior.

meta.symbol: falcon.uri.encode and encode_value did not re-escape already-escaped percent characters

text: falcon.uri.decode and parse_query_string could explode quadratically for many percent-encoded characters: Stop relying on these functions being slow for large numbers of percent-encoded characters; they are now linear time.

meta.symbol: falcon.uri.decode and parse_query_string could explode quadratically for many percent-encoded characters

text: parse_query_string('') did not return {}: Stop relying on parse_query_string('') returning something other than an empty dict; it now returns {}.

response serialization errors (faulty media handler, httpunsupportedmediatype) bubbled up to the application server: Stop relying on response serialization errors propagating to the application server; they are now caught and handled internally like other responder exceptions.

meta.symbol: response serialization errors (faulty media handler, httpunsupportedmediatype) bubbled up to the application server

text: forwarded_host did not include port when proxy headers were not set: Stop relying on forwarded_host omitting the port when not behind a proxy; it now includes the port.

meta.symbol: forwarded_host did not include port when proxy headers were not set

text: downloadable_as did not properly encode non-ascii filenames per rfc 6266: Stop relying on downloadable_as encoding non-ASCII filenames in a non-RFC-6266 way; it now follows RFC 6266 recommendations.

meta.symbol: downloadable_as did not properly encode non-ascii filenames per rfc 6266

text: compiledrouter mistakenly set route parameters while exploring non-matching routes: Stop relying on route parameters being set during exploration of non-matching routes; they are now only set on matching routes.

meta.symbol: compiledrouter mistakenly set route parameters while exploring non-matching routes

text: to_query_str did not correctly encode parameter keys and values, so simulate_request passed special characters like '&' incorrectly: Stop relying on to_query_str passing special characters like '&' unencoded; keys and values are now properly encoded.

meta.symbol: to_query_str did not correctly encode parameter keys and values, so simulate_request passed special characters like '&' incorrectly

text: Stop using falcon.Request.add_link() and use falcon.Response.append_link() instead.
Stop passing optional keyword arguments as positional arguments to falcon.HTTPError subclasses; use keyword arguments instead.

meta.symbol: positional arguments for optional kwargs of falcon.httperror subclasses

text: defaulted to false: Stop assuming middleware runs in the old order; independent_middleware now defaults to True.
content_length property (stream_len deprecated): Stop using Response.stream_len; use Response.content_length or Response.set_stream instead.
The deprecated falcon.testing.TestCase.api property was removed.
The deprecated falcon.testing.TestCase.api_class class variable was removed.
The deprecated falcon.testing.TestBase class was removed.
The deprecated falcon.testing.TestResource class was removed.
The deprecated protocol property was removed from the Request class.
The deprecated get_param_as_dict() method was removed from Request; use get_param_as_json() instead.
add_route() accepted method_map argument: The add_route() method for custom routers no longer takes a method_map argument; call falcon.routing.map_http_methods directly instead.

serialize() had no content_type; deserialize(raw): serialize() now receives an extra content_type argument; deserialize() now takes stream, content_type, and content_length instead of a single raw argument.
The deprecated falcon.routing.create_http_method_map() method was removed.
RequestOptions.auto_parse_qs_csv now defaults to False instead of True.
HTTPRequestEntityTooLarge was renamed to HTTPPayloadTooLarge.

plus character no longer unquoted in request path, only in query string: The '+' character is no longer unquoted/decoded in the request path; it is only unquoted in the query string. Existing code that expects '+' in the path to be treated as a space will silently break.

meta.symbol: plus character no longer unquoted in request path, only in query string

text: \ufffd character disallowed in requested static file paths: The U+FFFD (replacement character) is now disallowed in requested static file paths; requests containing it will be rejected instead of accepted.

meta.symbol: \ufffd character disallowed in requested static file paths

text: assumes '127.0.0.1' when remote_addr is missing: Stop checking for None from remote_addr; when REMOTE_ADDR is absent from WSGI environ, Falcon now returns '127.0.0.1' instead of None.
coerced header name via str(): Header-related methods of Response no longer call str() on the passed header name.
The deprecated stream_len property was removed from Response; use set_stream() or content_length instead.
Response.body and HTTPStatus.body are renamed to text; the old name is deprecated but still available.

raises medianotfounderror rendered as 400, or uses default value from get_media: Deserializing an empty body now raises MediaNotFoundError (rendered as 400) instead of returning None; pass a default to get_media to suppress.

meta.symbol: raises medianotfounderror rendered as 400, or uses default value from get_media

text: params_csv now defaults to false: Stop relying on simulate_request to parse query string CSV params by default; set params_csv=True explicitly if you need CSV parsing.
Stop treating falcon.HTTPError.to_json() output as a string; it now returns bytes.
Stop using falcon.API; use falcon.App instead. The old name is deprecated and will be removed.

only accepted 'true'/'false'/'yes'/'no' (or similar limited set): get_param_as_bool now treats 't' and 'y' as True, 'f' and 'n' as False, which may cause previously-invalid values to silently become valid booleans.

routes compiled on first request by default (compile=true to restore old behavior): CompiledRouter now defers route compilation until the first request instead of at add_route time; pass compile=True to add_route to compile immediately.

meta.symbol: routes compiled on first request by default (compile=true to restore old behavior)

text: Stop using Python 3.9; Flask 3.2.0 requires Python 3.10+.
Stop accessing flask.__version__; it has been removed.

requestcontext is now a deprecated alias; appcontext is not reused when dispatching a request: Stop relying on RequestContext being a separate context; it is now a deprecated alias of AppContext and an existing app context is not reused during request dispatch.

meta.symbol: requestcontext is now a deprecated alias; appcontext is not reused when dispatching a request

text: flask subclasses overriding dispatch methods used proxy objects and no appcontext parameter: If you subclass Flask and override request dispatch methods, update signatures to accept AppContext as the first parameter; the old signature still works with a deprecation warning.

meta.symbol: flask subclasses overriding dispatch methods used proxy objects and no appcontext parameter

text: if a teardown callback raised an error, remaining teardown callbacks were skipped: All teardown callbacks now run even if one raises; previously an error would stop subsequent callbacks.

meta.symbol: if a teardown callback raised an error, remaining teardown callbacks were skipped

text: Stop using should_ignore_error; it is deprecated. Handle errors in teardown handlers instead.
redirect() returned 302 by default: Stop relying on redirect() returning 302; it now returns 303 by default, telling the client to always switch to GET.
Stop using Python 3.8; Flask no longer supports it.
Setting SERVER_NAME no longer restricts requests to only that domain when host_matching=True or subdomain_matching=False is set.

meta.symbol: setting server_name no longer restricts requests to only that domain

text: no encoding parameter (default behavior): Flask.open_resource, open_instance_resource, and Blueprint.open_resource now take an encoding parameter that defaults to utf-8 when opening in text mode.
Request.max_content_length can now be customized per-request instead of only through the MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH config.
The -e path option now takes precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files.

meta.symbol: -e path did not take precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files

text: load_dotenv loads default files in addition to a path unless load_defaults=false: load_dotenv now loads default .env and .flaskenv files in addition to a specified path, unless load_defaults=False is passed.

meta.symbol: load_dotenv loads default files in addition to a path unless load_defaults=false

text: previously deprecated flask apis: All previously deprecated code has been removed; stop using any Flask APIs that were previously marked as deprecated.

flask and blueprint classes without sans-io bases: Flask (app) and Blueprint classes have been restructured to have Sans-IO bases, which may break code that directly inherits from or inspects these classes.

meta.symbol: flask and blueprint classes without sans-io bases

text: Minimum dependency versions have been updated: Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9.
Stop using Python 3.7; Flask requires Python 3.8 or later.
Stop using push() and pop() on _app_ctx_stack and _request_ctx_stack; they are removed.
Stop using FLASK_ENV, ENV config key, and app.env; they are removed.

meta.symbol: flask_env env var / env config / app.env

text: Stop using app.session_cookie_name, app.send_file_max_age_default, app.use_x_sendfile, app.propagate_exceptions, and app.templates_auto_reload; they are removed.

json_as_ascii, json_sort_keys, jsonify_mimetype, jsonify_prettyprint_regular config keys: Stop using JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, and JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR config keys; they are removed.

meta.symbol: json_as_ascii, json_sort_keys, jsonify_mimetype, jsonify_prettyprint_regular config keys

text: Stop using before_first_request and before_app_first_request decorators; they are removed.
Stop using json_encoder/json_decoder attributes on app and blueprint, and flask.json.JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder classes; they are removed.
flask.json.htmlsafe_dumps() / flask.json.htmlsafe_dump(): Stop using flask.json.htmlsafe_dumps and htmlsafe_dump; they are removed.
calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration produced a warning: Calling setup methods on blueprints after registration now raises an error instead of a warning.

meta.symbol: calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration produced a warning

text: from markupsafe import escape / from markupsafe import markup: Stop importing escape and Markup from flask; import them from markupsafe instead.

meta.symbol: from markupsafe import escape / from markupsafe import markup

text: session_cookie_domain does not fall back to server_name; default is no domain (exact match): SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN no longer falls back to SERVER_NAME; the default is no domain, which browsers interpret as an exact match not a subdomain match.

meta.symbol: session_cookie_domain does not fall back to server_name; default is no domain (exact match)

text: @locked_cached_property: Stop using locked_cached_property; use a lock inside the decorated function if locking is needed.
signals are always available; signals_available is deprecated: Stop checking signals_available; signals are always available as blinker>=1.6.2 is required.

meta.symbol: signals are always available; signals_available is deprecated

text: creating a blueprint with an empty name raises valueerror: Creating a blueprint with an empty name now raises a ValueError.

meta.symbol: creating a blueprint with an empty name raises valueerror

text: Stop using app.session_cookie_name, app.send_file_max_age_default, app.use_x_sendfile, app.propagate_exceptions, and app.templates_auto_reload; use the corresponding config keys instead.

using blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint shows a warning (will become error in next version): Using Blueprint decorators and functions intended for setup after registering the blueprint now shows a warning; in the next version this will become an error.

meta.symbol: using blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint shows a warning (will become error in next version)

text: Stop using before_first_request; run setup code when creating the application instead.

meta.symbol: run setup code when creating the application instead

text: Stop decorating custom CLI commands on app.cli or blueprint.cli with @with_appcontext; an app context is already active.

--eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run command: Stop using --eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run; they have been removed. The app is always eager loaded first, then lazily loaded in the reloader.

meta.symbol: --eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run command

text: Stop using Python 3.6; Flask requires Python 3.7+.
config.from_json(): Stop using config.from_json; use config.from_file(name, load=json.load) instead.
encoding parameter on json functions: Stop passing encoding parameter to json functions; it is no longer accepted.
Stop using flask.safe_join; use werkzeug.utils.safe_join instead.
Stop using total_seconds; use timedelta.total_seconds instead.
registering the same blueprint with the same name silently worked: Stop registering the same blueprint with the same name; use name= to specify a unique name.

meta.symbol: registering the same blueprint with the same name silently worked

text: Stop using as_tuple parameter on the test client; use response.request.environ instead.
cli passes script_info to app factory functions: Stop relying on script_info being passed to app factory functions from the CLI; it is no longer passed.

meta.symbol: cli passes script_info to app factory functions

text: blueprint names with dots now raise an error: Blueprint names must not contain a dot; using a dot in a blueprint name now raises an error.

meta.symbol: blueprint names with dots now raise an error

text: Registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated; use the 'name' option to give each registration a unique name.

meta.symbol: registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated

text: Stop using Python 2 or 3.5; Flask no longer supports them.
flask's own json support (no longer uses simplejson): Stop relying on simplejson being used internally; override app.json_encoder and json_decoder to use a different JSON module.

meta.symbol: flask's own json support (no longer uses simplejson)

text: errors on initial call cause immediate exit with traceback: The flask run command now exits immediately with a traceback on errors during the initial call, rather than deferring them.

meta.symbol: errors on initial call cause immediate exit with traceback

text: headers extended existing headers: When returning a (response, headers) tuple from a view, headers now replace rather than extend existing headers on the response.
Error handlers for the base HTTPException now receive an HTTPException instance instead of the original exception; access the original via e.original_exception.

meta.symbol: error handlers for base httpexception received the original exception directly.

text: Flask no longer supports Python 3.4.
Minimum Werkzeug version is now >= 0.15.
unprintable unicode characters in host header resulted in http 500: Unprintable Unicode characters in the Host header now produce a 400 Bad Request instead of a 500 Internal Server Error.

meta.symbol: unprintable unicode characters in host header resulted in http 500

text: url matching occurred when the request context was created: URL matching now happens after the request context is pushed, not when it's created, so custom URL converters can access the app and request contexts.

meta.symbol: url matching occurred when the request context was created

text: a catch-all error handler registered for httpexception will not handle routingexception: A catch-all error handler for HTTPException no longer catches RoutingException, which is used internally during routing.

meta.symbol: a catch-all error handler registered for httpexception will not handle routingexception

text: lists returned from view functions were treated the same as tuples (interpreted as response data): Returning a list from a view function no longer works like returning a tuple; only tuples are interpreted as response data.

meta.symbol: lists returned from view functions were treated the same as tuples (interpreted as response data)

text: Stop using Python 2.6 and 3.3; they are no longer supported.
Flask.make_response now raises TypeError instead of ValueError for bad response types.
Error handler caching has been removed; register handlers explicitly for each exception if you want to avoid traversing the MRO.
Old deprecated code has been removed.
Blueprint view function names may not contain dots; using a dot will cause an error.

meta.symbol: blueprint view function names may not contain dots

text: subdomain matching disabled by default; must pass subdomain_matching=true to flask constructor: Subdomain matching is disabled by default and setting SERVER_NAME no longer implicitly enables it; pass subdomain_matching=True to Flask constructor to enable.

meta.symbol: subdomain matching disabled by default; must pass subdomain_matching=true to flask constructor

text: a single trailing slash is stripped from blueprint url_prefix when registered: A single trailing slash is stripped from the blueprint url_prefix when it is registered with the app.

meta.symbol: a single trailing slash is stripped from blueprint url_prefix when registered

text: only accepts utf-8, utf-16, or utf-32 (autodetected): Request.get_json no longer accepts arbitrary encodings; incoming JSON must be UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32.

meta.symbol: only accepts utf-8, utf-16, or utf-32 (autodetected)

text: mimetype guessing and etag generation for file-like objects: send_file no longer guesses mimetype or generates ETags for file-like objects.

meta.symbol: mimetype guessing and etag generation for file-like objects

text: fails loudly (raises an error) when mimetype cannot be guessed: send_file now fails loudly instead of falling back to application/octet-stream when mimetype cannot be guessed.

meta.symbol: fails loudly (raises an error) when mimetype cannot be guessed

text: templates are no longer automatically reloaded outside of debug mode: Stop relying on automatic template reloading outside debug mode; set TEMPLATES_AUTO_RELOAD=True if you need it.

meta.symbol: templates are no longer automatically reloaded outside of debug mode

text: Stop using flask.run() to start the local debug server; use the flask CLI via the click CLI system instead.

error handlers that match specific classes are now checked first: Error handlers matching specific exception classes are now checked before generic handlers, which may change which handler catches your exceptions.

meta.symbol: error handlers that match specific classes are now checked first

text: flask did not log by default when debug is disabled: Stop assuming Flask is silent when debug is disabled; Flask now logs by default, configurable via LOGGER_HANDLER_POLICY.

meta.symbol: flask did not log by default when debug is disabled

text: Stop using any previously deprecated module functionality as it has been removed.
json responses are now terminated with a newline character: JSON responses now end with a newline character, which may break clients that don't expect trailing newlines.

meta.symbol: json responses are now terminated with a newline character

text: before_first_request handlers flipped the flag before invoking: before_first_request handlers now flip the flag after invoking, which changes behavior for handlers that may fail or have side effects.

meta.symbol: before_first_request handlers flipped the flag before invoking

text: session system now supports byte strings again (reverses a prior breaking change): The session system now accepts byte strings again; if you were working around a lack of byte string support, your workaround may break or be unnecessary.

meta.symbol: session system now supports byte strings again (reverses a prior breaking change)

text: Stop relying on pickle-based cookie serialization; cookies are now serialized as JSON by default.
flask.session (internal module alias): Stop importing flask.session as an internal module alias; use flask.sessions instead.
Stop relying on flask.g being scoped to the request context; it is now stored on the app context.
Stop using Flask.request_globals_class; rename it to Flask.app_ctx_globals_class.
Stop relying on Flask's custom JSON HTTP exception subclasses; they have been removed.
python 2.5 or earlier: Stop using Python versions older than 2.6; Flask now requires Python 2.6 or 2.7.
flask.request.json (property): Stop using request.json as a property; call request.get_json() method instead.

only returns the debug log setting if it was set explicitly: Logger now only returns the debug log setting if it was not set explicitly, so code relying on a default debug value from the logger may now get None.

meta.symbol: only returns the debug log setting if it was set explicitly

text: Tuple return values from functions are no longer arguments to the response object, they now have a defined meaning (status code, headers).

meta.symbol: have a defined meaning (status code, headers, etc.)

text: session was stored before callbacks: Session is now stored after callbacks so that if the session payload is stored in the session you can still modify it in an after request callback.

malformed json data now triggers a bad request http exception (400): Malformed JSON data now raises a Bad Request HTTP exception instead of a ValueError, so code catching ValueError on JSON parsing will no longer catch these errors and the HTTP response code changes from 500 to 400.

meta.symbol: malformed json data now triggers a bad request http exception (400)

text: mimetype guessing and etag support from file objects is deprecated; pass filenames or provide mimetype and etags manually: Stop relying on send_file to guess mimetype or generate etags from file objects; pass a filename string instead, or supply your own mimetype and etags.

meta.symbol: mimetype guessing and etag support from file objects is deprecated; pass filenames or provide mimetype and etags manually

text: static folder name is auto-detected for modules: You must now explicitly supply the static folder name when using modules; the old auto-detection is deprecated and will be removed in 1.0.

meta.symbol: static folder name is auto-detected for modules

text: After request functions are now called in reverse order of registration.

meta.symbol: called in reverse order of registration

text: OPTIONS is now automatically implemented by Flask unless the application explicitly adds 'OPTIONS' as method to the URL rule.

meta.symbol: options is now automatically implemented by flask unless the application explicitly adds 'options' as method to the url rule

text: Context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function.

meta.symbol: context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function

text: autoescaping is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml templates: Autoescaping is no longer active for all templates; it is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml.

meta.symbol: autoescaping is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml templates

text: zipped applications were supported: Support for zipped applications has been dropped.
create_jinja_loader was supported: Support for create_jinja_loader has been removed.

server listened on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) by default: The development server now binds to 127.0.0.1 by default instead of all interfaces; external machines can no longer reach the server unless explicitly configured.

meta.symbol: server listened on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) by default

text: attachment_filename, cache_timeout, add_etags: Stop using attachment_filename, cache_timeout, and add_etags parameters on send_file; use download_name, max_age, and etag instead.
Stop using filename parameter on send_from_directory; use path instead.
Stop accessing g via RequestContext; use the g object directly on the app context instead.
Stop using _app_ctx_stack.top and _request_ctx_stack.top; store extension data on g using a unique prefix like g._extension_name_attr instead.
flask_env environment variable and app.env attribute: Stop using FLASK_ENV env var or app.env to control debug mode; use --debug flag or app.run(debug=True) instead.

meta.symbol: flask_env environment variable and app.env attribute

text: setting or accessing json_encoder or json_decoder raises a deprecation warning: Setting or accessing json_encoder or json_decoder now raises a deprecation warning; migrate to the new JSON provider system.

meta.symbol: setting or accessing json_encoder or json_decoder raises a deprecation warning

text: Stop using JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, and JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR config keys; set these on app.json provider attributes instead.

meta.symbol: attributes on the default app.json provider

text: setting custom json_encoder and json_decoder classes on app or blueprint, and json.jsonencoder/jsondecoder classes: Stop setting custom json_encoder and json_decoder classes on the app or blueprint; override JSON behavior using the app.json provider interface instead.

meta.symbol: setting custom json_encoder and json_decoder classes on app or blueprint, and json.jsonencoder/jsondecoder classes

text: Stop using flask.json.htmlsafe_dumps and flask.json.htmlsafe_dump; the function is built into Jinja now.
Stop using attachment_filename, cache_timeout, add_etags, filename in send_file/send_from_directory; use download_name, max_age, etag, path instead.
Stop using RequestContext.g; use g directly or AppContext.g instead.
location header url remains relative, excluding scheme and domain: Redirect responses now use relative Location header URLs by default, excluding scheme and domain.

meta.symbol: location header url remains relative, excluding scheme and domain

text: Stop using 'filename' parameter in send_from_directory; use 'path' instead.
Stop passing the encoding option to JSON functions; it is deprecated.
Stop passing script_info to app factory functions; use click.get_current_context().obj instead.
Stop using Config.from_json; use Config.from_file with json.load instead.
responded with 200 ok and empty file: Stop passing text-mode io objects to send_file; it now raises ValueError instead of silently returning an empty file.

meta.symbol: responded with 200 ok and empty file

text: changed current working directory to file location: Loading a .env or .flaskenv file no longer changes the current working directory to the file's location.

meta.symbol: changed current working directory to file location

text: Stop using attachment_filename, cache_timeout, add_etags in send_file; use download_name, max_age, etag instead.
download_name sent via content-disposition: inline: send_file now passes download_name even when as_attachment=False by using Content-Disposition: inline.
conditional=true, max_age=none, cache-control: no-cache: send_file now defaults to conditional=True and max_age=None, setting Cache-Control to no-cache unless max_age is set.
Stop using helpers.safe_join; use werkzeug.utils.safe_join instead.
Stop using helpers.total_seconds(); use timedelta.total_seconds() instead.
Error handlers for 500/InternalServerError now receive an InternalServerError instance instead of the original exception directly; access the original via e.original_exception.

meta.symbol: error handlers for internalservererror or 500 received the original unhandled exception directly as the argument.

text: flask.json_available is deprecated and will be removed in 2.0.0; using it raises a deprecation warning.
flask.logger always logged to "flask.app": Stop relying on Flask.logger being named 'flask.app'; it now uses the app's import_name as its logger name.
flask's jsonmixin for request and response wrappers: Stop using Flask's JSONMixin; use Werkzeug's version instead (requires Werkzeug >= 0.15).

meta.symbol: flask's jsonmixin for request and response wrappers

text: flask.jinja_options was an immutabledict: Flask.jinja_options is now a plain dict instead of an ImmutableDict, so you can mutate it directly (but still before creating the environment).
Stop using flask.testing.make_test_environ_builder(); use flask.testing.EnvironBuilder instead.
send_file encoded filenames as latin-1 (iso-8859-1): send_file now encodes filenames as ASCII instead of Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1), which may break filenames with non-ASCII characters.
true (indented json by default): JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR now defaults to False; jsonify returns compact format by default instead of indented.
Stop using flask.ext namespace; import extensions directly by their package name (e.g. flask_sqlalchemy instead of flask.ext.sqlalchemy).
Stop overriding Flask.init_jinja_globals; extend Flask.create_jinja_environment instead.
Stop using Flask.error_handlers; use Flask.error_handler_spec and register handlers via Flask.errorhandler.
Stop using Flask.request_globals_class; use Flask.app_ctx_globals_class instead.
Stop using Flask.static_path; use Flask.static_url_path instead.
Stop using Request.module; use Request.blueprint instead.
Stop setting LOGGER_NAME and LOGGER_HANDLER_POLICY config; the logger is always named flask.app and handlers are auto-configured.
request.get_json cached the result even if parsing failed when silent=true: Request.get_json no longer caches the result if parsing fails when silent=True; subsequent calls will re-attempt parsing.

meta.symbol: request.get_json cached the result even if parsing failed when silent=true

text: request.get_json accepted arbitrary encodings: Request.get_json no longer accepts arbitrary encodings; only UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32 are supported.

serializing top-level arrays to jsonify (now supported, introduces security risk in ancient browsers): Stop using jsonify with top-level arrays if you need to support ancient browsers, as it introduces a security risk.

meta.symbol: serializing top-level arrays to jsonify (now supported, introduces security risk in ancient browsers)

text: Stop using request.json; use request.get_json() instead.

the automatically provided options method is now correctly disabled if the user registered an overriding rule with the lowercase-version 'options': If you registered a route with lowercase 'options', the automatic OPTIONS handler is now disabled, which may break code relying on both.

meta.symbol: the automatically provided options method is now correctly disabled if the user registered an overriding rule with the lowercase-version 'options'

text: render_template_string did not have autoescape turned on by default: Stop relying on render_template_string not auto-escaping; autoescape is now on by default.

meta.symbol: render_template_string did not have autoescape turned on by default

text: Stop using flask.ext to import extensions; import extensions directly instead.

send_from_directory now raises badrequest if the filename is invalid on the server os: send_from_directory now raises BadRequest for OS-invalid filenames, which may break code that previously passed such filenames.

meta.symbol: send_from_directory now raises badrequest if the filename is invalid on the server os

text: Request.on_json_loading_failed now returns a JSON formatted response by default instead of a non-JSON response.
environ ``werkzeug.request`` will be ``None`` after the response was returned to the WSGI server, so code accessing it post-response will get None instead of the request object.
flask.create_jinja_environment method: Stop overriding init_jinja_globals; override Flask.create_jinja_environment instead to customize Jinja globals.

exception propagation is linked to debug or testing: PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS now defaults to True if either DEBUG or TESTING is enabled, not just DEBUG alone; existing code relying on DEBUG-only propagation may see changed behaviour.

meta.symbol: exception propagation is linked to debug or testing

text: accessing unbound locals raises attributeerror: Accessing an unbound local variable now raises RuntimeError instead of AttributeError; catch RuntimeError instead if you were catching AttributeError for this case.
Stop using `resolution` in `buffer()`; use `quad_segs` instead.
Stop using `seed` in `sample_points`; use `rng` instead.
GeoPandas 1.2 requires Python 3.11 or greater; code running on Python 3.10 or older will fail to install or import.
GeoPandas 1.2 requires pandas 2.2 or greater; older pandas versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 1.2 requires numpy 2.0 or greater; older numpy versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 1.2 requires pyproj 3.7 or greater; older pyproj versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 1.2 requires shapely 2.1 or greater; older shapely versions are no longer supported.
The `use_pygeos` option has been removed entirely; it had no functionality and setting it will cause an error.
GeoPandas 1.1 requires Python 3.10 or greater; code running on Python 3.9 or older will fail to install or import.
GeoPandas 1.1 requires pandas 2.0 or greater; code running on older pandas versions will break.
GeoPandas 1.1 requires numpy 1.24 or greater; code running on older numpy versions will break.
GeoPandas 1.1 requires pyproj 3.5 or greater; code running on older pyproj versions will break.
Stop using GeoSeries.select() as it has been removed; the upstream pandas Series.select() no longer exists.
Stop using geom_almost_equals() as it has been removed; use geom_equals_exact() instead.
shapely<2, pygeos: GeoPandas 1.0 drops support for shapely<2 and PyGEOS; only shapely >= 2 is supported.
rtree spatial index: Spatial indexing based on the rtree package has been removed.
fiona (default i/o engine): The I/O engine now defaults to Pyogrio instead of Fiona.

align keyword defaults to none (treated as true): The `align` keyword in binary methods now defaults to `None` (treated as True); passing explicit True silences the warning about mismatched indices, but code relying on the old default may now see a warning.

meta.symbol: align keyword defaults to none (treated as true)

text: passing a named (geo)series as geometry argument could use that name as the active geometry column name: Passing a named (Geo)Series as the `geometry` argument to the GeoDataFrame constructor now always produces an active geometry column named 'geometry', ignoring the Series name.

meta.symbol: passing a named (geo)series as geometry argument could use that name as the active geometry column name

text: make_valid option in overlay used buffer(0): The `make_valid` option in `overlay` now uses the `make_valid` method instead of `buffer(0)`, which may produce different geometry repair results.

meta.symbol: make_valid option in overlay used buffer(0)

text: sjoin preserves the name of the right geodataframe's index if it has one: Stop assuming the right-index column from sjoin is always named 'index_right'; it may now be the original index name.

meta.symbol: sjoin preserves the name of the right geodataframe's index if it has one

text: passing an unaligned series silently auto-aligned: Stop passing unaligned Series as method arguments; GeoPandas now raises ValueError instead of silently auto-aligning.

meta.symbol: passing an unaligned series silently auto-aligned

text: explode(..., index_parts=true) was the default: Stop relying on explode() defaulting to index_parts=True; the default is now False, matching pandas.
set_geometry(ser) kept the old active geometry column name: Stop assuming set_geometry(ser) keeps the old geometry column name; it now uses ser.name, which may create a second column if names differ.

meta.symbol: set_geometry(ser) kept the old active geometry column name

text: Stop relying on delaunay_triangles operating element-wise per geometry; it now triangulates all geometries together. Use shapely.delaunay_triangles for per-geometry behaviour.

read_file returned a geodataframe with an empty geometry column when source had no geometry: Stop expecting read_file to always return a GeoDataFrame; if the source has no geometry field, it now returns a plain pandas DataFrame.

meta.symbol: read_file returned a geodataframe with an empty geometry column when source had no geometry

text: Stop using geopandas.datasets; use the geodatasets package instead.
Stop using geopandas.io.read_file, geopandas.io.to_file, and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis; use geopandas.read_file, geopandas.read_postgis, and GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries.to_file() instead.
Stop using GeometryArray.data; use np.asarray() or the to_numpy() method instead.
Stop using sindex.query_bulk; use sindex.query instead.
sjoin(op=...): Stop using the 'op' parameter in sjoin; use the 'predicate' parameter instead.
Stop using ^, |, &, - operators on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame; use symmetric_difference(), union(), intersection(), difference() methods instead.
Stop using plot_polygon_collection, plot_linestring_collection, plot_point_collection; use GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame .plot() method directly instead.
Stop using 'axes' and 'colormap' parameters in .plot(); use 'ax' and 'cmap' instead.

to_parquet(version=...), to_feather(version=...): The 'version' keyword in to_parquet and to_feather is no longer used for GeoParquet spec version; use 'schema_version' instead. The 'version' keyword now passes through to pyarrow.
Stop using the unary_union attribute; use the union_all() method instead.

set_geometry(drop=true/false): Stop using the drop keyword in set_geometry(); remove drop=False calls (default is same), and replace drop=True with gdf.set_geometry(new_geo_col).drop(columns=geo_col_name).rename_geometry(geo_col_name).
Stop using geopandas.use_pygeos; it is deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 1.1.
gdf.crs = new_crs: Stop manually overriding CRS by setting the crs property directly; use the set_crs() method instead.
GeoPandas 0.14 uses Shapely 2.0 by default instead of PyGEOS when both Shapely >= 2.0 and PyGEOS are installed; PyGEOS is only default with Shapely < 2.0.

meta.symbol: pygeos used by default when both shapely >= 2.0 and pygeos are installed

text: Stop using 'seed' keyword in sample_points; use 'rng' instead.
GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for Python 3.8.
GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for pandas 1.3 and below; minimum supported pandas version is now 1.4.
Stop using geom_almost_equals() and use geom_equals_exact() instead.
Stop using sindex.query_bulk() and use sindex.query() instead.
unary_union returns 'geometrycollection empty' for all-none geoseries: unary_union on an all-None GeoSeries will now return 'GEOMETRYCOLLECTION EMPTY' instead of None.

meta.symbol: unary_union returns 'geometrycollection empty' for all-none geoseries

text: creating a geodataframe from a dataframe could produce a view (mutating one would affect the other): GeoDataFrame created from a DataFrame is now a copy, not a view; mutating it will no longer affect the source DataFrame.

meta.symbol: creating a geodataframe from a dataframe could produce a view (mutating one would affect the other)

text: GeoPandas 0.13 drops support for pandas 1.0.5; minimum pandas version is now 1.1.
GeoPandas 0.13 requires shapely >= 1.7.1; older shapely versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 0.13 requires fiona >= 1.8.19; older fiona versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 0.13 requires pyproj >= 3.0.1; older pyproj versions are no longer supported.
GeoPandas 0.13 requires matplotlib >= 3.3.4; older matplotlib versions are no longer supported.

accessing .crs on a geodataframe without an active geometry column was deprecated but still worked: Accessing the `crs` attribute of a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column now raises an AttributeError instead of returning a value.

meta.symbol: accessing .crs on a geodataframe without an active geometry column was deprecated but still worked

text: gdf.geometry returned gdf['geometry'] when active geometry column was missing but a column named 'geometry' existed: Accessing `gdf.geometry` when the active geometry column is missing but a column named 'geometry' is present now raises an AttributeError instead of returning that column.

meta.symbol: gdf.geometry returned gdf['geometry'] when active geometry column was missing but a column named 'geometry' existed

text: Stop using GeometryArray.equals_exact(); use GeometryArray.geom_equals_exact() instead.
Stop using GeometryArray.almost_equals(); use GeometryArray.geom_almost_equals() instead.
explicit_crs_from_epsg(), epsg_from_crs(), get_epsg_file_contents(): Stop using explicit_crs_from_epsg(), epsg_from_crs(), and get_epsg_file_contents(); they have been removed.
contradictory crs silently accepted or behaved differently: Specifying a CRS in the GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries constructor that contradicts the underlying GeometryArray now raises a ValueError.

meta.symbol: contradictory crs silently accepted or behaved differently

text: emits userwarning (now succeeds): Writing an empty GeoDataFrame to a file now emits a UserWarning instead of raising a ValueError.

GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame set operations (__xor__, __or__, __and__, __sub__) and geopandas.io.file.read_file/to_file and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis now emit FutureWarning instead of DeprecationWarning.
Accessing the crs of a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column is deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 0.12.
GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for Python 3.7
GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for pandas 0.25; minimum supported pandas version is now 1.0.5
GeoPandas 0.11 requires shapely >= 1.7
GeoPandas 0.11 requires fiona >= 1.8.13.post1
GeoPandas 0.11 requires pyproj >= 2.6.1.post1
GeoPandas 0.11 requires matplotlib >= 3.2
GeoPandas 0.11 requires mapclassify >= 2.4.0
Stop using op= in sjoin(), use predicate= instead.
Stop using cascaded_union, use unary_union instead.
disallowed (raises error): Constructing a GeoDataFrame with a duplicated 'geometry' column now raises an error.
explode() currently returns MultiIndex with part indices but this will change; use index_parts keyword to control behaviour.
clip() left multipoints intact when partly outside clip bounds: clip() now correctly clips MultiPoints that are partly outside the clip bounds instead of leaving them intact

GeoPandas 0.10.0 dropped support for pandas 0.24
GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required numpy version to 1.18
GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required shapely version to 1.6

The set operators (&, |, ^, -) on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame are deprecated; use intersection(), union(), symmetric_difference(), difference() methods instead.
The .crs attribute of GeoSeries and GeoDataFrame now stores a pyproj.CRS object instead of a proj4 string or dict.
GeoDataFrame.to_file() now writes the index to the file when the index is named and/or non-integer; use index=True/False to control this.
GeoPandas 0.7.0 dropped support for Python 2.7; Python >= 3.5 is now required.
GeoPandas 0.7.0 now requires PROJ > 6 and pyproj > 2.2.
GeoSeries.dtype is now 'geometry' dtype instead of numpy object dtype.
GeoSeries.values now returns a GeometryArray instead of a numpy array; use np.asarray() to get a numpy array of Shapely scalars.

text: GeoSeries.isna no longer considers empty geometries as missing; use GeoSeries.is_empty to check for empty geometries.
GeoSeries.dropna now actually drops missing values (previously it dropped nothing).

text: Stop using `axes=` in plot(); use `ax=` instead.
Stop using `colormap=` in plot(); use `cmap=` instead.

previously allowed or behaved differently: Specifying a CRS in the GeoDataFrame constructor when no geometry column is provided, and calling set_crs on a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column, now raise a ValueError.
Passing non-geometry data to the GeoSeries constructor now raises a TypeError instead of returning a pandas.Series.

text: Stop using the 'objects' keyword in the intersection() method of the spatial index (sindex); it has been removed.
The helper functions in the geopandas.plotting module are deprecated for public usage and should not be imported.

text: geopandas.io functions for reading/writing files: The geopandas.io functions are deprecated; use top-level read_file() and to_file() instead.
will return empty spatial index: The sindex attribute on an empty GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries will in the future return an empty spatial index instead of None.
objects keyword in sindex.intersection(): The 'objects' keyword in the intersection method of the spatial index (sindex) is deprecated and will be removed.
raises a warning, will raise an error in future: GeoSeries constructor now raises a warning when passed non-geometry data; in the future this will raise an error.

expired oauth sessions treated as logged out users: Expired OAuth sessions are now treated as logged out users instead of being considered valid.

gr.JSON lines behavior has been fixed; existing code relying on the previous (broken) lines rendering may break.
gr.File has been migrated; existing code using gr.File may break due to internal changes.
gr.DateTime has been migrated; existing code using gr.DateTime may break due to internal changes.
gr.Dropdown has been migrated; existing code using gr.Dropdown may break due to internal changes.
gr.Textbox has been migrated to Svelte 5; existing code using gr.Textbox may break due to internal changes.
gr.Audio has been migrated to Svelte 5; existing code using gr.Audio may break due to internal changes.
