text: Replace queue=False with concurrency_limit=None on event triggers for quick events.
concurrency_count=x (global): Replace the global concurrency_count=X with concurrency_limit=X on the specific event trigger.
Stop using the tool= argument in gr.Image(); use gr.ImageEditor() instead.
Replace gr.Image(source='canvas', tools='sketch') with gr.ImageEditor(sources=(), brush=gr.Brush(colors=['#000000'])).
Replace gr.Image(source='canvas', tools='color-sketch') with gr.ImageEditor(sources=()).
Replace gr.Image(source='upload', tools='color-sketch') with gr.ImageEditor() (default behavior).
single image value from gr.image(): The ImageEditor component returns a dict with keys 'background', 'layers', and 'composite' instead of a single image value.
Stop using gr.mix; it has been removed entirely.
Stop using serializes; they have been removed.
files in the working directory are no longer served by default: Stop relying on files in the working directory being served automatically; they are no longer served by default.

meta.symbol: files in the working directory are no longer served by default

text: api_open=false by default: Stop assuming the API is open by default; api_open now defaults to False.
Stop using duplicate elem_ids on components; they have been removed.
Stop using interpretation; it has been removed entirely.
show_edit_button parameter in audio: Stop using show_edit_button in Audio; it has been removed.
interactive prop on frontend: Stop using the 'mode' prop on the frontend; use 'interactive' instead to match the backend.
gradio_cached_folder inside gradio temp directory: Stop relying on gradio_cached_folder being at its previous location; it now lives inside the gradio temp directory.
preprocess() returned temporaryfile: Stop expecting preprocess() on File and UploadButton to return a TemporaryFile; it now returns a string filepath.
Stop using all previously deprecated arguments and parameters; they have been removed in v4.
blocks.load() had a different style: Stop using the old Blocks.load() signature; it has been refactored to match the style of other listeners.
sse (server-sent events) for communication: Stop relying on websockets; communication now uses SSE (Server-Sent Events).
Stop using `gradio_component` CLI command; use `gradio component` instead.
`gr.Dataframe` now hides overflowing text when `wrap=False` instead of wrapping or clipping differently, which may break layouts relying on the old overflow behavior.
cancel events had api=true (exposed via api): Cancel events now have `api=False` set, meaning they are no longer exposed via the API endpoint.

meta.symbol: cancel events had api=true (exposed via api)

text: audio not normalized when converting to wav: Audio is now normalized when converting to wav, which changes the output signal level.

meta.symbol: audio not normalized when converting to wav

text: blocklabel used a non-<label> element: BlockLabel element now uses a <label> HTML element, which may affect CSS selectors and accessibility behavior.
interface internals used deprecated .update() method: Deprecated .update() usage has been removed from Interface internals; code relying on that internal behavior may break.
Stop using async methods in the Examples class; they are now sync methods.

meta.symbol: async methods in the examples class

text: Stop relying on the deprecation warning from gr.update; it has been removed.
tab and tabs were not updatable: Tab and Tabs components can now be updated after creation, which may change their behavior in existing code that relied on them being static.

meta.symbol: tab and tabs were not updatable

text: gr.select() had issues with dataframe cells: gr.select() behavior for Dataframe cells has been fixed, which may break code that worked around the previous buggy behavior.

meta.symbol: gr.select() had issues with dataframe cells

text: generated filepaths were not fully resolved on hf spaces with multiple replicas: Generated filepaths are now fully resolved when running on Hugging Face Spaces with multiple replicas, which may change file path behavior in existing deployments.

meta.symbol: generated filepaths were not fully resolved on hf spaces with multiple replicas

text: gr.load() did not accept bare component type for inputs and outputs: gr.load() now accepts a bare `Component` type in its `inputs` and `outputs` parameter type list, which may change type checking behavior for existing code.

meta.symbol: gr.load() did not accept bare component type for inputs and outputs

text: Stop using get_config() on components; the config is now derived automatically from constructor parameters.
Stop using .update() on components; instead return the component itself. .update() is deprecated.
api endpoints with api_name=false are blocked from client access: Stop relying on Clients being able to access API endpoints that set api_name=False; they are now prevented from doing so.

meta.symbol: api endpoints with api_name=false are blocked from client access

text: gr.Dropdown now supports separate display names and values for choices, breaking code that assumed choice strings were both name and value.
gr.Dropdown multiselect now supports `allow_custom_value`, which may change behaviour for code that didn't expect custom values in multiselect.

dropdown now has a `filterable` parameter (default likely true) that controls whether user can type to filter choices: Stop relying on Dropdown always being filterable by typing; set `filterable=True` explicitly if you need that behavior.

meta.symbol: dropdown now has a `filterable` parameter (default likely true) that controls whether user can type to filter choices

text: dropdown filtering by typing is now controlled by `filterable` parameter: Stop assuming Dropdown always allows filtering by typing; you must now pass `filterable=True` to enable it.

meta.symbol: dropdown filtering by typing is now controlled by `filterable` parameter

text:
markdown rendered with line breaks enabled (breaks=true): Stop relying on automatic line breaks in gr.Markdown; single newlines no longer produce
tags.

meta.symbol: markdown rendered with line breaks enabled (breaks=true)

text: aiohttp was a dependency of gradio: Stop relying on aiohttp being available as a transitive dependency of gradio; it has been removed.

meta.symbol: aiohttp was a dependency of gradio

text: choices accepted only simple values (same as display name): gr.Radio and gr.CheckboxGroup now accept separate display names and underlying values for each choice, which may break code that relied on the value being identical to the display name.

meta.symbol: choices accepted only simple values (same as display name)

text: Setting show_api=False on gr.Interface now completely hides the API page instead of partially hiding it.
Markdown and LaTeX processing for gr.Markdown and gr.DataFrame has moved from backend to frontend, which may change rendering behavior.
inputs and preprocessing are no longer passed on iterators: Stop relying on inputs and preprocessing being passed when using iterators; they are no longer passed.

meta.symbol: inputs and preprocessing are no longer passed on iterators

text: Empty dataframes in gr.DataFrame are now handled differently, which may change how empty state appears.
Message text in gr.Chatbot no longer overflows onto the copy button, changing layout behavior.
gr.File component now ensures files are uploaded to the server, which may change previous behavior where files were not uploaded.
audio streaming behavior: Audio streaming now properly processes WAV files as a single seamless stream without cracks, and streaming any audio type by yielding bytes now works flawlessly.
.update() method behavior: The .update() method for gr.Radio() and gr.CheckboxGroup() has been fixed, changing how these components are updated dynamically.
event listeners start only after components are mounted: Event listeners now only start listening after components are mounted, which may break code that relied on events firing during mount.

meta.symbol: event listeners start only after components are mounted

text: Client.predict now returns the final output instead of the first output for streaming endpoints.
output json has `is_stream: true` instead of `is_file: true`: Streamed audio outputs now use `is_stream: true` instead of `is_file: true` in the output JSON.

meta.symbol: output json has `is_stream: true` instead of `is_file: true`

text: Streamed audio outputs are now served from `/stream/` endpoint instead of `/file/` endpoint.
Setting gr.Textbox value to None now resets the textbox to an empty string instead of keeping the previous value.
gr.Dropdown now supports values with arbitrary characters and no longer clears the selected value when the dropdown is re-focused.
container parameter is now only available for textbox, number, and dropdown: Stop using container=True/False on components other than Textbox, Number, and Dropdown; it will be ignored or error.

meta.symbol: container parameter is now only available for textbox, number, and dropdown

text: dependencies were unpinned / loosely versioned: Gradio now pins many dependencies to major versions, which can cause downstream dependency conflicts and break existing apps that rely on unpinned or different versions of those dependencies.
Stop using the 'load_examples' named endpoint on gr.Interface; it has been removed.
Stop relying on gr.BarPlot.update automatically setting show_label to True; you must now explicitly pass show_label=True if you want the label shown.

meta.symbol: show_label was automatically set to true when calling gr.barplot.update

text: clear button resets to developer's original default value: The Clear button on Slider, CheckboxGroup, Radio, and Dropdown now sets the value to empty instead of the developer's original default.

meta.symbol: clear button resets to developer's original default value

text: Gradio no longer supports Python 3.7.
chatbot used $ as a default latex delimiter: The $ character is no longer a default LaTeX delimiter in Chatbot; you must set latex_delimiters explicitly.

meta.symbol: chatbot used $ as a default latex delimiter

text: using .style() method to set style arguments: The .style() method is deprecated; move all style arguments to the component constructor and use .update() instead.

meta.symbol: using .style() method to set style arguments

text: no longer allows accessing dotfiles or files in 'dot directories': The /file= route no longer allows accessing dotfiles or files in dot directories.

meta.symbol: no longer allows accessing dotfiles or files in 'dot directories'

text: gr.Chatbot() now parses markdown on the frontend using the `marked` library and `prism` instead of server-side parsing.
5 seconds timeout: The timeout for websocket messaging has been increased from 1 second to 5 seconds.

uses git pull/push and a .git/ folder in flagging/, accepts organization parameter: Stop passing organization to gr.HuggingFaceDatasetSaver; pass a full dataset id like 'username/my-dataset' as dataset_name instead, and the flagging/ folder is no longer a .git/ folder.

meta.symbol: uses git pull/push and a .git/ folder in flagging/, accepts organization parameter

text:
newlines (\n) automatically converted to
tags: Stop relying on \n being auto-converted to
in gr.Markdown() or gr.Chatbot(); you must now explicitly add
tags for line breaks.
re-exports in gradio.themes utilities (introduced in 3.24.0): Stop using any re-exported symbols from gradio.themes utilities that were introduced in 3.24.0; they have been removed.
_js return values are no longer double wrapped in an array: Stop relying on _js return values being double-wrapped in an array; they now return a single value directly.

meta.symbol: _js return values are no longer double wrapped in an array

text: Stop expecting the 'Remove All' button to appear in gr.Dropdown single-select mode; it is now hidden.
non-selected items in dropdown could also receive a checkmark: Stop relying on non-selected dropdown items showing a checkmark; only the selected item now gets one.

meta.symbol: non-selected items in dropdown could also receive a checkmark

text: events could be queued without the queue being configured (silently ignored or worked): If you queue an event but have not configured the queue, Gradio now raises an error instead of silently proceeding.

meta.symbol: events could be queued without the queue being configured (silently ignored or worked)

text: theme name was set to something else (e.g. local name or default): When loading a theme from the HuggingFace Hub, the theme name is now set to the upstream repo name instead of whatever was used before.

meta.symbol: theme name was set to something else (e.g. local name or default)

text: Stop using Markdown syntax for images in Chatbot; images are now passed as tuples with URL/filepath and optional alt text.

meta.symbol: markdown syntax for images in chatbot

text: generic_update allowed in-place mutation: Stop mutating the result of generic_update in-place; it now returns a shallow copy so mutations will not affect the original.

file names of uploaded files were not persisted (may have been lost or changed): File names of uploaded files are now persisted through any Gradio component; code that relied on file names being discarded or transient may break.

meta.symbol: file names of uploaded files were not persisted (may have been lost or changed)

text: gr.dropdown (multiselect=true) initial empty value was not an empty list: gr.Dropdown with Multiselect=True now has an initial empty value of [] (empty list), and gr.Dropdown with Multiselect=False now has an initial empty value of '' (empty string).

meta.symbol: gr.dropdown (multiselect=true) initial empty value was not an empty list

text: embed's initial_height persisted after loading: The embed's `initial_height` is now removed when loading is complete so the embed finds its natural height once loaded.
Stop using chatbot.style(color_map=...) to set bubble colors; it no longer works.
apps loaded and rendered immediately on page load: Gradio apps are now lazy-loaded by default; they only load and render when scrolled into view, instead of immediately on page load.

meta.symbol: apps loaded and rendered immediately on page load

text: the `default_enabled` parameter of `blocks.queue` had an effect: The `default_enabled` parameter of `Blocks.queue` has no effect and is ignored.

meta.symbol: the `default_enabled` parameter of `blocks.queue` had an effect

text: Importing gradio no longer breaks PIL.Image.registered_extensions().

meta.symbol: importing gradio did not affect pil.image.registered_extensions()

text: temporary files are assigned a path that is a hash based on file contents: Temporary file paths are now hashed from file contents instead of randomly generated, which may break code that relied on predictable or random temp file naming.

meta.symbol: temporary files are assigned a path that is a hash based on file contents

text: shareable links used ssh port-forwarding: Shareable links when share=True now use frp-based tunneling instead of ssh port-forwarding, which may change network behavior or firewall requirements.
images in chatbot were displayed at original size: Images in the chatbot component are now resized if they exceed a max width.

meta.symbol: images in chatbot were displayed at original size

text: gr.textbox(type="number"): Stop using gr.Textbox with type='number'; it now raises an exception and only 'text', 'email', or 'password' are allowed.
requests to /api endpoint are prevented from skipping the queue if queue is enabled: Requests to the /api endpoint no longer bypass the queue when the queue is enabled for that event.

meta.symbol: requests to /api endpoint are prevented from skipping the queue if queue is enabled

text: gradio does not control the html page title when embedded as a web component unless `control_page_title="true"` is added: When embedding Gradio as a web component, you must add `control_page_title="true"` to restore the old behaviour of controlling the page title; otherwise Gradio will not set the page title.

meta.symbol: gradio does not control the html page title when embedded as a web component unless `control_page_title="true"` is added

text: carousel component was usable (with deprecation warnings since 3.0): The `Carousel` component is deprecated and will raise an exception in the next release; stop using `Carousel` immediately.

meta.symbol: carousel component was usable (with deprecation warnings since 3.0)

text: gr.carousel(): Stop using gr.Carousel; it has been deprecated and will be removed.
Stop using `round`, `margin`, and `border` arguments in `Component.style()`; use `gr.Row(variant="compact")` to combine adjacent components without gaps.
Stop using the analytics dependency; it has been removed from the library.
blocks raises an error if duplicate component ids exist: Blocks now raises an error if you have duplicate component IDs; previously it silently allowed duplicates.

meta.symbol: blocks raises an error if duplicate component ids exist

text: gr.image(source='upload', tool='sketch') or similar old sketching api: Stop using the old Image component with tool='sketch' for standalone sketching; use gr.Sketchpad() for black-and-white standalone sketches and gr.Paint() for color standalone sketches instead.

meta.symbol: gr.image(source='upload', tool='sketch') or similar old sketching api

text: http long polling based queue: The queuing system has been changed from HTTP long polling to web sockets.
setting value to none did not reset components to original state: Setting a component's value to None now resets it to its original state instead of leaving it unchanged.

meta.symbol: setting value to none did not reset components to original state

text: integrate() method on some other class: Stop calling integrate() on the old class; call it on Blocks instead.

meta.symbol: integrate() method on some other class

text: gr.inputs and gr.outputs modules: Stop using deprecated gr.inputs and gr.outputs; they have been removed.
Stop using `Model3d` component; use `Image3D` instead.
textbox default value was not empty string: Textbox default value is now an empty string instead of a previous non-empty default.

meta.symbol: textbox default value was not empty string

text: form inputs had debounce behavior: Debounce has been removed from form inputs; input events fire immediately without delay.
gradio components imported directly (e.g. `gr.textbox`): Components must now be imported from `gradio.components` instead of directly from `gradio`.
typer (without [all]): Stop using `typer[all]` and use `typer` without the `[all]` extra, as it is no longer needed.

components with no default value, not set as input/output of an event, and no `interactive` kwarg would behave as if `interactive=false` (or some other default): If a component has no default value, is not bound as input/output of an event, and `interactive` is not set, it now behaves as `interactive=True` instead of the previous behaviour.

meta.symbol: components with no default value, not set as input/output of an event, and no `interactive` kwarg would behave as if `interactive=false` (or some other default)

text: Stop using Python 3.7, 3.8, or 3.9; Jinja2 3.2.0 requires Python 3.10+.
Jinja2 3.2.0 requires MarkupSafe >= 3.0; older MarkupSafe versions will cause install/import failures.
Jinja2 3.2.0 requires Babel >= 2.17; older Babel versions will cause install/import failures.
Python 3.6 is no longer supported; upgrade to Python 3.7+.
undefined no longer acts like strictundefined for the 'in' operator: Stop relying on Undefined raising an error when used with the 'in' operator; it will now return false instead of raising.

meta.symbol: undefined no longer acts like strictundefined for the 'in' operator

text: Stop using Python 2.7; Jinja2 no longer supports Python 2.7 or 3.5.
Stop using Python 3.5; Jinja2 no longer supports Python 3.5.
MarkupSafe dependency must now be at least version 1.1.
Babel optional dependency must now be at least version 2.1.
code previously marked deprecated: All code that was previously marked as deprecated has been removed.
renamed and unified decorator names: The function and filter decorators have been renamed and unified; old names are deprecated.
python 2.6, 3.3, 3.4 support: Stop using Python 2.6, 3.3, and 3.4; they are no longer supported.
finalize is only applied to output of expressions, not static template data: Stop relying on finalize being called for static template data; it now only applies to expression output.

meta.symbol: finalize is only applied to output of expressions, not static template data

text: wordwrap treated existing newlines as part of the line to wrap, creating short intermediate lines: Stop expecting wordwrap to wrap across existing newlines; each paragraph separated by newlines is now wrapped independently.

meta.symbol: wordwrap treated existing newlines as part of the line to wrap, creating short intermediate lines

text: chained comparisons of constants return false if any comparison returns false, matching python behavior: Stop expecting chained constant comparisons to return only the last comparison's result; they now return False if any comparison is False.

meta.symbol: chained comparisons of constants return false if any comparison returns false, matching python behavior

text: passing undefined to get_template (via extends, import, include) could behave inconsistently: Stop relying on passing Undefined values to get_template/extends/import/include working silently; it now consistently raises UndefinedError.

meta.symbol: passing undefined to get_template (via extends, import, include) could behave inconsistently

text: random filter was constant-folded (same value each render): The random filter is no longer constant folded and will produce a new random choice each time the template is rendered.

meta.symbol: random filter was constant-folded (same value each render)

text: this now sets the intended behavior in all situations however it does not restore the old behavior where limited assignments to outer scopes was possible: Assigning to a variable in an inner scope that is also read from an outer scope no longer allows limited assignments to outer scopes; the old behavior is not restored.

meta.symbol: this now sets the intended behavior in all situations however it does not restore the old behavior where limited assignments to outer scopes was possible

text: math operator precedence now follows standard mathematical rules: Math operator precedence has been corrected; existing templates relying on the old precedence may silently produce different results.

meta.symbol: math operator precedence now follows standard mathematical rules

text: urlize now sets rel noopener by default: Urlize now sets rel="noopener" by default on generated links, which may affect link behavior in browsers.

meta.symbol: urlize now sets rel noopener by default

text: macro autoescaping is based on runtime autoescaping at call time: Macro autoescaping now uses the runtime autoescaping setting at call time instead of at macro definition time, which may change escaping behavior of existing macros.

meta.symbol: macro autoescaping is based on runtime autoescaping at call time

text: tests now accept only a restricted expression as first argument: Tests no longer accept an arbitrary expression as the first argument; you must now use parentheses around complex expressions in tests.

meta.symbol: tests now accept only a restricted expression as first argument

text: scoping is now consistent; context is always propagated the same way: The scoping system has been overhauled so context propagation is consistent across include and import; existing templates relying on the old scoping differences may break.

meta.symbol: scoping is now consistent; context is always propagated the same way

text: subtraction now follows the same behavior as other operators on undefined values: Subtraction on undefined values now behaves consistently with other operators, which may change how undefined arithmetic is handled in templates.

meta.symbol: subtraction now follows the same behavior as other operators on undefined values

text: The truncate filter now counts the length of the truncated string, which may change output for existing templates.

meta.symbol: truncate filter now counts the length

text: macros allowed default arguments before non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) with broken behavior: Macros with default arguments followed by non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) now raise a syntax error instead of silently applying the default to the wrong parameter.

meta.symbol: macros allowed default arguments before non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) with broken behavior

text: cache keys use absolute file names if available: Cache keys now use absolute file names instead of load names, which invalidates all previously cached templates.

meta.symbol: cache keys use absolute file names if available

text: default cache size was 50: The default template cache size increased from 50 to 400, which increases memory usage.
slashes were not escaped in query strings for urlencode: The urlencode filter now escapes slashes in query strings, which changes the output for URLs containing slashes.

meta.symbol: slashes were not escaped in query strings for urlencode

text: is number test now accepts decimal as a number: The `is number` test now accepts Decimal values as numbers, which may change behavior for code that relied on Decimal not being a number.

meta.symbol: is number test now accepts decimal as a number

text: sum filter's first argument was the optional starting index (defaults to zero): The sum filter's argument order changed: the attribute name to sum by is now the first argument, and the optional starting index is the second argument.

meta.symbol: sum filter's first argument was the optional starting index (defaults to zero)

text: python 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 3.x (some versions): Python 2.5 is no longer supported; Python 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 are no longer supported; minimum Python 3 version is now 3.3.
Stop using the builtin markup class; switch to markupsafe.

stopiteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are intercepted and converted to undefineds: StopIteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are now intercepted and converted to undefined values instead of propagating.

meta.symbol: stopiteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are intercepted and converted to undefineds

text: unary operators like '-' had wrong precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parsed as '(-foo).bar'): Unary operator precedence was fixed: '-foo.bar' now evaluates as '-(foo.bar)' instead of '(-foo).bar', which may silently change expression results.

meta.symbol: unary operators like '-' had wrong precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parsed as '(-foo).bar')

text: empty implicit tuple expressions were allowed (e.g. `{% if %}`): Implicit tuple expressions can no longer be totally empty; `{% if %}` is now a syntax error.

meta.symbol: empty implicit tuple expressions were allowed (e.g. `{% if %}`)

text: `not` had lower precedence, requiring parentheses: `not (foo in bar)`: Priority of `not` was raised; `not foo in bar` now works without parentheses, which may change evaluation order in existing expressions.

meta.symbol: `not` had lower precedence, requiring parentheses: `not (foo in bar)`

text: attribute named 'overlay' on the environment: The undocumented attribute 'overlay' on the environment was renamed to 'overlayed' because it clashed with a method of the same name.
The speedup extension is now disabled by default, so templates relying on its behavior will silently run without it.

meta.symbol: speedup extension is now disabled by default

text: Index-based subscripting of variables with a constant value now returns an undefined object instead of raising an index error.

subscript (dot-notation and bracket syntax had fixed priority): Subscript is gone and replaced with Getitem and Getattr; dot-notation or bracket syntax can now give attributes or items higher priority.

meta.symbol: subscript (dot-notation and bracket syntax had fixed priority)

text: jinja2.__version__ attribute: Stop using jinja2.__version__; use importlib.metadata.version("jinja2") instead.
the |attr filter bypassed the environment's attribute lookup checks: The |attr filter no longer bypasses the sandbox; attribute access that was previously allowed may now be blocked.

meta.symbol: the |attr filter bypassed the environment's attribute lookup checks

text: The sandbox no longer allows clear() and pop() on known mutable sequence types; code relying on these will raise an error.

calling sync render() on an async template used a different mechanism: Calling sync render() for an async template now uses asyncio.run, which may change event loop behavior in existing async applications.

meta.symbol: calling sync render() on an async template used a different mechanism

text: template.generate_async returned a generator: Template.generate_async now returns an AsyncGenerator that must be properly closed with aclose(); old code that didn't close the generator may leak resources.
Jinja2 3.2.0 uses flit_core instead of setuptools as build backend; custom builds or packaging workflows relying on setuptools may break.
does not allow keys with / solidus, > greater-than sign, or = equals sign: The xmlattr filter now rejects keys containing /, >, or = characters in addition to spaces.

meta.symbol: does not allow keys with / solidus, > greater-than sign, or = equals sign

text: Replace contextfilter and contextfunction with pass_context.
Replace evalcontextfilter and evalcontextfunction with pass_eval_context.
Replace environmentfilter and environmentfunction with pass_environment.
importing markup and escape from jinja2: Markup and escape must now be imported from MarkupSafe instead of Jinja2.

meta.symbol: importing markup and escape from jinja2

text: Context subclasses must override resolve_or_missing instead of resolve; legacy resolve mode is no longer supported.
unicode_urlencode has been renamed to url_quote.
The xmlattr filter does not allow keys with spaces.

meta.symbol: does not allow keys with spaces

text: packageloader included a current directory (.) path segment: Stop relying on PackageLoader including a '.' path segment; template loading from zip imports may now resolve differently.

meta.symbol: packageloader included a current directory (.) path segment

text: Stop using jinja2.Markup() without an argument; use markupsafe.Markup() instead.
undefined.__contains__ raised typeerror: Using 'in' on an Undefined value now raises UndefinedError instead of TypeError.
urls without scheme linked as https://: The urlize filter now links URLs without a scheme as https:// instead of http://.

meta.symbol: urls without scheme linked as https://

text:
only 
, 
,  are line breaks; other characters unchanged: Template rendering now only treats 
, 
, and  as line breaks; other characters are left unchanged.

meta.symbol: only \n, \r\n, \r are line breaks; other characters unchanged

text: autoescape and with are deprecated; built into compiler: The autoescape and with extensions are deprecated; they are now built into the compiler.

meta.symbol: autoescape and with are deprecated; built into compiler

text: contextfunction / contextfilter: Stop using contextfunction and contextfilter; use pass_context instead.
evalcontextfunction / evalcontextfilter: Stop using evalcontextfunction and evalcontextfilter; use pass_eval_context instead.
environmentfunction / environmentfilter: Stop using environmentfunction and environmentfilter; use pass_environment instead.
Stop overriding Context.resolve; override resolve_or_missing instead.
import statement cannot end with a trailing comma: Stop using trailing commas in import statements; they are no longer allowed.

meta.symbol: import statement cannot end with a trailing comma

text: indent filter does not indent blank lines by default: The indent filter no longer indents blank lines by default; existing templates relying on blank lines being indented will produce different output.

meta.symbol: indent filter does not indent blank lines by default

text: block sets are now marked safe by default: Block sets are now marked as safe by default, which may cause double-escaping or missing escaping in templates that manually escaped them.

meta.symbol: block sets are now marked safe by default

text: uses decimal prefixes by default, second parameter to switch to binary: filesizeformat filter now uses decimal prefixes by default; pass a second parameter to use binary mode.

meta.symbol: uses decimal prefixes by default, second parameter to switch to binary

text: else is optional, evaluates to false: Else is now optional for conditional expressions; if omitted it evaluates to false.

meta.symbol: else is optional, evaluates to false

text: The `mono=` parameter in `util.valid_audio` has been removed after deprecation; code passing `mono=` will raise an error.
win_length parameter: The `win_length` parameter in `yin` and `pyin` has been removed; code passing `win_length` to these functions will raise an error.
`librosa.set_fftlib` is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.0; use `scipy.fft.set_backend` instead.
Stop using `filename=` in `librosa.get_duration`; use `path=` instead.
Stop passing `threshold=None` to `librosa.zero_crossings`; it is no longer supported.
`librosa.midi_to_note` (and similar notation converters) now returns `numpy.ndarray` instead of `list` when given iterable inputs.
Deprecations now raise `FutureWarning` instead of `DeprecationWarning`, which may change which warnings are visible by default.
Stop passing positional arguments to functions that now require keyword-only arguments; use keyword arguments instead.

meta.symbol: passing positional arguments to functions that now require keyword-only arguments

text: Stop using Python 3.6 with librosa; upgrade to a supported Python version.
use a different backend (audioread is deprecated): Stop using the audioread backend for audio loading; it is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

meta.symbol: use a different backend (audioread is deprecated)

text: The default resampling method changed from kaiser_best (resampy) to soxr_hq (soxr), which may produce different audio output quality and speed.
The display module now requires matplotlib >= 3.3; older matplotlib versions will cause import or runtime errors.
requires keyword arguments (0.9.0), then warns on positional (0.9.1): Most functions including librosa.load now require keyword arguments instead of positional arguments.

meta.symbol: requires keyword arguments (0.9.0), then warns on positional (0.9.1)

text: default padding mode (likely reflect/other): The default padding mode for most functions including STFT is now zero-padding instead of the previous default.
librosa.display.specshow now uses centered coordinate grids instead of non-centered grids.
Explicit ref values in librosa.effects.trim and librosa.effects.split now measure amplitude rather than power.
librosa.display.waveplot has been removed; use librosa.display.waveshow instead.
librosa.util.example_audio_file has been removed; use librosa.example() instead.
mono parameter default is now false: Stop relying on valid_audio's mono parameter defaulting to True; the default is now False, so audio that is not mono will no longer raise an error by default.

meta.symbol: mono parameter default is now false

text: Stop using librosa.filters.constant_q; use librosa.filters.wavelet instead.
Stop relying on librosa.effects.deemphasis modifying the input signal in-place; it now returns a new array without mutating the input.
Stop using librosa.display.waveplot and use librosa.display.waveshow instead.
Stop using Python versions older than 3.6; the library now requires Python >= 3.6.
Stop using librosa.output; the entire output module has been removed.
Stop using librosa.ifgram; the function has been removed.
librosa.icqt(amin=...): Stop passing the amin parameter to librosa.icqt; it has been removed.
a440 parameter in hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, chroma: Stop passing the A440 parameter to hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, or chroma features; it has been removed.

meta.symbol: a440 parameter in hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, chroma

text: tuning= parameter in cqt filter constructors: Stop passing the tuning parameter to CQT filter constructors; it has been removed.

meta.symbol: tuning= parameter in cqt filter constructors

text: Stop using librosa.util.roll_sparse; the function has been removed.
Stop using librosa.util.optional_jit; the decorator has been removed.
librosa.display.specshow now returns a QuadMesh object instead of the axes handle.
librosa.util.frame has had its default parameters removed, so you must now explicitly provide all arguments.
librosa.util.valid_audio now has more stringent shape requirements, which may reject previously valid audio arrays.
librosa.util.valid_audio no longer requires contiguous memory layout for input arrays.
librosa.feature.chroma_cqt now uses higher CQT resolution by default, which changes the output size and computation cost.
librosa.display.cmap now returns binary colormaps for boolean data instead of continuous colormaps.
librosa.tone produced signals of a certain length: librosa.tone now produces signals of corrected length; existing code relying on the previous (incorrect) length will break.

meta.symbol: librosa.tone produced signals of a certain length

text: rms energy calculation used frame-length normalization in spectrogram-based rms: RMS energy calculation now uses corrected frame-length normalization; existing code relying on the previous RMS values will get different results.

meta.symbol: rms energy calculation used frame-length normalization in spectrogram-based rms

text: rms calculation had incorrect fft normalization: RMS calculation now uses corrected FFT normalization; existing code relying on the previous RMS values will get different results.

meta.symbol: rms calculation had incorrect fft normalization

text: automatic tuning correction for cqt had an error: Automatic tuning correction for CQT is now corrected; existing code relying on the previous (erroneous) tuning behaviour will produce different results.

meta.symbol: automatic tuning correction for cqt had an error

text: librosa.stream seeking behaviour on file-handle inputs is now fixed; existing code relying on the previous (broken) seeking behaviour will break.

meta.symbol: librosa.stream seeking behaviour on file-handle inputs

text: Full-sequence DTW boundary condition check is now corrected; existing code relying on the previous boundary behaviour will produce different results.
Stop using librosa.ifgram and use librosa.reassigned_spectrogram instead.
Stop using librosa.util.roll_sparse as it has been deprecated.
Stop using the A440= parameter and use the standardized tuning= parameter instead.
librosa.util.valid_audio now enforces memory contiguity, so non-contiguous arrays will be rejected.
default audio decoder (audioread): librosa.load now uses pysoundfile as the default audio decoder instead of audioread, which may change supported file formats and behavior.
Stop using librosa's discrete cosine transform; use scipy.fftpack.dct instead.
delta function no longer supports the `trim` keyword argument: Stop passing the `trim` keyword argument to the `delta` function; it is no longer supported.

meta.symbol: delta function no longer supports the `trim` keyword argument

text: rmse (root mean square error): Stop using `rmse`; use `rms` instead.
iirt used a different default mode (not sos): `iirt` now uses `sos` mode by default, which may change its output behaviour.

meta.symbol: iirt used a different default mode (not sos)

text: The `output` module is deprecated and will be removed in version 0.8; stop using it.
Stop using librosa.feature.rmse; use librosa.feature.rms instead.
Stop using librosa.dtw and use librosa.sequence.dtw instead.
Stop using librosa.fill_off_diagonal and use librosa.util.fill_off_diagonal instead.
librosa.util.match_intervals now uses Jaccard similarity instead of raw intersection for matching logic, which changes which intervals are considered matching.
Stop using the real= parameter in librosa.cqt; it has been removed.
Stop using librosa.logamplitude; use librosa.amplitude_to_db instead.
Stop using librosa.beat.estimate_tempo; use librosa.beat.tempo instead.
Stop using the n_fft= parameter in librosa.feature.rmse; it has been removed.
Stop using the ref_power= parameter in librosa.power_to_db; it has been removed.
librosa.write_wav no longer accepts integer-typed waveforms; use soundfile.write for integer output.

librosa.util.normalize did not preserve data type: librosa.util.normalize now preserves the input data type instead of converting to float, which may change behaviour of downstream code expecting float output.

meta.symbol: librosa.util.normalize did not preserve data type

text: librosa.util.frame now has stricter validation requirements, which may reject inputs that previously worked.
librosa.dtw is now symmetric with respect to input arguments, which may change results if you relied on the previous asymmetric behaviour.
librosa.hz_to_midi had documentation errors: librosa.hz_to_midi documentation has been corrected; if you relied on the previously documented (incorrect) behaviour, your code may be wrong.
librosa.decompose.nn_filter has improved efficiency; if you relied on its previous performance characteristics or side effects, behaviour may differ.
The trim= parameter in librosa.feature.delta is deprecated and will be removed in 0.7.0; stop using it now.
librosa.feature.rmse now centers frames in the time-domain by default, changing output alignment for existing code.
librosa.display.specshow now rasterizes images by default, changing the rendering output format.
Stop using librosa.logamplitude; use librosa.amplitude_to_db, db_to_amplitude, power_to_db, or db_to_power instead.
Stop using librosa.beat.estimate_tempo; use librosa.beat.tempo instead.
librosa.output.write_wav normalized by default: librosa.output.write_wav no longer normalizes by default; normalization is disabled by default and only applies to floating point.
seaborn integration in librosa: Stop using seaborn integration; it has been removed and the display submodule now requires matplotlib >= 1.5.
Stop passing use_sns to display.cmap; the argument has been removed.
magma is now the default sequential colormap: The default sequential colormap is now magma instead of whatever was used before.

meta.symbol: magma is now the default sequential colormap

text: librosa.display.specshow now plots using pcolormesh instead of the previous method, and supports non-uniform time and frequency axes.
plots now render in natural coordinates (time or hz): All display plots now render in natural coordinates (e.g., time or Hz) instead of index-based coordinates.

meta.symbol: plots now render in natural coordinates (time or hz)

text: librosa.decompose.hpss with mask=True now returns soft masks instead of binary masks.
Stop using n_fft in librosa.feature.rmse; use frame_length instead.
cqt now uses tuning=0.0 by default: librosa.cqt now disables tuning estimation by default (tuning=0.0) instead of estimating from the signal.

meta.symbol: cqt now uses tuning=0.0 by default

text: stft windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default, breaking compatibility with the 0.4 series.

meta.symbol: stft windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default

text: cqt now returns properly scaled outputs continuous across octave boundaries: cqt now returns properly scaled outputs that are continuous across octave boundaries, breaking compatibility with the 0.4 series.

meta.symbol: cqt now returns properly scaled outputs continuous across octave boundaries

text: Stop using logamplitude; use amplitude_to_db or power_to_db instead.
ref_power parameter in logamplitude: Stop using ref_power in logamplitude; use ref instead.
Stop passing resolution to librosa.cqt; the parameter has been removed.
Stop passing aggregate to librosa.cqt; the parameter has been removed.
Stop passing mode to feature.chroma_cqt; the parameter has been removed.
centering parameter in onset_strength: Stop passing centering to onset_strength; the parameter has been removed.
core.peak_peak function: Stop using core.peak_peak; use util.peak_pick instead.
core.localmax function: Stop using core.localmax; use util.localmax instead.
feature.sync function: Stop using feature.sync; use util.sync instead.
core.ifptrack function: Stop using core.ifptrack; it has been removed.
feature.chromagram function: Stop using feature.chromagram; it has been removed.
feature.logfsgram function: Stop using feature.logfsgram; it has been removed.
filters.logfrequency function: Stop using filters.logfrequency; it has been removed.
output.frames_csv function: Stop using output.frames_csv; it has been removed.
segment.structure_feature class: Stop using segment.structure_Feature; it has been removed.
display.time_ticks function: Stop using display.time_ticks; it has been removed.
util.featureextractor class: Stop using util.FeatureExtractor; it has been removed.
util.buf_to_int function: Stop using util.buf_to_int; it has been removed.
util.small_float constant: Stop using util.SMALL_FLOAT; it has been removed.
librosa.display was automatically imported: librosa.display submodule is no longer automatically imported; you must import it explicitly.
Stop using power=0 in librosa.decompose.hpss; it is no longer supported.
deprecated (no replacement specified): Stop using the aggregate parameter in librosa.cqt and librosa.hybrid_cqt; it is deprecated.
Stop using `fmt` and use `time_fmt` instead in `librosa.display.time_ticks`.
Stop using `mode` and use `cqt_mode` instead in `librosa.feature.chroma_cqt`.
Stop using `resolution` and use `filter_scale` instead in `librosa.cqt`, `hybrid_cqt`, `pseudo_cqt`, and `librosa.filters.constant_q`.
The default `filter_scale` parameter in `librosa.cqt` changed from 2 to 1, which changes the time-frequency tradeoff and output shape.
featureextractor object: Stop using the `FeatureExtractor` object as it has been deprecated.
Stop using the 'centering' parameter in onset_strength; use 'center' instead.

onset_strength could return a longer sequence than the input spectrogram: onset_strength now always trims its output to match the input spectrogram duration, so previously valid longer outputs will be silently truncated.

meta.symbol: onset_strength could return a longer sequence than the input spectrogram

text: Stop importing sync from librosa.feature; import it from librosa.util.sync instead.
librosa.chord module: Stop using librosa.chord; it has been removed entirely.
chroma now starts at c by default: Chroma features now start at C instead of A by default, shifting all chroma bin indices.

meta.symbol: chroma now starts at c by default

text: All librosa-generated exceptions now use the new class librosa.ParameterError instead of generic exceptions.
numpy.fft (np.fft): FFT operations now use scipy.fftpack instead of numpy.fft, which may produce slightly different numerical results.
filters.constant_q did not return filter lengths: filters.constant_q now returns filter lengths in addition to previous outputs, changing the return tuple length.

meta.symbol: filters.constant_q did not return filter lengths

text: Stop using librosa.util.buf_to_int; it is deprecated.
Stop using librosa.output.frames_csv; it is deprecated.
Stop using librosa.segment.structure_feature; it is deprecated.
Stop using librosa.filters.logfrequency; it is deprecated.
Stop using librosa.feature.logfsgram; it is deprecated.
Spectrogram frames are now centered instead of left-aligned, which changes time alignment of all STFT-based features.
librosa.feature.logfsgram() had normalization: Normalization was removed from librosa.feature.logfsgram(), so it no longer normalizes output by default.
perceptual_weighting() was moved from librosa.feature into librosa, so imports from librosa.feature will break.
stack_memory() was moved from librosa.segment into librosa.feature, so imports from librosa.segment will break.
librosa.feature.chromagram() and logfsgram() used energy: librosa.feature.chromagram() and logfsgram() now use power instead of energy, changing output values.
Replace onset_estimate_bpm with estimate_tempo.
n_fft parameter in istft(): Stop passing n_fft to librosa.istft() as it has been removed.
librosa.mel_frequencies returned n_mels+2 values: librosa.mel_frequencies now returns n_mels values instead of n_mels+2 by default.
librosa.decompose.hpss now uses a default window of 31 instead of the previous value.
librosa.onset.onset_strength no longer de-trends by default; you must explicitly enable it.
librosa.ifgram matched ifgram.m interface: librosa.ifgram has broken compatibility with ifgram.m and now matches the stft interface.
librosa.decompose.hpss now uses a default power parameter of 2.0 instead of the previous value.
librosa.load returned double-precision: librosa.load() now returns single-precision (float32) audio by default instead of double-precision.
Replace librosa.output.segment_csv with librosa.output.frames_csv.

old kerning behavior (under-corrected kerning, factor=6): Kerning adjustments now correctly apply kerning; to restore old under-corrected behavior set rcParams['text.kerning_factor'] to 6 instead of 0.
Stop relying on pyplot.axes, Figure.add_axes, and Figure.add_subplot to return an existing Axes when keyword arguments match; they now always create a new Axes.

meta.symbol: previously, these functions would detect if you were attempting to create axes with the same keyword arguments as already-existing axes in the current figure, and if so, they would return the existing axes.

text: Stop passing keyword arguments to gca expecting them to match an existing Axes; kwargs are now only used when no Axes exist in the figure.

meta.symbol: previously, gca accepted keyword arguments; if they matched an existing axes, that axes was returned, otherwise new axes was created with those kwargs.

text: anglea and angleb options on bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) were allowed but did nothing: Bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) now apply angleA and angleB; previously these parameters were silently ignored.

meta.symbol: anglea and angleb options on bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) were allowed but did nothing

text: snapping disabled by default (grid boundary lines visible with transparency): pcolormesh now applies snapping by default, removing grid boundary lines visible with transparency; set rcParams['pcolormesh.snap'] = False to restore old behavior.

meta.symbol: snapping disabled by default (grid boundary lines visible with transparency)

text: use colormap.with_extremes() to copy first then set extremes: Stop using set_bad/set_under/set_over directly on builtin colormaps because they modify in place; use Colormap.with_extremes(bad=..., under=..., over=...) instead which copies first.

meta.symbol: use colormap.with_extremes() to copy first then set extremes

text: colormap.set_extremes() is new but suffers same in-place mutation issue as individual setters: Colormap.set_extremes() modifies the colormap in place just like set_bad/set_under/set_over, so you must copy first; prefer Colormap.with_extremes() which copies automatically.

meta.symbol: colormap.set_extremes() is new but suffers same in-place mutation issue as individual setters

text: xtick.color controlled both tick color and label color: Stop using xtick.color to set tick label color; use xtick.labelcolor instead, which defaults to 'inherit' from xtick.color.

meta.symbol: xtick.color controlled both tick color and label color

text: ytick.color controlled both tick color and label color: Stop using ytick.color to set tick label color; use ytick.labelcolor instead, which defaults to 'inherit' from ytick.color.

meta.symbol: ytick.color controlled both tick color and label color

text: webagg now reports the correct button type for middle-click events: WebAgg backend now correctly reports middle-click events instead of misreporting them as right clicks; code that relied on the old wrong mapping will break.

meta.symbol: webagg now reports the correct button type for middle-click events

text: font subsetting re-written in python; some composite characters and outlines may have changed slightly: Font subsetting in PDF and PostScript has been re-written from C to Python, which may slightly alter some composite characters and outlines in the output.

meta.symbol: font subsetting re-written in python; some composite characters and outlines may have changed slightly

text: Stop using beginarrow and endarrow parameters in ArrowStyle; use the new arrow parameter with arrow strings like '<-', ']-[', ']->' instead.
Stop using matplotlib.cm.get_cmap and use matplotlib.colormaps (or plt.colormaps) with item access instead.
Stop using matplotlib.cm.register_cmap and use matplotlib.colormaps.register() instead.
plot_surface ignored masked arrays and nans, rendering quads even with invalid data: plot_surface now hides quads that contain masked or NaN points, similar to contour with corner_mask=True

meta.symbol: plot_surface ignored masked arrays and nans, rendering quads even with invalid data

text: qt4 backends (qt4agg, qt4cairo): Stop using Qt4 backends; they have been removed. Use Qt5 or Qt6 backends (QtAgg, QtCairo, Qt5Agg, Qt5Cairo) instead.
asyncio-based timer support by default: WebAgg backend now defaults to asyncio instead of Tornado for timer support; code relying on Tornado-specific timer behaviour may break.

setting rcparams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation affected the saved output: rcParams['text.antialiased'] must be set before creating the figure; setting it in a context manager after figure creation is now a no-op.

meta.symbol: setting rcparams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation affected the saved output

text: label_outer() only removed ticklabels, leaving tick lines visible: label_outer() now has a remove_inner_ticks parameter; pass remove_inner_ticks=True to also remove inner tick lines, otherwise behavior may differ from before

meta.symbol: label_outer() only removed ticklabels, leaving tick lines visible

text: legend shadow parameter now accepts dicts in addition to booleans; dicts can contain any keywords for patches.Patch
returns flattened polycollection with only valid (unmasked) polygons: Axes.pcolor now returns a PolyQuadMesh instead of a flattened PolyCollection.

meta.symbol: returns flattened polycollection with only valid (unmasked) polygons

text: shadow darkness was fixed (implicit default): The Shadow patch now has a default shade of 0.7; previously the shadow darkness was fixed and not configurable.
old default label format (likely %1.3f or similar): Contour plots now default to using ScalarFormatter instead of the old default label format; pass fmt="%1.3f" to restore old behavior.

meta.symbol: old default label format (likely %1.3f or similar)

text: Axes.spines is now a Spines container class instead of an OrderedDict; attribute-style access (ax.spines.top) is supported but dict-like access still works.
calling collection.set_array(none) had no effect: Calling Collection.set_array(None) now turns off color mapping; previously it had no effect.

when a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', both faces and edges are left uncolored: When a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', edges are now left uncolored instead of being color-mapped.

meta.symbol: when a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', both faces and edges are left uncolored

text: subplots(sharex=True, sharey=True) now respects rcParams for tick label positioning when deciding which tick labels to hide.

meta.symbol: when sharex=true, x tick labels are hidden for axes not in the first column; when sharey=true, y tick labels are hidden for axes not in the last row.

text: 199 days: Generated leaf certificate validity is reduced from 199 to 197 days to stay under Chromium's 200-day limit with the 2-day notBefore backdate.
longer certificate expiry (previously >199 days): Stop relying on certificates being valid for more than 199 days; the CERT_EXPIRY constant has been reduced to 199 days.

default compression settings (slower, higher compression): Stop expecting default content-encoding compression to use slower/higher-compression settings; all algorithms now use fastest settings by default, which changes output size and performance characteristics.
passlib-based htpasswd parser supporting many hash formats: Stop using passlib-based htpasswd file parsing; the new parser only supports bcrypt and SHA-1 hashing, other hash formats will fail.

meta.symbol: passlib-based htpasswd parser supporting many hash formats

text: debian bullseye/bookworm (previous): Docker images are now built with Debian Trixie instead of the previous Debian release.
non-ascii characters are no longer escaped in json contentview: Non-ASCII characters in the JSON contentview are no longer escaped, which may change displayed output for non-ASCII payloads.

meta.symbol: non-ascii characters are no longer escaped in json contentview

text: user-provided addons triggered after dns resolution: In DNS proxy mode, user-provided addons now trigger before DNS resolution has taken place, which may break addons that relied on DNS resolution having already occurred.

meta.symbol: user-provided addons triggered after dns resolution

text: contentviews now return a plain `str` with the prettified data; syntax highlighting is signaled off-band: Stop relying on contentviews to return syntax-highlighted structured output; they now return a plain string and highlighting is handled separately via tree-sitter.

meta.symbol: contentviews now return a plain `str` with the prettified data; syntax highlighting is signaled off-band

text: a single interactive contentview supporting both known proto definitions and unknown protos: Stop using the old separate gRPC and Protobuf contentviews; they have been replaced by one interactive contentview that handles both known and unknown protos.

meta.symbol: a single interactive contentview supporting both known proto definitions and unknown protos

text: Stop using mitmproxy.dns.Message, use mitmproxy.dns.DNSMessage instead.
mitmweb api did not require an authentication token by default: mitmweb's API now requires an authentication token by default; you must provide a token when making API requests.

meta.symbol: mitmweb api did not require an authentication token by default

text: The `web_password` option replaces the randomly-generated token authentication; use `web_password` to set a fixed secret that survives restarts.
xsrf_token cookie without httponly; samesite=strict: mitmweb's xsrf_token cookie is now HttpOnly and SameSite=Strict, which may break scripts that read or send this cookie from JavaScript.
python 3.11 or below: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.12 or above; running on Python 3.11 or older will fail to start.
All filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default; to restore case-sensitive behaviour set MITMPROXY_CASE_SENSITIVE_FILTERS=1 environment variable.

meta.symbol: filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default

text: block_list addon lowercased filter expressions: The block_list addon no longer lowercases filter expressions; existing filters that relied on automatic lowercasing may break.
blocklist add-on checked status codes: The blocklist add-on no longer checks status codes; existing configurations that relied on status code filtering in blocklist will stop working.
json contentview sorted keys alphabetically: JSON contentview no longer sorts keys; the order of JSON object keys in the display now matches the original response order.
clicking the url in mitmweb places the cursor at the current position: Clicking the URL in mitmweb now places the cursor at the current position instead of selecting the entire URL.

meta.symbol: clicking the url in mitmweb places the cursor at the current position

text: tightened http detection heuristic: Custom TCP-based protocols that were previously misidentified as HTTP may no longer be intercepted by mitmproxy.
stricter http header validation: HTTP requests or responses with malformed headers that were previously accepted will now be rejected or dropped.
modify_headers filters can no longer reference the message body: Filters in the `modify_headers` option that reference the message body will silently stop working.

meta.symbol: modify_headers filters can no longer reference the message body

text: protobuf library was available as a dependency: The `protobuf` library is no longer installed as a dependency of mitmproxy; code that imports it must install it separately.

meta.symbol: protobuf library was available as a dependency

text: Stop checking Connection.tls_version for the string 'QUIC' on QUIC connections; it now returns 'QUICv1' instead.
matches against full host:port string: allow_hosts and ignore_hosts options now match against the full host:port string instead of just the hostname.
The minimum required version of the cryptography library is now 42.0; older versions will cause import errors or CryptographyDeprecationWarnings.
udp traffic exposed as a callback for each packet: Stop using per-packet callbacks for UDP traffic; use stream-based API instead.

meta.symbol: udp traffic exposed as a callback for each packet

text: Stop using list of strings for Subject Alternative Names; use cryptography.x509.GeneralNames instead.
Stop using 'e' to export a flow; use 'x' instead.
relative to current working directory: Stop using script paths relative to the current working directory; paths are now resolved relative to the config file.
app bundle containing mitmproxy/mitmweb/mitmdump cli tools: Stop using standalone mitmproxy binaries on macOS; use the new app bundle that contains the CLI tools instead.

meta.symbol: app bundle containing mitmproxy/mitmweb/mitmdump cli tools

text: mitmproxy-rs on macos now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only provides binary wheels (building from source requires a valid apple developer id): On macOS, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only ships as binary wheels; building from source requires a valid Apple Developer Id.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy-rs on macos now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only provides binary wheels (building from source requires a valid apple developer id)

text: mitmproxy-rs on windows now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only provides binary wheels: On Windows, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only ships as binary wheels.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy-rs on windows now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only provides binary wheels

text: string escaping in raw view: Stop relying on string escaping being shown in the raw view; it is now removed.
setting request.port did not update the host header: Setting Request.port now also updates the Host header if present, matching the behaviour of Request.host.

meta.symbol: setting request.port did not update the host header

text: Stop using the server_replay_nopop option; it has been renamed to server_replay_reuse.
python 3.9 or below supported: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.10 or above; Python 3.9 and earlier are no longer supported.
Stop using the onboarding_port option; the onboarding app now responds to all requests for the hostname specified in onboarding_host.

connection.client and connection.server accepted positional arguments: connection.Client and connection.Server now accept keyword arguments only; stop passing positional arguments to these constructors.

meta.symbol: connection.client and connection.server accepted positional arguments

text: The mode option is now a list of server specs instead of a single spec; update config.yaml to use a list.

setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now also disables early upstream connections to fetch tls certificate details: Setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now disables early upstream connections to fetch TLS certificate details, which may break code that relied on those early connections succeeding.

meta.symbol: setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now also disables early upstream connections to fetch tls certificate details

text: linux binary required glibc >= 2.?? (ubuntu 18.04 based): The Linux binary now requires glibc >= 2.31; running on older systems (e.g. Ubuntu 18.04) will fail with a runtime error.
Stop using pkg_resources; it has been removed as a dependency.

overambitious assertions in the http state machine: Assertions in the HTTP state machine have been removed; code that relied on those assertions crashing on invalid state will now proceed differently.

meta.symbol: overambitious assertions in the http state machine

text: Stop using Python 3.8; mitmproxy now requires Python 3.9 or above.

mailcap files are not considered anymore when opening an external viewer for message contents: Stop relying on mailcap files being used when opening an external viewer for message contents; they are no longer considered.

meta.symbol: mailcap files are not considered anymore when opening an external viewer for message contents

text: non-blocking (async) event hooks: Connection event hooks are now blocking; processing will only resume once the event hook has finished.
user-added header names preserved as-is: User-added header names are now lowercased when using HTTP/2, with a log message emitted to notify the user.
new events (see addon documentation): Stop using clientconnect, clientdisconnect, serverconnect, serverdisconnect, and log events; use the new replacement events documented in the addon docs.
render_priority method: Stop implementing should_render on ContentViews; implement render_priority instead.
The legacy protocol stack has been removed; code depending on it will break.
All deprecated pathod and pathoc tools and modules have been removed.

meta.symbol: pathod and pathoc tools and modules

text: mitmproxy does not assume tls in reverse proxy mode when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided: In reverse proxy mode, mitmproxy no longer assumes TLS when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided; existing configs relying on that auto-detection will silently connect without TLS.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy does not assume tls in reverse proxy mode when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided

text: http/2 push promises support: Support for HTTP/2 Push Promises has been dropped.
python 3.7 and below: Stop using Python versions below 3.8; mitmproxy now requires Python 3.8 or above.
pathod and pathoc tools and modules: Stop using pathod and pathoc; they are deprecated and will be removed in future releases.

meta.symbol: pathod and pathoc tools and modules

text: console_strip_trailing_newlines default was true (trailing newlines were stripped by default): Stop relying on trailing newlines being stripped from console output; they are now preserved by default unless you set console_strip_trailing_newlines=true.

meta.symbol: console_strip_trailing_newlines default was true (trailing newlines were stripped by default)

text: Stop using the SetHeaders addon; use the new ModifyHeaders addon instead.
Stop using the 'New -> File' menu option in mitmweb; use the renamed 'Clear All' menu option instead.
Stop using the `web_iface` option; use the renamed `web_host` option instead.
sigterm asked for confirmation before exiting: Sending SIGTERM to mitmproxy now exits immediately without prompting; SIGINT still asks for confirmation.

meta.symbol: sigterm asked for confirmation before exiting

text: mitmproxy no longer forces the host header on outgoing requests, which may break code that relied on the host header being set.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy no longer forces the host header on outgoing requests

text: serverconnect event is emitted only after connection has been established: The serverconnect event is now emitted only after a connection has been established, not before.

meta.symbol: serverconnect event is emitted only after connection has been established

text: certificates valid for longer than 2 years: Generated certificates now have a lifetime of 2 years instead of the previous longer duration; existing certificates may become invalid sooner.

meta.symbol: certificates valid for longer than 2 years

text: Error messages are now written to sys.stderr instead of sys.stdout; scripts capturing stdout will miss errors.
The config file can now be named config.yaml in addition to config.yml; if both exist, behavior may be ambiguous.

meta.symbol: config file must be named config.yml

text: external editor set via $mitmproxy_editor environment variable: The external editor is now configured via the $MITMPROXY_EDITOR environment variable; previous editor configuration methods may no longer work.

meta.symbol: external editor set via $mitmproxy_editor environment variable

text: old linux and macos releases no longer supported: Old Linux and macOS releases are no longer supported due to migration to GitHub CI.

meta.symbol: old linux and macos releases no longer supported

text: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.6 or later; running on Python 3.5 or earlier will fail to start.
--conf (pointed to options yaml file): The --conf flag is now --confdir and specifies the configuration directory instead of the options yaml file directly.
The allow_remote option has been replaced by block_global and block_private; code using allow_remote will break.
Custom events have been removed entirely; any addon relying on custom events will no longer work.
The cadir option has been renamed to confdir; code referencing cadir will break.

print statements are no longer captured; use ctx.log.info explicitly: Print statements in addons are no longer magically captured and translated to logs; they will silently do nothing visible in logs unless replaced with ctx.log.info.

meta.symbol: print statements are no longer captured; use ctx.log.info explicitly

text: Stop using the HTML outline content view; it has been removed.
python and locust export functionality: Stop using the Python and Locust export features; they have been removed.
websockets implementation based on previous library: The WebSockets implementation has been migrated to wsproto, which changes the underlying library and adds WebSocket compression support.

meta.symbol: websockets implementation based on previous library

text: protobuf decoding required protoc installed on host: Protobuf decoding no longer requires protoc to be installed on the host system.

meta.symbol: protobuf decoding required protoc installed on host

text: png, gif, jpeg parsing required pillow (c-based): PNG, GIF, and JPEG parsing no longer requires Pillow; parsing is now done in pure Python.

meta.symbol: png, gif, jpeg parsing required pillow (c-based)

text: flow file format version decoupled from mitmproxy version: The flow file format version is now decoupled from the mitmproxy version, which may break compatibility with older flow files.

meta.symbol: flow file format version decoupled from mitmproxy version

text: script reloading uses filesystem polling: Script reloading now polls the filesystem instead of using watchdog, which changes reload behavior and reliability.
Stdout in scripts is now redirected to ctx.log.warn instead of printing directly.

meta.symbol: stdout in scripts redirected to ctx.log.warn

text: ~d and ~u filters match against pretty_host: The ~d and ~u filters now match against pretty_host instead of host, which changes which flows are matched.
The private key is no longer included in the PKCS12 file in ~/.mitmproxy, which may break setups that relied on it being there.

meta.symbol: private key was included in pkcs12 file in ~/.mitmproxy

text: Updated to pyOpenSSL 17.5, cryptography 2.1.4, and OpenSSL 1.1.0g, which may break code relying on older APIs.
mitmweb updated to react 16: mitmweb has been updated to React 16, which may break custom mitmweb extensions or themes.
ipv6 ip addresses formatted incorrectly: IPv6 IP address formatting has been fixed, which may break code that relied on the old (incorrect) formatting.
simplified version output: The output of 'mitmproxy --version' has been simplified, which may break scripts parsing the version string.
pathoc did not include host header on connect requests: Pathoc now includes a host header on CONNECT requests, which may break tests or scripts that relied on the old behavior.

meta.symbol: pathoc did not include host header on connect requests

text: error handler was not called on httpexception: The error handler is now called on HTTPException, which may change error handling behavior in addons.

text: could not remove tls from upstream http connections: It is now possible to remove TLS from upstream HTTP connections, which changes the default behavior for upstream connections.

text: could not retroactively increase log verbosity: It is now possible to retroactively increase log verbosity, which changes logging behavior.

meta.symbol: could not retroactively increase log verbosity

text: logging from addons was not thread-safe: Logging from addons is now thread-safe, which may change the order or timing of log messages.

meta.symbol: logging from addons was not thread-safe

text: User scripts can now import names that match hook names (e.g. 'from mitmproxy import log') without errors, which changes import resolution behavior.
reverting duplicated flows crashed mitmproxy: The crash when reverting duplicated flows has been fixed, which changes the behavior of flow reversion.
successive requests went to wrong host after redirect: Successive requests after a redirect now go to the correct host, which changes behavior for redirected flows.

meta.symbol: successive requests went to wrong host after redirect

text: outgoing connections bind to correct interface: Outgoing connections now bind to the correct interface, which changes network routing behavior.

meta.symbol: outgoing connections bind to correct interface

text: custom certificates are no longer ignored in reverse proxy mode: Custom certificates are now respected in reverse proxy mode, which changes TLS behavior for reverse proxy setups.

text: flows from mitmproxy 0.17 could not be imported: Flows created with mitmproxy 0.17 can now be imported, which changes flow import compatibility.

text: http/2 flow replay was broken: HTTP/2 flow replay now works correctly, which changes replay behavior for HTTP/2 flows.
Exporting flows to curl no longer crashes, which changes export behavior.

meta.symbol: exporting flows to curl no longer crashes

text: Refreshing Set-Cookie headers no longer crashes, which changes cookie refresh behavior.

meta.symbol: refreshing set-cookie headers no longer crashes

text: connection indicator added to mitmweb: A connection indicator has been added to mitmweb to alert when the proxy server stops running, which changes the UI.
certificates with cyrillic domains were not supported: Support for certificates with Cyrillic domains has been added, which changes certificate handling for non-ASCII domains.

meta.symbol: certificates with cyrillic domains were not supported

text: A new Request.make method has been added to simplify request creation in scripts, which changes the API for creating requests.
generated certificate stored for each flow: The generated certificate is now stored for each flow, which changes flow metadata and storage.

text: proxy authentication not stored with flows: Proxy authentication is now stored with flows, which changes flow metadata and may expose authentication data.

text: transparent mode broken on ipv6-enabled macos: Transparent mode on IPv6-enabled macOS systems now works correctly, which changes network behavior on macOS.

text: multiple content-length headers not handled correctly: HTTP messages with multiple Content-Length headers are now handled correctly, which changes parsing behavior for malformed messages.

text: ipv6 authority form in connect requests not parsed correctly: IPv6 authority form parsing in CONNECT requests has been fixed, which changes how IPv6 CONNECT requests are handled.

text: event log display broken in mitmweb: The event log display in mitmweb has been fixed, which changes the UI behavior.

meta.symbol: event log display broken in mitmweb

text: brotli encoding no longer causes crash: The crash with brotli encoding has been fixed, which changes behavior for brotli-encoded content.

meta.symbol: brotli encoding no longer causes crash

text: properly falls back to ipv4 when ipv6 unavailable: The proxy now properly falls back to IPv4 when IPv6 is unavailable, which changes network connection behavior.

meta.symbol: properly falls back to ipv4 when ipv6 unavailable

text: setting palette options works correctly: Setting palette options has been fixed, which changes UI customization behavior.
clearing event log no longer crashes: Clearing the event log no longer crashes, which changes event log management behavior.

meta.symbol: clearing event log no longer crashes

text: Duplicating flows no longer crashes, which changes flow duplication behavior.
The testsuite now works when the path contains a '.', which changes test execution behavior.

text: wbxml content view outdated: The WBXML content view has been updated, which changes how WBXML content is displayed.
could not create response to connect in http_connect: It is now possible to create a response to CONNECT requests in http_connect, which changes the http_connect hook behavior.

meta.symbol: could not create response to connect in http_connect

text: filters for websocket flows added: Filters for WebSocket flows have been added, which changes flow filtering behavior.
could not toggle interception: The ability to toggle interception has been added, which changes interception behavior.
ldap proxy authentication backend added: LDAP has been added as a proxy authentication backend, which changes authentication options.
--keep-host-header option added: The --keep-host-header option has been added to retain the host header in reverse proxy mode, which changes reverse proxy behavior.
options editor added to mitmweb: An options editor has been added to mitmweb, which changes the mitmweb UI and configuration interface.
static web-based flow viewer added: A static web-based flow viewer has been added, which changes how flows can be viewed.
Request streaming has been added for HTTP/1.x and HTTP/2, which changes how requests are processed.

text: content views not using kaitai struct: Content views have been reimplemented using Kaitai Struct, which changes how content is parsed and displayed.

text: ico file parser added: A parser for ICO files has been added, which changes content view capabilities.
split view added to mitmproxy ui: A split view has been added to mitmproxy's UI to split into two separate panes, which changes the UI layout.

text: key binding viewer and editor added: A key binding viewer and editor has been added, which changes UI interaction.

text: could not spawn chrome from mitmproxy: A command to spawn a preconfigured Chrome browser instance from mitmproxy has been added, which changes workflow.
