text: mitmproxy had display errors on wsl: mitmproxy now fully supports Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) with display errors worked around, which changes behavior on WSL.

text: xss scanner addon added: An XSS scanner addon has been added, which changes addon availability.
Certificate installation instructions are now provided on mitm.it, which changes the mitm.it behavior.

meta.symbol: no certificate installation instructions on mitm.it

text: http/2 disabled by default: HTTP/2 is now enabled by default; existing setups relying on HTTP/2 being off will now use HTTP/2 unless explicitly disabled.
kaitai struct (kaitai.io) for image parsing: Image ContentView now uses Kaitai Struct instead of Pillow; any code or custom content viewers depending on Pillow-based image parsing will break.
python 2 supported: All mitmproxy tools are now Python 3 only (3.5+); any scripts or deployments running on Python 2 will no longer work.
previous config file format: The config file format is now a single YAML file; existing config files in the old format will not be read and must be converted.

http/2 enabled by default (in 0.18): HTTP/2 is temporarily disabled by default due to widespread protocol implementation errors; existing setups relying on HTTP/2 being on will silently fall back to HTTP/1.1.
Stop importing from libmproxy; import from mitmproxy instead.

.raw_content = body as seen on wire, .content = decompressed body, .text = decompressed and decoded body: Use .raw_content for wire-level body, .content for decompressed body, and .text for decompressed+decoded body; existing code relying on previous semantics will silently get different data.

meta.symbol: .raw_content = body as seen on wire, .content = decompressed body, .text = decompressed and decoded body

text: Stop using the certforward feature; it has been removed entirely.
g/g keyboard shortcuts (previous behaviour): The g and G keyboard shortcuts in mitmproxy console have changed to match less behaviour; existing muscle memory for these keys will break.

previous crypto defaults (sha1, 1024 bit rsa, 1024 bit dh): Crypto defaults changed to SHA256, 2048-bit RSA, and 4096-bit DH parameters; existing hardcoded assumptions about key sizes or hash algorithms will break.

meta.symbol: previous crypto defaults (sha1, 1024 bit rsa, 1024 bit dh)

text: options previously manipulated directly via keybindings: Stop using keybindings to manipulate options directly; use the new options screen ('o' shortcut) instead.

meta.symbol: options previously manipulated directly via keybindings

text: interception certificates stored in-memory: Interception certificates are now stored in-memory instead of on disk, which fixes a race condition and improves Windows compatibility but may affect workflows that relied on certificate files on disk.

upstream certs mode was optional/disabled by default: Upstream certs mode is now enabled by default, which changes how upstream certificates are handled and may break setups that relied on the previous default behaviour.

meta.symbol: upstream certs mode was optional/disabled by default

text: 0.7 serialization format: Saved traffic from mitmproxy 0.8 is not compatible with 0.7 due to server certificates now being stored and serialized.
0.6 serialization format: Serialized data from mitmproxy 0.6 is not compatible with 0.7 due to the move to typed netstrings.

mitmproxy and mitmdump had different/unregularized command-line options: Command-line options for mitmproxy and mitmdump have been regularized, so existing scripts using old option names or formats may break.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy and mitmdump had different/unregularized command-line options

text: mitmproxy only renders the first 100k of large documents and avoids running xml indenter on non-xml data: Viewing large request/response bodies now truncates at 100k and skips XML indentation for non-XML data, so full body content is no longer displayed.

meta.symbol: mitmproxy only renders the first 100k of large documents and avoids running xml indenter on non-xml data

text: Stop using attracting_component_subgraphs(); use component functions and construct subgraphs manually.
Stop using connected_component_subgraphs(); use component functions and construct subgraphs manually.
Stop using weakly_connected_component_subgraphs(); use component functions and construct subgraphs manually.
Stop using strongly_connected_component_subgraphs(); use component functions and construct subgraphs manually.
Stop using biconnected_component_subgraphs(); use component functions and construct subgraphs manually.
Stop using G.nodes_with_selfloops(); use nx.nodes_with_selfloops(G) instead.
Stop using G.number_of_selfloops(); use nx.number_of_selfloops(G) instead.
Stop using G.selfloop_edges(); use nx.selfloop_edges(G) instead.
Stop using G.node; use G.nodes instead.
Stop using G.fresh_copy(); use G.__class__() instead.
Stop using SubMultiGraph; use subgraph_view instead.
Stop using SubMultiDiGraph; use subgraph_view instead.
Stop using MultiGraphView; use generic_graph_view instead.
Stop using MultiDiGraphView; use generic_graph_view instead.
Stop using MultiReverseView; use reverse_view instead.
Stop using is_list_of_ints(); use is_bunch_of_ints() which now tests int(item)==item instead of isinstance(item, int), so floats with integer values are accepted.
weakly_connected_component_subgraphs function: Stop using weakly_connected_component_subgraphs; it has been removed.
Stop using cb.iterable; replace it with np.iterable.
Stop using is_list_of_ints; it has been renamed to make_list_of_ints.
implicit or different i,j ordering: to_numpy_array now explicitly uses i,j (row, column) convention; existing code relying on a different implicit ordering may silently produce transposed matrices.

non-32-bit numbers written as strings: When exporting to GML, numbers that do not fit in 32 bits are now written as strings instead of numbers; code that reads GML output and expects numeric types may break.
transitive_closure did not return self-loops when cycles present: transitive_closure now returns self-loops when cycles are present; existing code that did not expect self-loops may break.

meta.symbol: transitive_closure did not return self-loops when cycles present

meta.symbol: from_pandas_edgelist now handles edgeattr when no columns match request

text: g.node (deprecated node attribute view): Stop using G.node; it has been removed. Use G.nodes instead.
Stop using deprecated signatures of add_path, add_star, add_cycle; they have been removed.

text: spring_layout fixed nodes bug fixed: spring_layout bug with fixed nodes has been fixed; existing code that worked around the bug may now behave differently.
betweenness centrality subset fixed: betweenness_centrality subset parameter bug has been fixed; existing code relying on the buggy behavior may produce different results.
unionfind set extraction fixed: UnionFind set extraction bug has been fixed; existing code relying on the buggy behavior may break.
a* returned wrong solution in some cases: A* algorithm bug that returned wrong solutions has been fixed; existing code relying on the buggy output may break.

meta.symbol: a* returned wrong solution in some cases

text: inverse_line_graph had edge case bugs: inverse_line_graph edge case bugs have been fixed; existing code relying on buggy behavior may break.
lexicographical_topological_sort had a bug: lexicographical_topological_sort bug has been fixed; existing code relying on buggy behavior may break.
partial_duplication_graph had wrong operation order: partial_duplication_graph operation order bug has been fixed; existing code relying on buggy behavior may produce different graphs.
shell_layout kept 1-node shells away from origin: shell_layout now keeps shells with 1 node at the origin instead of away from it; existing code relying on the old positioning may break.

text: label_propagation had wrong edge direction: label_propagation edge direction bug has been fixed; existing code relying on buggy behavior may produce different community assignments.

to_directed had a bug: to_directed function bug has been fixed; existing code relying on buggy behavior may break.
outedgedataview cyclical references removed: OutEdgeDataView cyclical references have been removed; existing code relying on those references may break.

text: Stop using Python 3.5; NetworkX 2.5 requires Python 3.6, 3.7, or 3.8.

Stop using is_string_like(obj); use isinstance(obj, str) instead.

Stop using _naive_greedy_modularity_communities (private); use naive_greedy_modularity_communities (public) instead.
minimum_st_node_cut could return non-set: minimum_st_node_cut now always returns a set; code relying on a different return type will break.
create_py_random_state(has_numpy=...): Stop passing has_numpy to create_py_random_state; the argument has been removed.
Stop using context_manager reversed; use reversed_view instead.
Stop using make_str; it has been deprecated.
Stop using is_string_like; it has been deprecated.
contracted_nodes without copy flag: contracted_nodes now has a copy flag; default behaviour may change so pass copy=True/False explicitly.

text: all_shortest_paths handles zero-weight edges incorrectly: all_shortest_paths now correctly handles zero-weight edges; existing code relying on the old (incorrect) behaviour will break.

from_pandas_edgelist does not overwrite source and target columns: from_pandas_edgelist no longer overwrites source and target columns when converting to DataFrame; existing code relying on overwrite behaviour will break.

text: draw_networkx handles kwds implicitly: draw_networkx now handles kwds explicitly; existing code relying on implicit kwd handling may break.
some functions return empty generator: Some functions now return an empty generator instead of an empty list; code iterating or checking type may break.
numpy float types inferred incorrectly: numpy float types are now correctly inferred; existing code relying on the old incorrect inference may break.
from_graph6_bytes updated args: from_graph6_bytes arguments have been updated; existing code using old argument signatures may break.
view_pygraphviz without show kwarg: view_pygraphviz now has a `show` kwarg; default behaviour may change so pass show=True/False explicitly.
chordal_graph_cliques returns generator: chordal_graph_cliques is being turned into a generator; code relying on list methods may break.
raises networkxerror when k < 2: A NetworkXError is now raised when k < 2; existing code passing k < 2 without handling the error will break.
g.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloops_edges(g)) fails: G.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloops_edges(G)) now works; existing code that worked around this may break.
from_pandas_edgelist allows custom keys for multiedges: from_pandas_edgelist now allows custom keys for multiedges; existing code relying on default key behaviour may break.

text: to/from_numpy_matrix removed internally: to_numpy_matrix and from_numpy_matrix have been removed internally; stop using them.
np.matrix used in algebraic connectivity module: np.matrix has been removed from the algebraic connectivity module; code relying on matrix output may break.

meta.symbol: np.matrix used in algebraic connectivity module

text: np.matrix used in attribute_ac: np.matrix has been removed from attribute_ac; code relying on matrix output may break.
np.int used: np.int has been replaced with np.int_; code using np.int may break on newer NumPy.
Stop using Python 3.6; NetworkX 2.6 requires Python 3.7, 3.8, or 3.9.
decorator library: Stop relying on the 'decorator' library being available as a dependency of NetworkX; it has been removed.
jython/ironpython support: Stop using NetworkX on Jython or IronPython; support code has been removed.
unionfind.union selects arbitrary root: UnionFind.union now selects the heaviest root as the new root instead of an arbitrary one.
pagerank uses a non-scipy default: pagerank now uses scipy by default instead of the previous default.
simrank_similarity uses a non-numpy default: simrank_similarity now uses numpy by default instead of the previous default.
hits uses a non-scipy implementation: hits now uses the scipy implementation by default instead of the previous implementation.
When converting with to_*_array/matrix, nodes in nodelist that are not in G now raise an exception instead of being silently ignored; add them to G first with G.add_nodes_from(nodelist).
draw_networkx_edges now always returns FancyArrowPatch objects instead of LineCollection for undirected graphs, which may cause performance regression for large graphs.
raised keyerror for mapping keys not in graph when copy=false: relabel_nodes now silently ignores keys in mapping that are not nodes in the graph, instead of raising KeyError when copy=False.

text: Stop using performance() and coverage(); use partition_quality() instead, which computes both metrics simultaneously.
label_propagation_communities now returns a dict_values object instead of a generator; wrap with iter() if you need a generator.
returned (tree, root) tuple where root was a uuid string: prefix_tree no longer returns a (tree, root) tuple; it now returns only tree, and the root node is always 0 instead of a UUID.

Stop using the Treebank reference tokeniser; use NLTK's own Tokenizer class instead.

text: Stop using DataFrame.iteritems, Series.iteritems, and HDFStore.iteritems; use DataFrame.items, Series.items, and HDFStore.items instead.

was commutative (a.equals(b) == b.equals(a)): Stop relying on MultiIndex.equals being symmetric when one side has extension array dtype; a.equals(b) may differ from b.equals(a).

now give zero (correct) result: Rolling.var and Rolling.std now return zero (correct) when the window contains identical values.
now give correct (non-nan) result: Rolling.skew and Rolling.kurt no longer return NaN when the window contains identical values.

returns cached identical series object: Accessing a single column of a DataFrame as a Series (df['col']) now always returns a new object every time when Copy-on-Write is enabled, not a cached Series.
lazy copy (deferred until modification): Series constructor with copy=False now creates a lazy copy instead of an immediate copy, deferring the copy until data is modified.

DataFrame.from_records now respects Copy-on-Write when called with a DataFrame.

DataFrame.replace with inplace=True now respects the Copy-on-Write mechanism.
DataFrame.transpose now respects the Copy-on-Write mechanism.
respects copy-on-write mechanism: Inplace arithmetic operations like ser *= 2 now respect the Copy-on-Write mechanism.
DataFrame.__getitem__ now respects Copy-on-Write when the DataFrame has MultiIndex columns.
Series.__getitem__ now respects Copy-on-Write when the Series has a MultiIndex.
Series.view now respects the Copy-on-Write mechanism.
overflow consistent with numpy (int64 overflow): Stop relying on groupby cumsum/cumprod to silently cast to float on overflow; they now overflow like numpy int64 operations.

image.__del__ was available: Image.__del__ is deprecated and should not be relied upon.
py_unicode was used: Py_UNICODE has been replaced with Py_UCS4, which may affect C extension compatibility.
gif deltas are now created from background colour of gif frames if disposal mode is 2: GIF delta frames are now created from the background colour of frames when disposal mode is 2.

meta.symbol: gif deltas are now created from background colour of gif frames if disposal mode is 2

text: TIFF IFD offsets are now stored as unsigned integers, which may affect large TIFF files.

meta.symbol: tiff ifd offsets are now stored as unsigned int

text: preview application path is no longer static: The Preview application path on macOS is no longer hardcoded and is now resolved dynamically.

meta.symbol: preview application path is no longer static

text: ttb text positioning was incorrect: Top-to-bottom (ttb) text positioning has been corrected, which may shift text placement.
1-bit pdf dimensions were incorrect: Dimensions of 1-bit PDF images have been fixed, which may change output size.
opening mmap image through path on windows is fixed: Opening memory-mapped images via Path on Windows now works correctly.

meta.symbol: opening mmap image through path on windows is fixed

text: Stop using PIL.VERSION; it has been removed.

image.thumbnail always resized the image: Image.thumbnail no longer resizes the image if it is already the destination size, so code that relied on a copy being made or on the image always being modified may silently behave differently.

image.__exit__ and __del__ always closed the original fp: Image.__exit__ and __del__ now only close the original file pointer if it is exclusive, so shared file pointers may remain open after context manager exit.

meta.symbol: image.__exit__ and __del__ always closed the original fp

text: eps subprocess stdout was set to devnull: EPS subprocess stdout changed from devnull to None, which may alter subprocess behaviour or error handling.

meta.symbol: eps subprocess stdout was set to devnull

text: Stop using Python 3.4; Pillow no longer supports Python 3.4.
Stop using PIL.OleFileIO; it has been removed.
imageops undocumented functions: Stop using the undocumented functions in ImageOps; they have been removed.
ContainerIO.isatty() now returns a bool instead of an int; code checking for 0/1 may break.
EPS subprocess stdin changed from devnull to None, which may affect subprocess behavior.
Stop assigning to Image.size; it is now read-only and assignments will raise an error.
gif seek removes previous info items: After seeking to a new frame in a GIF, previously set info items are cleared; code that relied on info persisting across seeks will silently lose data.

meta.symbol: gif seek removes previous info items

text: ImageFilter.Kernel and ImageFilter.BuiltinFilter swapped inheritance; isinstance checks and custom subclasses relying on the old hierarchy will break.

meta.symbol: imagefilter.builtinfilter was a subclass of imagefilter.kernel

text: draw.draw_line, draw.draw_point, and font.getabc methods existed: Stop using draw.draw_line, draw.draw_point, and font.getabc; they have been removed.

meta.symbol: draw.draw_line, draw.draw_point, and font.getabc methods existed

text: Stop using PILLOW_VERSION and VERSION; use PIL.__version__ instead.
PIL.version has been renamed to PIL._version and removed from the module's public API.
info dict had priority over encoderinfo when saving gif images: When saving GIF images, encoderinfo now takes priority over info, which may silently change output if both are set.

meta.symbol: info dict had priority over encoderinfo when saving gif images

text: Saving a multiframe image as PDF now saves only a single frame instead of all frames.

meta.symbol: saving a multiframe image as pdf now saves only a single frame

text: now only extracts the first exif segment: Only the first Exif segment is now extracted; additional Exif segments are ignored.

meta.symbol: now only extracts the first exif segment

text: Stop using _imaging.crc32; use the built-in Python crc32 implementation instead.
The scripts directory has been removed; scripts are no longer shipped with Pillow.
check parameter in _save methods: Stop passing the check parameter to _save in BmpImagePlugin, PngImagePlugin, ImImagePlugin, PalmImagePlugin, and PcxImagePlugin; it has been removed.

decompression bomb error raised for images 2x larger than a decompression bomb warning: A decompression bomb error is now raised for images 2x larger than the warning threshold, instead of at the same threshold.

meta.symbol: decompression bomb error raised for images 2x larger than a decompression bomb warning

text: The zip argument in PngImagePlugin.add_text() now requires a bool type; passing non-bool values will break.
source directory layout prevents accidental import of pil: The source directory layout has been reworked to prevent accidental import of PIL, which may break imports that relied on the old layout.

meta.symbol: source directory layout prevents accidental import of pil

text: __main__ code in imagecms: The __main__ code has been removed from ImageCms; running ImageCms as a script no longer works.
Stop using platform.dist() in setup; replace with file existence checks.
Stop using PIL.OleFileIO; it now raises an ImportError directing users to the olefile package.
olefile was required: The olefile dependency is now optional; code that relied on it being always available may break if olefile is not installed.
Stop using ImageOps.gaussian_blur() and use ImageFilter.GaussianBlur instead.
Stop using ImageOps.gblur() and use ImageFilter.GaussianBlur instead.
Stop using ImageOps.unsharp_mask() and use ImageFilter.UnsharpMask instead.
Stop using ImageOps.usm() and use ImageFilter.UnsharpMask instead.
Stop using ImageOps.box_blur() and use ImageFilter.BoxBlur instead.
renamed parameter (not shadowing built-in dict): Stop using the parameter named 'dict' in PDF saving; use the renamed parameter instead.
deprecated code from pil 1.1.4 era: Stop using all deprecated code that was removed in this version.
Stop using the WITH_DEBUG compilation flag; it has been removed.

image.crop always returned a new image: Image.crop now returns a copy only when crop dimensions match the image dimensions; otherwise it may return a view, so modifying the cropped image may affect the original.

meta.symbol: image.crop always returned a new image

text: imagefile.parser raised unboundlocalerror in some cases: ImageFile.Parser now raises TypeError instead of UnboundLocalError; catch TypeError instead.

meta.symbol: imagefile.parser raised unboundlocalerror in some cases

text: imagedraw.setink() method: Stop using ImageDraw.setink() as it has been removed.
imagedraw.setfill() method: Stop using ImageDraw.setfill() as it has been removed.
imagedraw.setfont() method: Stop using ImageDraw.setfont() as it has been removed.
no spaces allowed in tiff keyword argument names: Stop using spaces in TIFF keyword argument names; they were deprecated in 2.7 and are now removed.

meta.symbol: no spaces allowed in tiff keyword argument names

text: missing resolutionunit defaulted to some interpretation: When ResolutionUnit is missing in TIFF, the default interpretation is now inch instead of whatever it was before.

meta.symbol: missing resolutionunit defaulted to some interpretation

text: Stop using the internal _stretch() method on Image objects; it has been removed.
Stop using the handles_eof flag in decode.c; it has been removed.
pil.olefileio (vendored copy): Stop importing PIL.OleFileIO; the vendored olefile copy has been removed in favor of the upstream olefile package.
Stop using Image._makeself(); use Image._new() instead.
zero-size images rejected (3.4 behavior): Zero-size images (0 width or height) are now allowed again, reverting the 3.4 change that rejected them.
Stop using Python 2.6 and Python 3.2; Pillow no longer supports these Python versions.
The default box argument for Image.paste() now matches documented behaviour; existing code relying on the old default may produce different results.
Stop using Image.core.open_ppm; it has been removed and negative image size checks are now in Image.py.
image.crop was lazy (deferred): Image.crop() is now an immediate operation instead of lazy; code that relied on deferred cropping behaviour will break.
pixel centers were at integer coordinates (0.5 offset behavior): Image.transform now respects pixel centers at 0.5 offset, which shifts output compared to previous behavior.

meta.symbol: pixel centers were at integer coordinates (0.5 offset behavior)

text: saving rgba image as jpeg silently succeeded (dropping alpha): Saving an RGBA image as JPEG now emits a warning instead of silently dropping the alpha channel.

meta.symbol: saving rgba image as jpeg silently succeeded (dropping alpha)

text: The enum values for Resolution Unit and Predictor in TiffTags.py were fixed, which may change the numeric values previously used.

crc checks are skipped in png files when load_truncated_images is enabled: When LOAD_TRUNCATED_IMAGES is enabled, CRC checks in PNG files are now skipped, which may allow corrupted data to load silently.

meta.symbol: crc checks are skipped in png files when load_truncated_images is enabled

text: large text blocks in png are ignored when load_truncated_images is enabled: When LOAD_TRUNCATED_IMAGES is enabled, large text blocks in PNG files are now ignored instead of being loaded.

meta.symbol: large text blocks in png are ignored when load_truncated_images is enabled

text: pthread-based incremental.c with old file decoder/encoder interface: The pthread-based Incremental.c was removed and the file decoder/encoder interface was rewritten, which may break custom decoders/encoders.

meta.symbol: pthread-based incremental.c with old file decoder/encoder interface

text: Stop passing image info as a list to Image.new; use the current dict-based format instead.

meta.symbol: passing image info as a list (pre-1.5.2 format) to image.new

text: deprecated functions raise notimplementederror: Deprecated functions now raise NotImplementedError instead of Exception, so catching Exception broadly will no longer catch them.
imagefile loads images in read-only mode: ImageFile now loads images in read-only mode, so any code that tries to modify loaded image data in-place will fail.

meta.symbol: imagefile loads images in read-only mode

text: Stop using the as_dict method; it was deprecated in 3.0.0 and a warning is now emitted.
imagesequence iterator was not an iterator: ImageSequence Iterator is now a proper iterator; code that relied on it being iterable via __getitem__ without __iter__ may break.

meta.symbol: imagesequence iterator was not an iterator

text: image.crop accepted non-integer arguments without rounding: Image.crop now rounds its arguments to the nearest integer; previously non-integer arguments were accepted as-is, which could shift crop regions.

meta.symbol: image.crop accepted non-integer arguments without rounding

text: Exif support has been rolled back to the pre-3.0 format; code relying on the 3.0 Exif format will break.
gpsexif data in v3.0 form: GPS Exif data has been restored to the v1 format; code relying on the v3.0 GPS Exif structure will break.

arcs, chords, and pie slices now use float coordinates: Drawing arcs, chords, and pie slices now uses float coordinates instead of integers; code passing integer arguments may silently misbehave if it relied on integer truncation.

meta.symbol: arcs, chords, and pie slices now use float coordinates

text: The pilprint script replaced os.popen with subprocess.Popen; code calling pilprint or relying on its os.popen behavior may break.
_imagingtiff.c and pilplus stuff: Old _imagingtiff.c and pilplus code has been removed; any code depending on these modules will break.
Stop using ImageDraw.setink; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using ImageDraw.setfill; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
converted qimage to pillow image using ppm format (lost alpha): When converting from QImage to Pillow Image, the intermediate format changed from ppm to png, which now preserves the alpha channel.

meta.symbol: converted qimage to pillow image using ppm format (lost alpha)

text: qt could accept unaligned image data: Qt now requires 32-bit aligned image data; unaligned data will cause errors or corruption.

meta.symbol: qt could accept unaligned image data

text: old tiff imagefiledirectory implementation: The Tiff ImageFileDirectory has been rewritten; code relying on its internal structure or behavior may break.
used print statements for debug output: Pillow now uses the logging module instead of print statements; code that captured stdout for debug info will break.

meta.symbol: used print statements for debug output

text: Stop using Tk versions earlier than 8.4; they are no longer supported.
Stop using FreeType 2.0; it is no longer supported.
underscores in tiff tag kwargs: Use underscores, not spaces, in TIFF tag keyword arguments.
Stop using the Sane module from Pillow; it has been split into a separate repository at https://github.com/python-pillow/Sane.
nearest (or other) filtering for thumbnails: Image.thumbnail now uses Bicubic filtering by default instead of the previous default.
new radius interpretation (iterated fast box blur): ImageFilter.GaussianBlur radius parameter is interpreted differently than before due to replacement with iterated fast box blur.

meta.symbol: new radius interpretation (iterated fast box blur)

text: Image.resize was replaced: the underlying implementation changed from affine-based resize to convolution-based stretch.
Stop using ImagePalette.new() as it has been removed.
arg (animated raster graphics) support: Stop using ARG (Animated Raster Graphics) format support as it has been removed.
default interpolation (likely nearest or bilinear): Image.thumbnail now defaults to Image.ANTIALIAS interpolation instead of the previous default.
image.__eq__ without class checking: Image.__eq__ now checks that both objects are the same class before comparing, which may break equality comparisons between different image types.
FreeTypeFont.textsize() now includes font offsets in its return value, which may break code that relied on the previous dimensions.
transformed images not returning profile: Transformed images now return a Profile object, which may break code that did not expect this return value.
transparency resource is removed after p->rgba conversion: After converting from P mode to RGBA mode, the transparency resource is removed.

meta.symbol: transparency resource is removed after p->rgba conversion

text: indexed transparency was not handled during conversions between l, rgb, and p modes: Indexed transparency is now handled when converting between L, RGB, and P modes, which may change the output of existing code that relied on the old behavior.

meta.symbol: indexed transparency was not handled during conversions between l, rgb, and p modes

text: conversions from rgba to p mode were not enabled: Conversions from RGBA to P mode are now enabled, which may change behavior for code that previously failed or was not supported.

meta.symbol: conversions from rgba to p mode were not enabled

text: palette handling when converting from p to rgb and back to p was incorrect: Palette handling when converting from P mode to RGB and back to P mode has been fixed, which may change output for existing code.

meta.symbol: palette handling when converting from p to rgb and back to p was incorrect

text: saving a mode p image as png with transparency set to palette color 0 was broken: Saving a mode P image as PNG where transparency is palette color 0 now works correctly, which may change output for existing code.

meta.symbol: saving a mode p image as png with transparency set to palette color 0 was broken

text: heuristic for saving progressive and optimized jpegs with high quality values has been improved: The heuristic used when saving progressive and optimized JPEGs with high quality values has been improved, which may change output file size/quality.

meta.symbol: heuristic for saving progressive and optimized jpegs with high quality values has been improved

text: pixelaccess returns unsigned ints for i16 mode: PixelAccess now returns unsigned integers for I16 mode instead of signed integers.

meta.symbol: pixelaccess returns unsigned ints for i16 mode

text: transparency header was not saved by default for pngs: PNG transparency header (tRNS) is now saved by default, which may change output file size or behavior for consumers that did not expect a transparency header.

meta.symbol: transparency header was not saved by default for pngs

text: lcms version 1: LCMS support was upgraded from version 1 to version 2; code relying on LCMS v1 API or behavior will break.
lossless webp is supported via save(lossless=true): Passing 'lossless=True' to save() now enables lossless WebP; existing code that did not expect this parameter may need updates.

meta.symbol: lossless webp is supported via save(lossless=true)

text: Compression can now be passed as an option to save() for TIFF files; existing code that relied on default compression behavior may produce different output.
image.point may have had issues with numpy arrays: Image.point behavior with numpy arrays was fixed; code that worked around previous bugs may break or behave differently.

meta.symbol: image.point may have had issues with numpy arrays

text: rgba tiff with missing extrasamples tag may have been handled differently: RGBA TIFF files missing the ExtraSamples tag are now supported; previously failing files may now load, or code that relied on the old error behavior may break.

meta.symbol: rgba tiff with missing extrasamples tag may have been handled differently

text: transform and resize did not handle alpha premultiplication: Alpha premultiplication is now supported for transform and resize; existing code that manually handled premultiplication may produce double-premultiplied results.

meta.symbol: transform and resize did not handle alpha premultiplication

text: pillow < 2.0.0 supports python 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7: Pillow 2.0.0 drops support for Python 2.4 and 2.5; code running on those versions will fail to import.
StringIO has been replaced with BytesIO; code passing strings where bytes are expected will break.
python 3 compatible approach without changing basestring: basestring is no longer available in Python 3; code using basestring checks will break on Python 3.

meta.symbol: python 3 compatible approach without changing basestring

text: previous py_version_hex value: PY_VERSION_HEX has been changed, which may break code that checks specific version hex values.
everything under pil namespace: All modules have been moved under the PIL namespace; imports from outside PIL will fail.
silently skipping unavailable features: Using --enable-x will now raise an error if the feature x is not available, instead of silently skipping.
explicit --disable-* options: Features can now be explicitly disabled via --disable-* options, which may cause previously auto-detected features to be missing.
Stop using 'import Image' and use 'from PIL import Image' instead.
set to the number of iterations from netscape loop extension: The GIF loop info property now reflects the actual number of iterations from the NETSCAPE loop extension instead of always being 1.

meta.symbol: set to the number of iterations from netscape loop extension

text: stores all repeated iptc tags; multiple instances become a list, single instances remain strings: Repeated IPTC tags are now stored as a Python list of all instances instead of only the last instance as a string.

meta.symbol: stores all repeated iptc tags; multiple instances become a list, single instances remain strings

text: arg plugin was available: Stop using the ARG plugin; it is now marked as obsolete.
display profile (imagecms wording): Stop using the term 'display profile' in ImageCms; variable names and docstrings now use 'proof profile' instead.
image.show used its own implementation: Image.show now delegates to ImageShow; the 'display' utility is picked when available, and custom display utilities must be registered via ImageShow.
previous jpeg cmyk handling: JPEG CMYK handling has been revised; existing code relying on the old CMYK behavior may produce different results.
ImageDraw.text now correctly positions strings where the first character has a negative left bearing, instead of clipping it.
ImageDraw.floodfill is now available as an experimental function.

frombuffer(mode, size, data) worked without extra arguments: Image.frombuffer default arguments no longer match fromstring; calls using only (mode, size, data) will print a warning and must be updated to include the full argument list.

meta.symbol: frombuffer(mode, size, data) worked without extra arguments

text: ImageEnhance.Contrast now calculates the mean value correctly, changing the contrast adjustment behaviour.
resolution unit handled incorrectly: TIFF reader/writer now handles resolution unit correctly, which may change how existing TIFF files are read or written.
inverted cmyk jpeg files are now corrected on read: Inverted CMYK JPEG files are now automatically corrected when reading, changing the appearance of such images.

meta.symbol: inverted cmyk jpeg files are now corrected on read

text: ImageColor.getcolor no longer has a grayscale rounding error, changing the exact color values returned for grayscale inputs.

cmyk jpeg images were treated with varying polarity (possibly adobe vs. conventional cmyk): CMYK JPEG images now always use Adobe CMYK polarity; existing images that relied on the old polarity will have inverted colors.

meta.symbol: cmyk jpeg images were treated with varying polarity (possibly adobe vs. conventional cmyk)

text: imagedraw.draw class (instantiated directly): Stop instantiating ImageDraw.Draw as a class; use the factory function instead. If you need custom draw classes, inherit from ImageDraw class.
threaded show viewer (windows): Stop using the threaded 'show' viewer on Windows; the old 'show' implementation is used instead.
imagedraw.line() without width parameter: ImageDraw.line() now has a width option, but the implementation does not support line joins, so polylines will not look good with width.

point() mapping from mode i to mode l with any lookup table: When using point() to map from mode 'I' to mode 'L', only 16-bit values are supported (others are clipped), the lookup table must contain exactly 65536 entries, and the mode argument must be set to 'L'.

meta.symbol: point() mapping from mode i to mode l with any lookup table

text: Stop using the old WMF driver; it has been replaced with a WMF stub plugin.
_imagingft.c now requires FreeType 2.1 or newer; building with older versions needs manual work.
ImageDraw now uses an embedded default font when no font is specified, instead of requiring an explicit font via load() or truetype().
show() used an external viewer on windows: The show() method now uses a built-in image viewer (ImageWindow) on Windows instead of an external viewer.

meta.symbol: show() used an external viewer on windows

text: exif data was extracted via _getexif() method: EXIF extraction now uses the exif dictionary with key 0x8825 (GPSInfo) instead of the _getexif() method.

meta.symbol: exif data was extracted via _getexif() method

text: ImageDraw.Draw now accepts "RGBA" as the second argument to enable experimental RGBA-on-RGB drawing; existing code passing a mode string may silently get different behaviour if RGBA is passed.
ImageWin.Dib can now be constructed directly from an image object; existing code that wrapped an image in another object before passing it may break.
ImageWin now accepts window handles wrapped in ImageWin.HWND in addition to device contexts; existing code passing a raw window handle where a device context was expected may silently misbehave.

meta.symbol: also accepts window handles wrapped in imagewin.hwnd

text: always writes compression tag, even with default compression: TIFF writer now always writes the Compression tag, even when using default compression; existing code relying on the tag being absent may break.

meta.symbol: always writes compression tag, even with default compression

text: can now use rgb tuples or colour names; drawing layer automatically assigns color indexes: Drawing on mode P images now accepts RGB tuples and colour names, automatically assigning color indexes; existing code that manually managed palette indexes may conflict with automatic assignment.

meta.symbol: can now use rgb tuples or colour names; drawing layer automatically assigns color indexes

text: ImageDraw, Image.new, and Image.paste now accept CSS3-style color strings; existing code that passed a string that could be misinterpreted as a CSS3 color may silently produce different colours.

meta.symbol: did not accept css3-style color strings in imagedraw, image.new, image.paste

text: antialias filter (pre-1.1.3c1): Stop using the ANTIALIAS filter; it caused segmentation violations due to an improperly allocated internal buffer.
regex module: Stop using the 'regex' module; all uses have been replaced with the 're' module.
Stop using Image.offset() and use ImageChops.offset() instead.
Stop using bdf2pil.py; use pilfont.py instead.

paste method generated a temporary source image when passed a colour value: The paste method now accepts a colour value (number or tuple) directly instead of requiring a source image, and handles it efficiently at the C level.

meta.symbol: paste method generated a temporary source image when passed a colour value

text: When using convert with palette=Image.ADAPTIVE, dithering cannot be used.
ImageDraw.rectangle() now includes the right and bottom edges when drawing filled rectangles, so existing code that relied on the old boundary may draw one pixel larger than expected.
returned ('y', 'cb', 'cr') or possibly different tuple: getbands() on a YCbCr image now returns ('Y', 'Cb', 'Cr') instead of any previous band naming.
The mode name 'YCrCb' has been replaced with 'YCbCr'. Any code using 'YCrCb' as a mode string will break.
Stop relying on GIF files being non-interlaced by default; pass interlace=0 to save() to get the old non-interlaced behavior.

first line in string buffer was bottom line; rgb had 4 bytes per pixel: Stop relying on fromstring/tostring default format having bottom-line-first and 4 bytes per pixel for RGB; use explicit 'raw' 'RGBX' arguments to get old behavior.

meta.symbol: first line in string buffer was bottom line; rgb had 4 bytes per pixel

text: bmp driver saved mode "1" images in some other mode or format: The BMP driver now saves mode "1" images; when loading, 8-bit files with grayscale palettes get mode "L" and other 8-bit files get mode "P".

meta.symbol: bmp driver saved mode "1" images in some other mode or format

text: im driver did not read/save "1" images: The IM driver now reads and saves "1" images (file modes "0 1" or "L 1").

meta.symbol: im driver did not read/save "1" images

text: jpeg driver did not save "1" images: The JPEG driver now saves "1" images as 8-bit grayscale (will load as mode "L").

meta.symbol: jpeg driver did not save "1" images

text: gif driver did not save "1" images: The GIF driver now saves "1" images; the image will be loaded as a "P" image.

meta.symbol: gif driver did not save "1" images

text: adding a palette to an "l" image kept it as "l": If a palette is added to an "L" image via putpalette, it is automatically converted to a "P" image.

meta.symbol: adding a palette to an "l" image kept it as "l"

text: imagetk did not honour alpha layer in rgba images: ImageTk now honours the alpha layer in RGBA images, treating it as a mask (only fully transparent pixels become transparent).

meta.symbol: imagetk did not honour alpha layer in rgba images

text: photoimage used a special image type: The PhotoImage code in ImageTk now uses a Tcl command ("PyImagingPaste") instead of a special image type, and TKAPPINIT MUST BE MODIFIED.

meta.symbol: photoimage used a special image type

text: image.new() returned uninitialized image by default: Stop relying on Image.new() returning uninitialized memory; use None as the color argument to get an uninitialized image, or expect black-initialized images.

meta.symbol: image.new() returned uninitialized image by default

text: pilconvert no longer optimizes by default: Stop expecting pilconvert to produce smallest files by default; use the -o flag to enable optimization, and -q to set JPEG quality.

meta.symbol: pilconvert no longer optimizes by default

text: pil only scans fully-qualified directory names in the path for plugins: Stop relying on PIL finding plugins from non-fully-qualified directory names in the path; only fully-qualified directory names are scanned.

meta.symbol: pil only scans fully-qualified directory names in the path for plugins

text: mng (multi-image network graphics) read support: Stop using MNG support; it has been removed entirely.
must pass a tuple: (red, green, blue), (red, green, blue, alpha), or (cyan, magenta, yellow, black): For multilayer images, the colour argument to 'new' must now be a tuple, not a single value.

meta.symbol: must pass a tuple: (red, green, blue), (red, green, blue, alpha), or (cyan, magenta, yellow, black)

text: pad byte set to some other value (likely 0): RGB unpackers and converters now set the pad byte to 255 (full opacity) instead of the previous value.

meta.symbol: pad byte set to some other value (likely 0)

text: ImageDraw 'line' and 'polygon' methods now also accept Path objects, not just lists.

can be constructed directly from an image and passed directly to tkinter: ImageTk PhotoImage can now be constructed directly from an image and passed directly to Tkinter instead of using the 'image' attribute.

meta.symbol: can be constructed directly from an image and passed directly to tkinter

text: Using 'paste' on a displayed ImageTk PhotoImage automatically updates the display.
takes same arguments as tkinter bitmapimage class: ImageTk BitmapImage constructor now takes the same arguments as the Tkinter BitmapImage class, using 'foreground' option for colour.

meta.symbol: takes same arguments as tkinter bitmapimage class

text: optional floating point scale and offset adjust data to 8-bit pixel range: Image putdata now accepts optional floating point scale and offset parameters to adjust data into 8-bit pixel range.

meta.symbol: optional floating point scale and offset adjust data to 8-bit pixel range

text: added extent method for cropping, stretching, shrinking, mirroring subregions: Image transform now supports the EXTENT method to quickly crop, stretch, shrink, or mirror a subregion from another image.

meta.symbol: added extent method for cropping, stretching, shrinking, mirroring subregions

text: returns string with xbm representation of image: Image now provides a 'tobitmap' method returning a string containing an XBM representation of the image.

meta.symbol: returns string with xbm representation of image

text: accepts arbitrary python sequences (including strings): Where earlier versions accepted lists, this version accepts arbitrary Python sequences (including strings, in some cases).
Image.paste() now allows the box to extend outside the target image; the size of the box, the image to be pasted, and the optional mask must still match.

paste now accepts '1' masks where zero means transparent and any other pixel value means opaque: The paste method now accepts '1' masks where zero means transparent and any other pixel value means opaque, which is faster than using an 'L' transparency mask.

meta.symbol: paste now accepts '1' masks where zero means transparent and any other pixel value means opaque

text: point() did not accept a mode argument: Image.point() now takes an optional mode argument, currently only usable to convert 'L' or 'P' images to '1' images (thresholding).

meta.symbol: point() did not accept a mode argument

text: getdata() did not exist or returned a copy: Image.getdata() returns a reference to the existing image data, so changes to the image are immediately reflected in the returned sequence object.

meta.symbol: getdata() did not exist or returned a copy

text: (0,0) was located elsewhere (e.g. lower left corner): The coordinate system origin changed so that (0,0) is now in the upper left corner instead of the lower left corner.

meta.symbol: (0,0) was located elsewhere (e.g. lower left corner)

text: image.join(): The Image join function has been renamed to merge; stop using join and use merge instead.
Stop using Image.tostring; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using Image.fromstring; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using Image.offset; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using ImageFileIO; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using ImageFont.FreeTypeFont; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
imagefont.truetype(file=...): Stop using the `file` keyword argument in ImageFont.truetype; it has been removed.
imagepalette._make* functions: Stop using ImagePalette private _make functions; they have been removed.
Stop using ImageWin.fromstring; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using ImageWin.tostring; it has been removed with no direct replacement.
Stop using 'sendDataToCloud' modebar button; use 'sendChartToCloud' instead.

returns notimplemented to give the other operand an opportunity to handle the comparison: The __eq__ method on graph_objects classes now returns NotImplemented instead of True/False, which changes how equality comparisons behave when comparing with non-plotly objects.

meta.symbol: returns notimplemented to give the other operand an opportunity to handle the comparison

text: numpy with a version less than 1.22 is no longer supported.
Stop using create_hexbin_mapbox; use create_hexbin_map instead.

axis autorange increases to avoid clipping outside text labels on bars: Axis autorange now increases when bar charts have outside text labels to prevent clipping, which may change the axis range of existing plots.

meta.symbol: axis autorange increases to avoid clipping outside text labels on bars

text: kaleido v0 (deprecated): Kaleido v0 is deprecated and will emit deprecation warnings; use Kaleido >= v1.0.0 for image export.
fig.show() may have worked incorrectly when ipython is installed: fig.show() behaviour when ipython is installed has been fixed; existing code relying on the broken behaviour may break.

meta.symbol: fig.show() may have worked incorrectly when ipython is installed

text: width/height in plot layout are now respected during kaleido image export: Width and height in plot layout are now respected during Kaleido image export; existing code that relied on them being ignored may produce different-sized images.

meta.symbol: width/height in plot layout are now respected during kaleido image export

text: Stop using the pointcloud trace type; it has been removed from the API.
Stop using the heatmapgl trace type; it has been removed from the API.
tenacity dependency: Stop relying on tenacity being available as a transitive dependency; it has been dropped.
jupyter notebook version 6 and earlier: Stop using Jupyter Notebook version 6 or earlier; the minimum supported version is now 7.0.0.
titlefont, titleposition, titleside, titleoffset: Stop using titlefont, titleposition, titleside, and titleoffset attributes; they have been removed.
pointcloud traces, heatmapgl traces, gl2d subplots: Stop using pointcloud and heatmapgl traces and gl2d subplots; they have been removed.

meta.symbol: pointcloud traces, heatmapgl traces, gl2d subplots

text: Stop using bardir; use orientation instead.
Stop using annotation.ref; use annotation.xref and annotation.yref instead.
alpha channel of error bar color attribute: Stop using error bar opacity attribute; use the alpha channel of the error bar color attribute instead.

meta.symbol: alpha channel of error bar color attribute

text: Stop using gl3d.cameraposition; use gl3d.camera instead.
plot3dpixelratio config option: Stop using plot3dPixelRatio in config; it has been removed.
zauto, zmin, zmax on surface traces: Stop using zauto, zmin, and zmax on surface traces; they have been removed.

meta.symbol: zauto, zmin, zmax on surface traces

text: Stop using autotick attributes on cartesian axes; they have been removed.
transforms api: Stop using transforms in the API; they have been removed.
Mapbox-based traces are deprecated; migrate to Maplibre-based traces.
Stop using mapbox traces (scattermapbox, choroplethmapbox, densitymapbox) and mapbox subplot; use maplibre-based scattermap, choroplethmap, densitymap traces and map subplots instead.

meta.symbol: mapbox traces (scattermapbox, choroplethmapbox, densitymapbox) and mapbox subplot

text: Stamen map tile styles now point to stadiamaps.com and require an API key via config.mapboxAccessToken; existing code using Stamen styles without an API key will break.

meta.symbol: stamen styles now point to stadiamaps.com

text: Python 3.6 and Python 3.7 are no longer supported; code running on those versions will fail to install or run.
autorange now supports 'min', 'max', 'min reversed', 'max reversed': Stop using only 'reversed' for autorange; use 'min reversed' or 'max reversed' for partial-range reversed autoranging.
scaleanchor now accepts 'false' to remove a constraint set by default: Stop assuming scaleanchor cannot be disabled; set scaleanchor='false' to remove a default axis scaling constraint.

meta.symbol: scaleanchor now accepts 'false' to remove a constraint set by default

text: insiderange did not exist; grid lines and tick labels of counter axis could overlap when positioned inside: Stop manually positioning tick labels to avoid overlap; use insiderange on cartesian axes to prevent overlap between visible grid lines and tick labels of the counter axis when positioned inside.

meta.symbol: insiderange did not exist; grid lines and tick labels of counter axis could overlap when positioned inside

text: use_2to3 setuptools argument: Stop using the `use_2to3` argument in setuptools configuration as it has been removed.
Stop using distutils.Version and use packaging.Version instead.
Stop using ff.create_annotated_heatmap as it is deprecated.
Stop using ff.create_county_choropleth as it is deprecated.
Stop using ff.create_gantt as it is deprecated.
json keys were sorted alphabetically: Stop relying on JSON keys being sorted alphabetically; the serialization engines no longer sort keys.

arrays with dtype object are converted to list values when added to a figure: pandas/numpy arrays with dtype of Object are now converted to list values when added to a Figure (this was a regression in 5.0.0 that was fixed, but the fix changes behaviour from pre-5.0.0)

meta.symbol: arrays with dtype object are converted to list values when added to a figure

text: Stop using Python versions older than 3.6; they are no longer supported.
area traces: Stop using `area` traces; they have been removed.
scatter.(t|r) attributes: Stop using `scatter.t` and `scatter.r` attributes; they have been removed.
layout.(radial|angular)axis attributes: Stop using `layout.radialaxis` and `layout.angularaxis` attributes; they have been removed.
Modebar no longer shows hovermode or spikeline buttons by default; add them back with `fig.update_layout(modebar_add=["v1hovermode", "toggleSpikeLines"])`.
"aa" text only appears on legend items when `mode="text"`: The "Aa" text no longer appears on legend items unless `mode="text"` is set.

meta.symbol: "aa" text only appears on legend items when `mode="text"`

text: textposition default was something else: In `bar` traces, `textposition` now defaults to `"auto"` instead of its previous default.
heatmapgl traces: Stop using `heatmapgl` traces; they are deprecated.
pointcloud traces: Stop using `pointcloud` traces; they are deprecated.
transform attributes: Stop using all `transform` attributes; they are deprecated.
Stop using the `retrying` dependency; it has been replaced with `tenacity`.

category_orders always takes into account every value, even those absent from data: Plotly Express now always considers every value in `category_orders` for discrete mappings and facets, even values not present in the data.

meta.symbol: category_orders always takes into account every value, even those absent from data

text: numeric strings are auto-typed as categorical data in default templates: Axes now auto-type numeric strings as categorical data instead of linear in default templates, which may break code expecting numeric axis behavior.

meta.symbol: numeric strings are auto-typed as categorical data in default templates

text: autotypenumbers is set to strict in default templates: The default template now sets autotypenumbers to strict, so numeric strings will no longer be auto-converted to numbers on axes.

meta.symbol: autotypenumbers is set to strict in default templates

text: x and y parameters were ignored when an image trace was used (rgb data or binary_string=true): px.imshow now uses x and y parameters for Image traces (RGB/binary_string=True), but only accepts numerical values, rejecting date or string values that worked with Heatmap traces.

meta.symbol: x and y parameters were ignored when an image trace was used (rgb data or binary_string=true)

text: plotly express automatically converted datetime columns of input dataframes to utc: Plotly Express no longer converts datetime columns of input dataframes to UTC, so datetime values will remain in their original timezone.

meta.symbol: plotly express automatically converted datetime columns of input dataframes to utc

text: add_traces() now accepts int-like values (e.g. numpy ints) for rows/cols, not just Python int.
row and col parameters now accept int-like values (e.g. numpy ints), not strictly Python int.
In px.bar, when x or y is missing and the provided value is not continuous, the missing axis now defaults to a column of 1s named 'count' instead of per-trace integers starting at 0.

histfunc now defaults to 'sum' when both x and y are provided (or x,y,z for density_heatmap/density_contour): In px.histogram (and density_heatmap/density_contour), when both x and y (and z) are provided, histfunc now defaults to 'sum' instead of ignoring the provided data.

meta.symbol: histfunc now defaults to 'sum' when both x and y are provided (or x,y,z for density_heatmap/density_contour)

text: default to 'overlay' when x (or y in h orientation) is also mapped to color: violinmode, boxmode and stripmode now default to 'overlay' instead of 'group' when the categorical axis is also mapped to color.

meta.symbol: default to 'overlay' when x (or y in h orientation) is also mapped to color

text: data frame treated as 'wide' data and melted internally: When neither x nor y is provided but data_frame is, Plotly Express now treats the data frame as 'wide' data and melts it internally instead of producing an empty figure.

meta.symbol: data frame treated as 'wide' data and melted internally

text: inferred to be the index of data_frame if provided, otherwise a stable index of integers starting at 0: When x or y is missing, it is now inferred as the data_frame index (if data_frame provided) or a stable index starting at 0, instead of per-trace integers starting at 0.

meta.symbol: inferred to be the index of data_frame if provided, otherwise a stable index of integers starting at 0

text: Child graph objects like figure.layout.xaxis are no longer created eagerly during construction; they are created lazily on first property access.
Stop setting arbitrary keys on px.defaults; only documented keys are now accepted.
no longer require the patch argument: Stop passing the patch argument to update_annotations, update_shapes, and update_layout_images; it is no longer required.

meta.symbol: no longer require the patch argument

text: no default height (inherits default height of regular figures): plotly.express figures no longer default to 600px height; they now inherit the default figure height. To restore old behavior, set px.defaults.height=600 or pass height=600 to px functions.

meta.symbol: no default height (inherits default height of regular figures)

text: no longer default to paper coordinates; plotly.js determines default reference frame based on context: Stop relying on add_annotations/add_shapes/add_images defaulting to paper coordinates; the reference frame is now determined by plotly.js based on context.

meta.symbol: no longer default to paper coordinates; plotly.js determines default reference frame based on context

text: Stop using the misspelled `stepss` property name on `graph_objects.indicator.Gauge`; use `steps` instead.
Stop using plotly.plotly; use chart_studio.plotly instead.
Stop using plotly.api; use chart_studio.api instead.
Stop using plotly.dashboard_objs; use chart_studio.dashboard_objs instead.
Stop using plotly.grid_objs; use chart_studio.grid_objs instead.
Stop using plotly.presentation_objs; use chart_studio.presentation_objs instead.
Stop using plotly.widgets.GraphWidget; use plotly.graph_objects.FigureWidget instead.

Stop using the fileopt argument when calling chart_studio.plotly.plot; in-place modifications to previously published figures are no longer supported and a figure will always overwrite a figure with the same name.
'plotly' template is the default theme: All figures now use the 'plotly' template as the default theme instead of no template or a different default.
geographic shape files bundled in core plotly package: Stop using create_choropleth without installing the optional plotly-geo package, or install plotly-geo to get geographic shape files.

meta.symbol: geographic shape files bundled in core plotly package

text: create_choropleth and create_gantt now return Figure objects instead of dictionaries, so code expecting dict methods (e.g. .keys(), .items()) will break.

return a reference to the calling figure: Figure add trace methods (.add_trace, .add_traces, .add_scatter, etc.) now return the figure instead of the trace, so code that captures the return value expecting a trace will break.

meta.symbol: return a reference to the calling figure

text: not converted to utc: datetime objects that include timezones are no longer converted to UTC, so timezone-aware datetimes will be displayed as-is instead of being normalized to UTC.
When updating a tuple property (e.g. layout.annotations) with a longer list, extra elements are now appended instead of being truncated or causing an error.
previous default colorscale: The default colorscale for the plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates is now plasma instead of the previous default.
previous colorway order: The default colorway (sequence of colors for traces) for the plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates has been reordered.

reverted to previous value (not 'constant'): The layout.legend.itemsizing setting in built-in templates was reverted from 'constant' back to the previous default, so legend entries may appear smaller than in 3.8.0.
title as a string (e.g. layout.title = 'text'): Stop setting layout.title as a plain string; set layout.title.text instead, or pass a string which will be mapped to title.text.
Stop using titlefont, titlefontfamily, etc. as top-level properties; use title.font, title.font.family, etc. instead.
update() returned none or void: The update method on graph_objs now returns the updated object, enabling chaining; code that relied on update returning None may break if it checks the return value.
show_link defaulted to true: The 'send data to cloud' button is now hidden by default; set show_link=True explicitly if you need the link/button.

unknown config options produce a warning but are still passed to plotly.js: Passing unknown config keys now triggers a warning instead of being silently ignored; code that relied on silent dropping may see warnings.

meta.symbol: unknown config options produce a warning but are still passed to plotly.js

text: old plotly logo in 'produced with plotly' modebar button: The 'Produced with Plotly' modebar button now uses the new Plotly logo instead of the old one.

meta.symbol: old plotly logo in 'produced with plotly' modebar button

text: barpolar trace type: Stop using 'area' trace type; use 'barpolar' trace type instead, which replaces and augments area traces.
skip_invalid argument: Stop using `_raise` argument in Figure/FigureWidget constructors; use `skip_invalid` instead.
Stop using `get_ordered()` method on Figure/FigureWidget; use `to_ordered_dict()` instead.
invalid properties/values now always raise a valueerror with a description: Passing invalid properties or values to graph objects now raises a ValueError instead of being silently accepted.

meta.symbol: invalid properties/values now always raise a valueerror with a description

text: offline_mode parameter: Stop using the 'offline_mode' parameter in create_choropleth; it has been removed from the call signature.

persistent selection api for centroid points is automatically enabled: Persistent selection API for centroid points is now automatically enabled in create_choropleth, which may change existing plot interactivity.

meta.symbol: persistent selection api for centroid points is automatically enabled

text: fips values on hover are 0-padded to 5 digits: FIPS values displayed on hover are now 0-padded to ensure they are 5 digits, which may break code that parses or expects unpadded FIPS strings.

meta.symbol: fips values on hover are 0-padded to 5 digits

text: hover_info default was not 'none' for county lines data: The default hover_info for county lines data is now 'none', so county line hover info will no longer appear unless explicitly set.

meta.symbol: hover_info default was not 'none' for county lines data

text: returned something other than a plotly figure: Stop relying on create_gantt and create_dendrogram returning non-Plotly-figure types; they now return a Plotly figure.

meta.symbol: returned something other than a plotly figure

text: Stop calling offline.init_notebook_mode() before offline.iplot() if you were relying on it being mandatory; it is now optional.

plotly.offline.plot and plotly.offline.iplot now accept configuration options as arguments; existing code passing positional arguments may break if the new config options change argument order or expected types.
Network failures now always raise plotly.exceptions.PlotlyRequestException instead of possibly PlotlyError or requests.exceptions.RequestException.

meta.symbol: could be plotlyerror, plotlyrequestexception, or requests.exceptions.requestexception

text: plotly.py:sign_in now validates to the plotly server specified in your config and raises a PlotlyError if it cannot make a successful request.
plotly.figure_factory will raise an ImportError if numpy is not installed.
Stop using plotly.tools.FigureFactory; use plotly.figure_factory.* instead.
plotly.tools._*_imported private flags (e.g. _numpy_imported) have been removed.
plotly.py._api_v2 has been removed.
The plot-schema from https://api.plot.ly/plot-schema is no longer updated on import.
Grid column unique-identifiers uploaded via plotly.plotly.upload now use a different format, which may break existing plots that reference grid columns by their old identifier.

meta.symbol: old format of unique-identifiers in columns of grids uploaded via plotly.plotly.upload

text: default is now 'probability density': create_distplot now defaults to 'probability density' instead of 'probability' for the histnorm parameter.
colormap parameter: Stop using `palette` in `create_scatterplotmatrix`; use `colormap` instead.
use_palette parameter: Stop using `use_palette` in `create_scatterplotmatrix`; it has been removed.
color_func parameter: Stop using `dist_func` in `create_trisurf`; use `color_func` instead.
could call init_notebook_mode multiple times without consequence (1.9.13 behavior): You must call `init_notebook_mode` before making an `iplot` call; calling it multiple times is safe but required.

meta.symbol: could call init_notebook_mode multiple times without consequence (1.9.13 behavior)

text: offline mode deleted jupyter notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables: Offline mode no longer deletes Jupyter Notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables, so code relying on those globals being absent will break.

meta.symbol: offline mode deleted jupyter notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables

text: Offline plotly no longer requires a paid license; plotly.js is shipped inside the package for unlimited free use in IPython notebooks.

meta.symbol: offline plotly is now free and uses bundled plotly.js

text: Stop using `plotly.offline.download_plotlyjs`; it is deprecated and plotly.js is no longer downloaded independently from this package.

meta.symbol: download_plotlyjs function existed and was used to independently download plotly.js

text: streaming validation existed (is streamable checks): Stop relying on streaming validation; the 'is streamable' and streaming validation checks have been removed.
sharing keyword argument with options 'public' | 'private' | 'secret': Stop using world_readable; use sharing='public'|'private'|'secret' instead.
height and width parameters accepted directly in iplot(): Stop passing height and width as arguments to iplot; set them in the figure's layout dict instead.

meta.symbol: height and width parameters accepted directly in iplot()

text: silently ignoring error messages in the response from plot or iplot: If the response from plot or iplot contains an error message, an exception is now raised instead of silently continuing.

meta.symbol: silently ignoring error messages in the response from plot or iplot

text: class-scoped fixtures defined as instance methods (without @classmethod): Stop defining class-scoped fixtures as instance methods; use @classmethod decorator instead.

meta.symbol: class-scoped fixtures defined as instance methods (without @classmethod)

text: calling request.getfixturevalue() during teardown to request a fixture not already requested: Calling request.getfixturevalue() during teardown to request a fixture that was not already requested is now deprecated and will error in pytest 10.

meta.symbol: calling request.getfixturevalue() during teardown to request a fixture not already requested

text: Stop using the private config.inicfg attribute; use config.getini() to access configuration values instead.
deprecated since pytest 7.2, scheduled for removal in pytest 10: Configuring hooks using markers is deprecated and scheduled to be removed in pytest 10.

meta.symbol: deprecated since pytest 7.2, scheduled for removal in pytest 10

text: external plugin pytest-pastebin: Stop using the --pastebin option; use the external pytest-pastebin plugin instead.
The private FixtureDef.has_location attribute is deprecated and will be removed in pytest 10.
Stop using pytest.console_main(); use pytest.main() instead.
pytest.raises, pytest.warns and pytest.deprecated_call now use ParamSpec for the callable overload type hint instead of Any, and func can now be passed as a kwarg.
uses exception type's name instead of repr: DID NOT RAISE error messages now use the exception type's name instead of its repr.

meta.symbol: uses exception type's name instead of repr

text: unittest.skiptest was considered an interactive exception, triggering pytest_exception_interact hook: Stop relying on pytest_exception_interact being called for unittest.SkipTest; it will no longer be invoked.

meta.symbol: unittest.skiptest was considered an interactive exception, triggering pytest_exception_interact hook

text: an exception from pytest_fixture_post_finalizer could prevent fixtures from being torn down, causing additional errors in following tests: If you relied on an exception in pytest_fixture_post_finalizer to block subsequent fixture teardown, that behaviour is gone; fixture teardown will continue despite the exception.

meta.symbol: an exception from pytest_fixture_post_finalizer could prevent fixtures from being torn down, causing additional errors in following tests

text: --strict-markers and --strict-config specified through addopts were silently ignored (regression in pytest 9.0): --strict-markers and --strict-config specified through addopts are no longer silently ignored; they now take effect.

meta.symbol: --strict-markers and --strict-config specified through addopts were silently ignored (regression in pytest 9.0)

text: only logs which reached the root logger were captured: Logging capture (caplog, 'Captured log calls' reporting) now captures logs from non-propagating loggers, not just those reaching the root logger.

meta.symbol: only logs which reached the root logger were captured

text: tests attribute of <testsuite> could report incorrect number when test passes but fails during teardown: JUnit XML <testsuite> tests attribute now always matches the actual number of <testcase> elements, fixing a mismatch when a test passes but fails during teardown.

meta.symbol: tests attribute of <testsuite> could report incorrect number when test passes but fails during teardown

text: pytest_fixture_post_finalizer was called extra times for the same fixture teardown in some cases: pytest_fixture_post_finalizer hook is no longer called extra times for the same fixture teardown in some cases.

meta.symbol: pytest_fixture_post_finalizer was called extra times for the same fixture teardown in some cases

text: terminal progress feature enabled by default (pytest 9.0.0): The terminal progress feature added in pytest 9.0.0 is now disabled by default except on Windows; re-enable with -p terminalprogress.

meta.symbol: terminal progress feature enabled by default (pytest 9.0.0)

text: The private config.inicfg attribute was broken in pytest 9.0.0 and is now restored via a compatibility shim, but will be deprecated in 9.1 and removed in pytest 10.

meta.symbol: config.inicfg was changed in a breaking manner in pytest 9.0.0

text: toml integer log levels must be quoted: TOML integer log levels must now be quoted as strings in configuration.

meta.symbol: toml integer log levels must be quoted

text: tmp_path_retention_count toml type was number: The TOML type of tmp_path_retention_count setting changed from number to string.
verbosity settings toml type was number: The TOML type of verbosity settings changed from number to string.

meta.symbol: verbosity settings toml type was number

text: Stop using [tool.pytest.ini_options] and use [tool.pytest] instead for native TOML configuration; both tables cannot be used at the same time.
now also impacts test discovery when using --pyargs: Setting consider_namespace_packages=true now also affects test discovery via --pyargs, not just package imports.

meta.symbol: now also impacts test discovery when using --pyargs

text: faulthandler can now interrupt the pytest process after timeout when faulthandler_exit_on_timeout is true: faulthandler timeout now can interrupt the pytest process (exit) instead of only dumping tracebacks, controlled by faulthandler_exit_on_timeout config option (default false).

meta.symbol: faulthandler can now interrupt the pytest process after timeout when faulthandler_exit_on_timeout is true

text: When strict_parametrization_ids is set, pytest errors on non-unique parametrization IDs instead of silently appending 0, 1, ... to make them unique.

meta.symbol: pytest automatically made non-unique parametrization ids unique by appending 0, 1, ...

text: --strict now enables strict mode (which enables strict_config, strict_markers, strict_parametrization_ids, strict_xfail): The previously-deprecated --strict flag now enables strict mode, which activates multiple strictness options.

meta.symbol: --strict now enables strict mode (which enables strict_config, strict_markers, strict_parametrization_ids, strict_xfail)

text: `pytest a/ a/b` is equivalent to `pytest a/` — if an argument overlaps another, only the prefix remains: When passing overlapping directory arguments like `pytest a/ a/b`, only the prefix argument (`a/`) is used, and the overlapping subdirectory argument (`a/b`) is ignored.

meta.symbol: `pytest a/ a/b` is equivalent to `pytest a/` — if an argument overlaps another, only the prefix remains

text: Running pytest with the same file path twice (e.g. `pytest x.py x.py`) now runs the file once instead of twice.
python 3.9 was supported: Python 3.9 is no longer supported by pytest.

ci mode activated if $ci or $build_number was defined (even if empty): CI mode is now only activated when $CI or $BUILD_NUMBER is defined and set to a non-empty value; previously an empty string was enough.

meta.symbol: ci mode activated if $ci or $build_number was defined (even if empty)

text: The non-public `config.args` attribute now only contains strings; previously it could contain pathlib.Path instances.
PytestRemovedIn9Warning deprecation warnings are now errors by default instead of warnings, and the affected features will be removed in pytest 9.1.

meta.symbol: deprecation warnings are now errors by default

text: pytester in subprocess mode now raises an error for non-str pytester.plugins values instead of silently ignoring them; specify plugins by name or use runpytest_inprocess instead.

meta.symbol: pytester in subprocess mode silently ignored non-str pytester.plugins values

text: warn+skip: Async tests now fail instead of warning+skipping if no suitable async plugin is installed.
raise warning: Tests that return any value other than None now fail instead of just raising a warning.
Stop using pytest on Python 3.8; upgrade to Python 3.9 or later.
yield in test functions now raises an explicit error: Stop using yield in test functions; it now raises an explicit error instead of a deprecation warning.

meta.symbol: yield in test functions now raises an explicit error

text: Stop passing match="" to pytest.raises; use match="^$" to check an exception has no message, or pass a real pattern.

meta.symbol: passing an empty string to match in pytest.raises would silently match any exception message.

text: pythonpath now configures $PYTHONPATH earlier, which affects plugins loaded via the -p command-line option, potentially changing plugin behavior.

meta.symbol: pythonpath was configured later during initialization, after -p plugins were loaded.

text: fixtures are now clearly represented as a 'fixture object' in output: Fixtures are now displayed as 'fixture object' instead of as a normal function in pytest output, which may break code or tooling that parses test output.

meta.symbol: fixtures are now clearly represented as a 'fixture object' in output

text: Using pytest.mark.usefixtures with no arguments now emits a warning instead of being silently ignored.

--stepwise now persists the last failed test across runs even when pytest is executed without the flag in between; state auto-resets if the test suite changes: --stepwise now remembers the last failed test across sessions even when running without the flag in between, and auto-resets if the test suite changes.

meta.symbol: --stepwise now persists the last failed test across runs even when pytest is executed without the flag in between; state auto-resets if the test suite changes

text: using pytest.mark.usefixtures on pytest.param silently did nothing: Stop using pytest.mark.usefixtures on pytest.param; it now produces an error instead of silently doing nothing.

meta.symbol: using pytest.mark.usefixtures on pytest.param silently did nothing

text: The assertion rewrite warning message now uses ';' instead of ':' so it can be filtered with standard Python warning filters before calling pytest.main.
scientific notation (e.g., `42 ± 1.0e+00`): Stop parsing pytest.approx repr output in scientific notation for tolerances between 0.001 and 1000; it now uses decimal notation.

pytest.approx considered numpy boolean values (np.true_, np.false_) unequal to python booleans (true, false): pytest.approx now treats numpy boolean values (np.True_, np.False_) as equal to Python booleans (True, False), so comparisons that previously returned False now return True.

meta.symbol: pytest.approx considered numpy boolean values (np.true_, np.false_) unequal to python booleans (true, false)

text: pytest.approx used to treat booleans as numeric values (0/1) for approximate comparison: pytest.approx now uses strict equality (==) for boolean values instead of approximate numeric comparison.
