meta.symbol: pytest.approx used to treat booleans as numeric values (0/1) for approximate comparison

text: Stop relying on -rx or -ra to show xfail tracebacks; use --xfail-tb instead.

meta.symbol: in pytest 8.0, -rx or -ra would show tracebacks for xfail results.

text: fixturedef.cached_result[2] was just the exception object (exc): FixtureDef.cached_result[2] is now a tuple (exc, tb) instead of just exc; plugins accessing cached_result[2] must handle a tuple.

meta.symbol: fixturedef.cached_result[2] was just the exception object (exc)

text: setupstate.stack failures were just the exception object (exc): SetupState.stack failures are now a tuple (exc, tb) instead of just exc; plugins accessing stack failures must handle a tuple.

meta.symbol: setupstate.stack failures were just the exception object (exc)

text: parameters compared using `==` (with `is` fallback): Parametrization parameters are now compared using `==` instead of `is`; if a parameter does not support `==`, `is` is used as fallback.
trivial change log fragment type: Stop using the 'trivial' changelog fragment type; use 'contrib', 'misc', or 'packaging' instead.
log file mode was hard-coded to "w" (truncate): Stop relying on log files always being truncated; use --log-file-mode to control write vs append mode.

meta.symbol: log file mode was hard-coded to "w" (truncate)

text: all exceptions from multiple fixture finalizers are reported as an exception group: Stop catching only the first exception from fixture finalizers; all exceptions are now grouped and raised together.

meta.symbol: all exceptions from multiple fixture finalizers are reported as an exception group

text: _pytest.nodes.iterparentnodeids() function: Stop using _pytest.nodes.iterparentnodeids(); it has been removed. Traverse the node hierarchy directly instead.
fixturemanager._getautousenames() took a nodeid string: Stop passing a nodeid string to FixtureManager._getautousenames(); pass a Node object instead.
fixturemanager.getfixturedefs() took a nodeid string: Stop passing a nodeid string to FixtureManager.getfixturedefs(); pass a Node object instead.

session fixture teardowns executed once when max-fails reached (pytest 8.0.0 fix): The fix to --maxfail handling from pytest 8.0.0 was reverted in 8.0.2, so session fixture teardowns may execute multiple times when max-fails is reached.

meta.symbol: session fixture teardowns executed once when max-fails reached (pytest 8.0.0 fix)

text: PytestRemovedIn8Warning deprecation warnings now raise errors instead of warnings by default; use filterwarnings in pytest.ini to temporarily revert to warnings.
pluggy>=1.3.0 is now required; older pluggy versions will cause import errors.
files and directories collected in alphabetical order jointly: Files and directories are now collected in alphabetical order jointly, not files-before-directories; test ordering may change.

meta.symbol: files and directories collected in alphabetical order jointly

text: collects only the pkg/__init__.py module: Running `pytest pkg/__init__.py` now collects only that module, not the entire package; use `pytest pkg` to collect the whole package.
pytest.Package is no longer a subclass of pytest.Module or pytest.File; isinstance checks and type-based plugin code will break.

meta.symbol: pytest.package was a pytest.module or pytest.file

text: package only collects files in its own directory; sub-directories get their own collector nodes: Package no longer collects recursively; each sub-directory gets its own collector node forming a tree.

meta.symbol: package only collects files in its own directory; sub-directories get their own collector nodes

text: session only collects initial arguments; recursion is done by directory collector nodes: Session no longer recurses into directories; directory collector nodes handle recursion.

meta.symbol: session only collects initial arguments; recursion is done by directory collector nodes

text: session.name was the rootdir directory name: session.name is now an empty string instead of the rootdir directory name.

meta.symbol: session.name was the rootdir directory name

text: pytest.warns now re-emits unmatched warnings when the context closes; previously it consumed all warnings, hiding unmatched ones.
new-style hook wrappers (pluggy 1.2.0+): pytest now uses new-style hook wrappers internally; plugins that want to use new-style wrappers must require pytest>=8.
pytest.WarningsRecorder.pop() now returns the most-closely-matched warning instead of the first warning that is an instance of the requested type.

picks the scope of the highest-scope fixture for the parameter set: When multiple fixtures exist for an indirect parameter, the scope of the narrowest-scope fixture is now picked for the parameter set, instead of the highest-scope fixture.

meta.symbol: picks the scope of the highest-scope fixture for the parameter set

text: abstract class, cannot be instantiated directly: FixtureRequest is now an abstract class and cannot be instantiated directly; use TopRequest or SubRequest instead.

meta.symbol: abstract class, cannot be instantiated directly

text: pytester now uses monkeypatch to manage CWD, so calling monkeypatch.undo() may restore the CWD unexpectedly; use monkeypatch.context() instead.
Stop using ExceptionInfo.from_exc_info(); use ExceptionInfo.from_exception() instead.

only exception string and message shown: When all traceback entries are hidden, pytest no longer shows the last hidden frame; it now shows only the exception string and a message suggesting --full-trace.

meta.symbol: only exception string and message shown

text: conftest.py files probed all the way to filesystem root: When --confcutdir is not specified and no config file exists, the conftest cutoff directory is now set to the rootdir instead of probing all the way to the filesystem root.

meta.symbol: conftest.py files probed all the way to filesystem root

text: testpaths were considered for loading initial conftests even when not utilized (e.g. when explicit paths were given on the command line): testpaths are no longer used to load conftests when explicit paths are given on the command line

meta.symbol: testpaths were considered for loading initial conftests even when not utilized (e.g. when explicit paths were given on the command line)

text: testpaths was not honored to load root conftests: testpaths is now honored to load root conftests, which may cause conftests to be loaded from testpaths directories that were previously ignored

meta.symbol: testpaths was not honored to load root conftests

text: when multiple errors are raised in teardown, only the last error was raised (others discarded): Stop relying on only the last teardown error being raised; now an ExceptionGroup containing all errors is raised.

meta.symbol: when multiple errors are raised in teardown, only the last error was raised (others discarded)

text: pytest.PytestUnhandledCoroutineWarning is now a deprecation and will raise an error in pytest 8.
Stop configuring hook specs/impls using attributes/marks; use pytest.hookimpl and pytest.hookspec instead.
pytest supported running tests written for nose: Stop using plain setup/teardown functions and @with_setup decorator for nose compatibility; they are deprecated.

meta.symbol: pytest supported running tests written for nose

text: returning non-none from a test function emits a deprecation warning and will become an error: Stop returning non-None values from test functions; return None or use assert instead, as returning a value now emits a deprecation warning and will become an error.

meta.symbol: returning non-none from a test function emits a deprecation warning and will become an error

text: marks inherited from super-classes were ordered after marks from sub-classes; pytestmark attribute contained inherited marks; only first super-class marks applied in multiple inheritance: Marks from sub-classes are now ordered before marks from super-classes in MRO order; pytestmark attribute no longer contains inherited marks; marks are inherited according to full MRO

meta.symbol: marks inherited from super-classes were ordered after marks from sub-classes; pytestmark attribute contained inherited marks; only first super-class marks applied in multiple inheritance

text: pytest._fillfuncargs function: Stop using pytest._fillfuncargs; it has been removed with no replacement.

pytest.approx accepted unordered sequences like sets: pytest.approx now raises TypeError when given an unordered sequence such as a set, and custom classes implementing only __iter__ and __len__ are no longer supported.

meta.symbol: pytest.approx accepted unordered sequences like sets

text: Symbolic link components are no longer resolved in conftest paths, so a conftest appearing twice via symlinks will be executed twice.

meta.symbol: symbolic link components are no longer resolved in conftest paths

text: pytester created its own monkeypatch internally: pytester now requests a monkeypatch fixture instead of creating one internally, which may affect tests that rely on pytester's internal monkeypatch state.

meta.symbol: pytester created its own monkeypatch internally

text: unittestfunction.obj returned bound method: UnitTestFunction.obj now returns an unbound method instead of a bound method, which fixes a crash during failed teardown in unittest TestCases with non-default __init__.
py.path.local | str: Stop treating the first return value of reportinfo() as a py.path.local; call py.path.local(fspath) to convert, or use pathlib.Path / item.location / item.path instead.
Stop relying on --version output going to stderr; it now writes to stdout.
Stop using the pytest_cmdline_preparse hook; it has been removed.
Stop using pytest.approx in a boolean context (e.g. assert pytest.approx(a, b)); it now raises an error. Use assert actual == pytest.approx(expected) instead.
pytest.instance collector type: Stop using pytest.Instance; importing it returns a dummy type and emits a deprecation warning.
Stop ignoring PytestRemovedIn7Warning deprecation warnings; they are now errors by default and the affected features will be removed in pytest 7.1.
py.path.local arguments for hooks: Stop passing py.path.local arguments to hooks; use pathlib.Path or str instead.
py.path.local arguments to node constructors: Stop passing py.path.local to Node constructors; use the new 'path' attribute with pathlib.Path or str instead.

directly constructing _pytest.mark.structures.mark, markdecorator, markgenerator, _pytest.python.metafunc, _pytest.runner.callinfo, _pytest._code.exceptioninfo, _pytest.config.argparsing.parser, _pytest.config.argparsing.optiongroup, _pytest.pytester.hookrecorder: Stop directly constructing these private classes; they now issue a deprecation warning and may become a hard error in pytest 8.
raising unittest.skiptest during collection: Stop raising unittest.SkipTest during test collection; use pytest.skip() instead.
parser.addoption(..., help=".. %default .."): Stop using %default in parser.addoption help strings; use %(default)s instead.
parser.addoption(..., type="int/string/float/complex"): Stop passing type as a string like 'int' to parser.addoption; pass the actual Python type like int instead.

defining a custom node type that inherits from both item and collector (e.g. file): Stop defining custom pytest node types that are both Item and Collector; it was never sanely supported and now issues a warning.

meta.symbol: defining a custom node type that inherits from both item and collector (e.g. file)

text: Stop using pytest_cmdline_preparse hook; use pytest_load_initial_conftests instead.
Stop using msg= and use reason= instead when calling pytest.skip(), pytest.fail(), or pytest.exit().
Deprecation warnings scheduled for removal in a major version now use PytestRemovedInXWarning instead of PytestDeprecationWarning directly.
unittest.TestCase.setUpClass is now called correctly even when a test name contains '/', because the assumption changed from test names not containing '/' to test paths not containing '::'.
--stepwise-skip required --stepwise to be explicitly enabled: Using --stepwise-skip now implicitly enables --stepwise, so you no longer need to pass both flags.
pytest.cache.set did not preserve key order when saving dicts: pytest.Cache.set now preserves key order when saving dicts; code relying on unordered dicts may break if it assumed no ordering.

meta.symbol: pytest.cache.set did not preserve key order when saving dicts

text: pytest.mark.skip silently ignored positional reason argument when keyword reason was also given: pytest.mark.skip now raises TypeError if reason is given both positional and as keyword; previously the test was silently skipped.

meta.symbol: pytest.mark.skip silently ignored positional reason argument when keyword reason was also given

text: pytester.makefile accepted ext values without leading dot: pytester.makefile now errors if the ext argument is missing the leading dot; previously it silently accepted it.

meta.symbol: pytester.makefile accepted ext values without leading dot

text: Stop relying on nose-compatible setup()/teardown() being called per test function; they are now called only once per module.
Stop using py.path.local with _pytest.code.getfslineno(); it now returns Path.
Stop passing py.path.local to _pytest.python.path_matches_patterns(); it now expects Path.
py.path.local only: Stop passing only py.path.local to Traceback.cut(); it now accepts any os.PathLike[str], which may change type inference or overload resolution.

approx treated array-like objects differently (not converting to numpy.ndarray): approx now converts array-protocol-exposing non-scalar arguments to numpy.ndarray before comparison, changing how array-like objects are compared.

meta.symbol: approx treated array-like objects differently (not converting to numpy.ndarray)

text: pytest now requires Python 3.6 or higher; older Python versions are no longer supported.
Stop using --strict; use --strict-markers instead.
Stop using @pytest.yield_fixture; use @pytest.fixture instead.
MonkeyPatch.context() is now a classmethod and must be called on the class, not an instance.
pytest.approx no longer raises TypeError for non-numeric types; it now uses strict equality comparison instead.
funcargnames read-only property on fixturerequest, metafunc, function: Stop using the funcargnames property on FixtureRequest, Metafunc, and Function classes; use fixturenames instead.

meta.symbol: funcargnames read-only property on fixturerequest, metafunc, function

text: @pytest.fixture no longer supports positional arguments; pass all arguments by keyword instead.
Direct construction of Node subclasses now raises an error; use from_parent instead.
The TerminalReporter no longer has a writer attribute; use the public functions of TerminalReporter instead of accessing TerminalWriter directly.
Stop using --result-log; use the pytest-reportlog plugin instead.
Stop using pytest._fillfuncargs; use function._request._fillfixtures() instead, though this is not a public API.
Stop using -k '-expr' syntax; use -k 'not expr' instead.
pytestdeprecationwarning was a warning (non-fatal): PytestDeprecationWarning is now raised as an error by default instead of a warning.
The result type of capfd.readouterr() (and similar) is no longer a namedtuple, so isinstance(..., tuple) checks will fail.
The internal junitxml plugin was rewritten to use xml.etree.ElementTree; the order of attributes in XML elements might differ and some unneeded escaping is no longer performed.
pytestdeprecationwarning now generates errors instead of warning messages: Stop relying on PytestDeprecationWarning being only a warning; it now raises an error by default.

meta.symbol: pytestdeprecationwarning now generates errors instead of warning messages

text: symlinks were resolved during collection and conftest.py matching (introduced in 3.9.0): Stop relying on symlinks being resolved during test collection; symlinks are no longer followed.

meta.symbol: symlinks were resolved during collection and conftest.py matching (introduced in 3.9.0)

text: -k no longer matches against names of directories outside the test session root: -k no longer matches against directory names outside the test session root; tests previously selected by directory name may no longer match.

meta.symbol: -k no longer matches against names of directories outside the test session root

text: pytest.Package.name is now just the directory name, not the full path; code relying on the full path will break.

meta.symbol: returns just the name of the directory containing __init__.py

text: evaluated using python's eval(): -m and -k expressions are no longer evaluated with Python's eval(); Python constants, keywords or other operators no longer work.
had explicit windows console support: Explicit Windows console support was removed from TerminalWriter; colorama library handles it now.
supported writing bytes: TerminalWriter no longer supports writing bytes; only strings are accepted.
had reline method and chars_on_current_line property: The reline method and chars_on_current_line property were removed from TerminalWriter.

meta.symbol: had reline method and chars_on_current_line property

text: accepted stringio and encoding arguments: The stringio and encoding arguments were removed from TerminalWriter.
accepted a callable instead of a file: TerminalWriter no longer accepts a callable instead of a file; only file objects are supported.

meta.symbol: accepted a callable instead of a file

text: item.catch_log_handler and item.catch_log_handlers attributes are no longer available.
were available: The --no-print-logs option and log_print ini option are removed; use --show-capture instead.
The unused args parameter was removed from pytest.Function.__init__; passing it will raise an error.
was a hook specification: The pytest_doctest_prepare_content hook specification has been removed; it hasn't been triggered for at least 10 years.

pytest --version now displays just the pytest version; use pytest --version --version for verbose info including plugins: pytest --version now shows only the version number; use --version --version to see plugin details.

meta.symbol: pytest --version now displays just the pytest version; use pytest --version --version for verbose info including plugins

text: logging format errors (e.g. missing arguments) were silently printed to stderr and hidden for passing tests: Logging calls with format errors (e.g. missing arguments) now raise an exception and fail the test instead of being silently hidden.

meta.symbol: logging format errors (e.g. missing arguments) were silently printed to stderr and hidden for passing tests

text: --basetemp rejects empty string, cwd, and parent directories with an error: Passing an empty string, the current working directory, or a parent directory to --basetemp now causes pytest to exit with an error.

meta.symbol: --basetemp rejects empty string, cwd, and parent directories with an error

text: callinfo.duration was based on time.time() (stop - start): Test duration measurements are now based on time.perf_counter() instead of time.time(), making them more precise but potentially different from previous values.

meta.symbol: callinfo.duration was based on time.time() (stop - start)

text: caplog.set_level() did not override cli/config log_level: caplog.set_level() now overrides any log_level set via CLI or configuration file, so existing code that relied on CLI/config log_level being unaffected by caplog.set_level() will now see the override take effect.

meta.symbol: caplog.set_level() did not override cli/config log_level

text: Stop relying on pytest.mark.skipif or pytest.mark.xfail evaluating string expressions incorrectly when the same string refers to different global values; evaluation is now correct.

+ meant something expected is missing, - meant unexpected extras: The meaning of +/- in error diffs has been reversed: '-' now means something expected is missing and '+' means there are unexpected extras.

meta.symbol: + meant something expected is missing, - meant unexpected extras

text: default was -rn (no summary): The default for -r changed from -rN (no summary) to -r fE, which displays failures and errors in the short test summary.

match keyword argument was swallowed and ignored when pytest.raises was used as a function (not context manager): When using pytest.raises as a function, the match keyword argument is now passed to the tested function instead of being ignored.

meta.symbol: match keyword argument was swallowed and ignored when pytest.raises was used as a function (not context manager)

text: assertion rewriting hooks persisted after test item finished (e.g. after pytester's testdir.runpytest): Assertion rewriting hooks are now scoped to the current item and restored afterwards, which may break code that relied on hooks persisting after test execution.

meta.symbol: assertion rewriting hooks persisted after test item finished (e.g. after pytester's testdir.runpytest)

text: pytest.exit was not handled when emitted from pytest_sessionfinish hook: pytest.exit is now handled when emitted from the pytest_sessionfinish hook, which may change behaviour for code that previously relied on it being ignored at that point.

meta.symbol: pytest.exit was not handled when emitted from pytest_sessionfinish hook

text: fixturedef objects now properly register their finalizers with autouse and parameterized fixtures that execute before them: FixtureDef finalizers are now properly registered with autouse and parameterized fixtures that execute before them, changing teardown order and timing.

meta.symbol: fixturedef objects now properly register their finalizers with autouse and parameterized fixtures that execute before them

text: --report-log option (via pytest-reportlog plugin): Stop using --resultlog; it is deprecated and will be removed. Use the --report-log option from the pytest-reportlog plugin instead.
The default for log auto-indentation changed from "on" to "off", restoring the pre-v4.6.0 behavior.
list (or mutable sequence): Config.InvocationParams.args is now always a tuple instead of a mutable sequence; code that modifies it in-place will break.
pytest.main now returns a pytest.ExitCode instance instead of a plain int (except for custom exit codes where it still returns int).

reported as failures: Collection errors are now reported as errors (not failures) in the terminal's short test summary; code or CI that counts failures vs errors separately will see a different count.

uses __name__ attribute of any object for the id: Parametrization now uses the __name__ attribute of any object for the id, not just functions and classes; objects with __name__ will get different test IDs than before.

meta.symbol: uses __name__ attribute of any object for the id

text: Stop using Request.getfuncargvalue() and use Request.getfixturevalue() instead.
Stop passing a string as the second argument to pytest.raises() or pytest.warns(); pass a callable or context instead.

meta.symbol: passing a string as the second argument to pytest.raises() or pytest.warns()

text: Stop using the message parameter of pytest.raises(); it has been removed.
pytest.raises(), pytest.warns(), and ParameterSet.param() now use native keyword-only syntax; passing keyword arguments positionally will raise TypeError.

meta.symbol: native keyword-only syntax for pytest.raises(), pytest.warns(), and parameterset.param()

text: Stop using the pytest.config global variable; it has been removed.
Stop using tmpdir_factory.ensuretemp() method; it has been removed.
The scope parameter of @pytest.fixture can now be a callable receiving fixture name and config as keyword-only parameters, changing the accepted types.

failures to connect to the pastebin server are now reported without failing the pytest run: The --pastebin option now reports connection failures to the pastebin server instead of silently ignoring them.

meta.symbol: failures to connect to the pastebin server are now reported without failing the pytest run

text: fixture scope was not respected when indirect fixtures were used during parametrization: Fixture scope is now properly respected when indirect fixtures are used during parametrization; previously scope was ignored.

meta.symbol: fixture scope was not respected when indirect fixtures were used during parametrization

text: Stop passing arguments to pytest.fixture() as positional arguments; pass them as keyword arguments instead.

meta.symbol: passing arguments to pytest.fixture() as positional arguments

text: unittest2 backport support code in pytest: Stop relying on pytest's internal support code for unittest2; it has been removed.

meta.symbol: unittest2 backport support code in pytest

text: pytest accepted unambiguous prefixes of command-line arguments (e.g. --doctest-mod for --doctest-modules): Stop using shortened prefixes of command-line arguments; you must type the full option name (e.g. --doctest-modules instead of --doctest-mod).

meta.symbol: pytest accepted unambiguous prefixes of command-line arguments (e.g. --doctest-mod for --doctest-modules)

text: PytestDeprecationWarning now raises an error instead of a warning; add 'ignore::pytest.PytestDeprecationWarning' to filterwarnings in pytest.ini as a temporary stopgap.

exceptioninfo.__str__ gave a different (shorter) representation than __repr__: ExceptionInfo objects from pytest.raises now have the same str representation as repr; code that asserted on str(e) may break if it relied on the old shorter format.

meta.symbol: exceptioninfo.__str__ gave a different (shorter) representation than __repr__

text: --result-log option and module: Stop using --result-log; it has been removed (postponed to pytest 6.0 but now gone).
Stop using funcargnames; use fixturenames instead.
pytest.exitcode (intenum): Session.exitcode is now a pytest.ExitCode IntEnum instead of a plain int; compare using enum members or int values.

-q again has an impact on the style of the collected items (--collect-only) when --log-cli-level is used: The -q flag now affects the style of collected items during --collect-only when --log-cli-level is also used, reversing a previous regression where it had no effect.

meta.symbol: -q again has an impact on the style of the collected items (--collect-only) when --log-cli-level is used

text: upper-case variants (e.g. -rff) were accepted and produced summary entries: Using upper-case report chars like 'F' or 'S' in the -r option (e.g. -rFf) now causes duplicate summary entries; use lower-case variants instead.

meta.symbol: upper-case variants (e.g. -rff) were accepted and produced summary entries

text: pluggy is pinned to < 1.0 and will not automatically update to pluggy 1.0, which contains planned breaking changes.
-qq was being considered as -v (verbose): The -qq option was previously being interpreted as -v (verbose); now it correctly applies very quiet mode.

default internal plugins (e.g. capture) were always loaded even with -p no:plugin: -p no:plugin now correctly prevents default internal plugins from being loaded; previously they were always imported, e.g. making capfd fixture available even when blocked.

meta.symbol: default internal plugins (e.g. capture) were always loaded even with -p no:plugin

text: Collector.repr_failure now defaults to 'short' traceback style instead of 'long' when --tb=auto
Some verbosity related attributes of the TerminalReporter plugin are now read only properties instead of mutable attributes.
xfail (lowercase): The verbose word for expected failures in the teststatus report changes from 'xfail' to 'XFAIL'.
errors raised by id functions were suppressed and turned into warnings: Errors raised by id functions in parametrize are no longer suppressed; they now propagate as exceptions.

meta.symbol: errors raised by id functions were suppressed and turned into warnings

text: Stop using config.warn and Node.warn; they have been removed.
yield-based test functions: Yield tests are no longer supported; convert them to regular test functions with fixtures.
Stop applying marks directly to values in parametrize; use pytest.param(value, marks=...) instead.

meta.symbol: applying marks directly to values in @pytest.mark.parametrize

text: Stop using Metafunc.addcall; use @pytest.mark.parametrize instead.
pytest.main('string'): Stop passing a string to pytest.main; always pass a list of strings.
Stop using [pytest] in setup.cfg; use [tool:pytest] instead.
Stop using node.Class, node.Function, node.Module; use pytest.Class, pytest.Function, pytest.Module instead.
Stop implementing the pytest_namespace hook; it has been removed.
Stop using request.cached_setup; use modern fixtures instead.
pycollector.makeitem method: Stop using PyCollector.makeitem; it has been removed.
Stop defining fixtures with pytest_funcarg__ prefix; use @pytest.fixture decorator instead.
calling fixtures directly produced a warning: Calling a fixture function directly is now an error, not a warning.

meta.symbol: calling fixtures directly produced a warning

text: Stop using Node.get_marker(name); use Node.get_closest_marker(name) instead.
Stop using the pytest.config global; it is deprecated and will be removed.
raises(..., 'code(as_a_string)') and warns(..., 'code(as_a_string)') were accepted: Stop passing code as a string to pytest.raises() and pytest.warns(); this form is deprecated.
removedinpytest4warnings are now errors by default: RemovedInPytest4Warnings are now raised as errors instead of warnings, which will break code that relied on them being non-fatal.

meta.symbol: removedinpytest4warnings are now errors by default

text: raises(..., match=<non-none falsey value>) did not raise typeerror: Using a non-None falsey value (like empty string, 0, False) as the match parameter in raises() now raises TypeError instead of silently accepting it.

meta.symbol: raises(..., match=<non-none falsey value>) did not raise typeerror

text: -ra now shows errors and failures last in the summary: The -ra flag now displays errors and failures at the end of the summary instead of at the beginning.

meta.symbol: -ra now shows errors and failures last in the summary

text: the 'collecting …' message is only printed/updated every 0.5s: Stop expecting the 'collecting …' message to appear on every collection event; it now throttles to once per 0.5 seconds.

meta.symbol: the 'collecting …' message is only printed/updated every 0.5s

text: Unregistered marks now raise Failed exceptions instead of ValueError.

deprecation warnings are shown even if you customize warnings filters: Deprecation warnings are now shown even if you customize the warnings filters yourself; custom filters no longer override pytest's deprecation warnings.

meta.symbol: deprecation warnings are shown even if you customize warnings filters

text: always a list of (name, value) tuples: Stop relying on .user_properties being a tuple; it is now always a list.

meta.symbol: always a list of (name, value) tuples

text: not regex-escaped (actual regex): Stop expecting the match string in @pytest.mark.filterwarnings to be regex-escaped; it is now treated as a raw regular expression.
Stop using Config.warn() and Node.warn(); they are deprecated.
Warnings are now captured and displayed during test collection, which may cause new warning output during the collection phase.

meta.symbol: warnings are now captured and displayed during test collection

text: calling a fixture function directly (e.g. @pytest.fixture def myfix(): ...; myfix()): Stop calling fixture functions directly; doing so now emits a RemovedInPytest4Warning and will be removed in pytest 4.

meta.symbol: calling a fixture function directly (e.g. @pytest.fixture def myfix(): ...; myfix())

text: continues calling finalizers in the stack even when a finalizer in a former scope raises an exception: When a finalizer raises an exception, finalizers in remaining scopes are still called instead of stopping.

meta.symbol: continues calling finalizers in the stack even when a finalizer in a former scope raises an exception

text: keyboardinterrupt enters the debugger instead of stopping the test session: With --pdb, KeyboardInterrupt now enters the debugger instead of stopping the test session.

meta.symbol: keyboardinterrupt enters the debugger instead of stopping the test session

text: pytest no longer changes the log level of the root logger when the log-level parameter has greater numeric value than that of the level of the root logger: Stop relying on pytest overriding the root logger's level when log-level is set to a higher numeric level than the root logger's current level; pytest now leaves the root logger level unchanged.

meta.symbol: pytest no longer changes the log level of the root logger when the log-level parameter has greater numeric value than that of the level of the root logger

text: Stop using record_xml_property fixture; use record_property fixture instead.
Stop defining pytest_plugins in non-top-level conftest.py files; it is now deprecated and leaks plugins to the entire directory tree.

meta.symbol: defining pytest_plugins in non-top-level conftest.py files

text: Stop using record_xml_property; use record_property instead, which is compatible with xdist, markers and any reporter.

dontreadfromstdin was iterable and resolved to an iterator without crashing even when stdin is not allowed to be read: During test collection, when stdin is not allowed to be read, the DontReadFromStdin object now allows itself to be iterable and resolved to an iterator without crashing.

meta.symbol: dontreadfromstdin was iterable and resolved to an iterator without crashing even when stdin is not allowed to be read

text: new logging behavior (see logging docs): The logging functionality has undergone incompatible changes; consult the logging documentation for details.
Stop passing multiple overrides in a single -o flag; use separate -o flags for each override (e.g. pytest -o foo=1 -o bar=2).
Parametrized automatic test IDs now use the function's __name__ attribute instead of the fallback argument name plus counter.
Replace py.std with standard library imports.
Stop using pytest on Python 2.6 or 3.3; those Python versions are no longer supported.
Stop using ParameterSet.deprecated_arg_dict; it has been removed.

params list could be mutated after pytest.fixture() call before first fixture invocation: Stop mutating the params list passed to pytest.fixture after the decorator call; it is now frozen immediately and changes will be silently ignored.

meta.symbol: params list could be mutated after pytest.fixture() call before first fixture invocation

text: exit code 2 (interrupted): When --maxfail is reached, pytest now exits with code 1 (failed) instead of code 2 (interrupted).
whitespace is stripped from marker names when reading from ini config: Marker names in INI config now have whitespace stripped from them, so markers with intentional whitespace will break.

meta.symbol: whitespace is stripped from marker names when reading from ini config

text: fixture paths matched against folders sharing a prefix: Fixture paths now match against actual path segments instead of prefix matching, so fixtures that previously matched by prefix will no longer match.

meta.symbol: fixture paths matched against folders sharing a prefix

text: pytest.approx supported >, >=, <, <= operators: Stop using >, >=, <, <= with pytest.approx; they no longer work.
Stop passing marks directly as parameters in pytest.mark.parametrize; use pytest.param(...) to apply marks to parameters instead.

meta.symbol: passing marks directly as parameters in pytest.mark.parametrize

text: pytest no longer chains the context of `raise exception from none` in test reports: Stop relying on pytest chaining exception context from `raise exception from None`; it now matches Python's traceback behavior and suppresses the context.

meta.symbol: pytest no longer chains the context of `raise exception from none` in test reports

text: pytest warning capture no longer overrides existing warning filters; deprecationwarning and pendingdeprecationwarning are no longer shown by default: Stop relying on pytest showing DeprecationWarning and PendingDeprecationWarning by default, as they are no longer displayed unless you configure warning filters yourself.

meta.symbol: pytest warning capture no longer overrides existing warning filters; deprecationwarning and pendingdeprecationwarning are no longer shown by default

text: pytest.warns checked for class equality when matching warnings: pytest.warns now matches subclasses of the expected warning type, not just exact class equality, so tests that relied on exact class matching may now pass unexpectedly or catch broader warnings.

meta.symbol: pytest.warns checked for class equality when matching warnings

text: pytest.raises did not assert error message matching: pytest.raises now requires the match keyword argument to assert error message content; existing code using pytest.raises without match may silently pass when it should fail.

meta.symbol: pytest.raises did not assert error message matching

text: --pdbcls no longer implies --pdb; you must pass --pdb explicitly if you want to drop into debugger on failure.

common items removed from dict comparison output when verbosity=1: At verbosity=1, common items are now removed from dict comparison output, and all assertion messages are truncated unless verbosity >= 2.

meta.symbol: common items removed from dict comparison output when verbosity=1

text: RecordedWarning is now a namedtuple (immutable); existing code that mutates RecordedWarning instances will raise an error.
hook now accepts argname as additional parameter: pytest_make_parametrize_id hook now receives an additional argname parameter; custom implementations with the old signature will fail.

meta.symbol: hook now accepts argname as additional parameter

text: pytest_namespace hooks used internally: All internal uses of pytest_namespace hooks have been removed, preparing for removal in pytest 4.0; plugins relying on this hook may break.
coroutine functions no longer recognized as yield tests: pytest no longer recognizes coroutine functions as yield tests; async functions will not be treated as generator/yield tests.

meta.symbol: coroutine functions no longer recognized as yield tests

text: sys.exc_info() contained the exception caught by the context manager even when the expected exception occurred: After using pytest.raises in context-manager form, sys.exc_info() is now empty instead of containing the caught exception

meta.symbol: sys.exc_info() contained the exception caught by the context manager even when the expected exception occurred

text: teardown output was not reported on test failure: Teardown output is now reported when a test fails, which may surface new teardown failures or change existing test output

meta.symbol: teardown output was not reported on test failure

text: unittest.TestCase objects are now cleaned up after tests complete, which may break code relying on their persistence

when pytest_plugins was defined as a string (not a sequence), many more modules were being rewritten than necessary: pytest_plugins defined as a string now correctly limits assertion rewriting, so fewer modules are rewritten than before

meta.symbol: when pytest_plugins was defined as a string (not a sequence), many more modules were being rewritten than necessary

text: teardown and cleanups were called when running tests from unittest.testcase subclasses with --pdb enabled: When using --pdb with unittest.TestCase subclasses, tearDown and cleanups are no longer called, allowing post-mortem debugging but breaking code that relied on teardown running during debugging

meta.symbol: teardown and cleanups were called when running tests from unittest.testcase subclasses with --pdb enabled

text: --assert=plain (for --no-assert and --nomagic), -r (for --report): Stop using --genscript (removed), --no-assert (use --assert=plain), --nomagic (use --assert=plain), and --report (use -r).
importerror in plugins issued a warning: ImportErrors in plugins now cause a fatal error instead of just issuing a pytest warning.

meta.symbol: importerror in plugins issued a warning

text: python 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 supported: Stop using pytest on Python 3 versions older than 3.3; support for Python 3 < 3.3 has been removed.
py.test-x* (versioned suffixed entry points): Stop using py.test-X* versioned entry points; they have been removed.

tests continued after collection errors: pytest now exits immediately if a collection error occurs; use --continue-on-collection-errors to restore the old behaviour of continuing despite collection errors.
pytest pdb module (plugin): The pytest pdb plugin has been renamed to 'debugging' to avoid clashes with the builtin pdb module.

previously, requesting a parametrized fixture at runtime with request.getfixturevalue silently ran only once (params were never defined): Calling request.getfixturevalue on a parametrized fixture now raises an error instead of silently running only once.

meta.symbol: previously, requesting a parametrized fixture at runtime with request.getfixturevalue silently ran only once (params were never defined)

text: Stop using [pytest] in setup.cfg; use [tool:pytest] instead.
Stop writing yield-based tests; they are deprecated and will be removed in pytest-4.0.
Stop using pytest_funcarg__ prefix to declare fixtures; it is deprecated and will be removed in pytest-4.0.
Stop passing a string to pytest.main(); pass a list of arguments instead.

meta.symbol: passing a command-line string to pytest.main()

text: Stop using getfuncargvalue; use getfixturevalue instead.
warnings summary not shown by default: pytest now shows the warnings summary by default; use --disable-pytest-warnings to hide it.

meta.symbol: warnings summary not shown by default

text: sub-expressions that were false were hidden as redundant: Assertion rewriting now always includes full explanation; sub-expressions that are False are no longer hidden.

meta.symbol: sub-expressions that were false were hidden as redundant

text: duplicate option names silently accepted (caught later by argparse): OptionGroup.addoption() now raises an error if option names were already added, instead of failing later from argparse.

meta.symbol: duplicate option names silently accepted (caught later by argparse)

text: setup_method/setup_module etc. required a parameter: The parameter to xunit-style setup/teardown methods (setup_method, setup_module, etc.) is now optional and may be omitted.
optparse type usage triggers deprecationwarnings: Using optparse type usage now triggers DeprecationWarnings.

assertion failure output always includes full assertion explanation including sub-expressions that are false: Assertion failure output now always includes full explanation with all sub-expressions, even those that evaluate to False.

meta.symbol: assertion failure output always includes full assertion explanation including sub-expressions that are false

text: pytest.exit(msg) during startup never showed the message: pytest.exit(msg) during startup now writes the message to stderr; previously the message was never shown.

meta.symbol: pytest.exit(msg) during startup never showed the message

text: The incorrect test deselection reason is no longer displayed.

meta.symbol: incorrect test deselection reason is no longer displayed

text: deprecated; use modern alternatives: The --resultlog command line option is deprecated and should no longer be used.
fixture lookup error message had no 'e' prefix on first line and no '>' prefix on subsequent lines: Fixture lookup error messages now have an 'E' prefix on the first line and '>' prefix on the rest.

meta.symbol: fixture lookup error message had no 'e' prefix on first line and no '>' prefix on subsequent lines

text: Text documents without any doctests no longer appear as 'skipped' in test output.

meta.symbol: text documents without any doctests no longer appear as 'skipped'

text: Rootdir determination now considers only valid paths, which may change which directory is detected as the root.

meta.symbol: rootdir determination now considers only valid paths

text: Stop using pytest on Python 2.5 or lower; the oldinterpret module has been removed.

meta.symbol: support for python 2.5 and lower, including _pytest.assertion.oldinterpret module

text: comparisons always show in full when ci or build_number env vars are set, even without -vv: Comparisons now always display in full when CI or BUILD_NUMBER environment variable is set, regardless of -vv flag.

meta.symbol: comparisons always show in full when ci or build_number env vars are set, even without -vv

text: collection progress ('collecting x items') always displayed: Collection progress message 'collecting X items' now only shows when running in a terminal, not when output is piped or in CI.

meta.symbol: collection progress ('collecting x items') always displayed

text: accepted any warning (pre-2.8.0) or only deprecationwarning (2.8.0): deprecated_call now only passes for DeprecationWarning or PendingDeprecationWarning; other warning types will cause the assertion to fail.

meta.symbol: accepted any warning (pre-2.8.0) or only deprecationwarning (2.8.0)

text: sets were serialized when persisting data between test runs: pytest-cache no longer serializes sets; code relying on set persistence between test runs will break.

meta.symbol: sets were serialized when persisting data between test runs

text: Stop using --genscript; it is deprecated and will warn.
Stop using config._tmpdirhandler; use the tmpdir_factory fixture instead.
exit code 0 when no tests run: pytest now exits with status code 5 when no tests are run at all, instead of 0.

meta.symbol: exit code 0 when no tests run

text: pytest no longer loads conftest files from setup.cfg/pytest.ini/tox.ini directories and upwards by default; use --confcutdir to override.

meta.symbol: conftest files loaded from setup.cfg/pytest.ini/tox.ini directories and upwards by default

text: The pluginmanager.add_shutdown() API may break for 3rd party plugins that relied on it due to internal refactoring of the pluginmanager API.
# doctest: skip registered doctests as passed: Using '# doctest: SKIP' now registers doctests as SKIPPED instead of PASSED.

meta.symbol: # doctest: skip registered doctests as passed

text: applying markers in classes propagated those markers to superclasses that also had markers: Applying markers in classes no longer propagates those markers to superclasses which also have markers.

meta.symbol: applying markers in classes propagated those markers to superclasses that also had markers

text: tmpdir fixture creates directories in $temp/pytest-$user subdirectory: The tmpdir fixture now creates directories in a subdirectory ($TEMP/pytest-$USER) instead of directly in $TEMP.

meta.symbol: tmpdir fixture creates directories in $temp/pytest-$user subdirectory

text: diverted tests (tests collected in one file but coming from another, e.g. base class in different file) were reported in some unspecified way: Diverted test reporting now shows nodeid with a postfix indicating the source file, which may break scripts parsing test output

meta.symbol: diverted tests (tests collected in one file but coming from another, e.g. base class in different file) were reported in some unspecified way

text: old style '-' for negation at beginning of strings: The '-' prefix for negation in -k expressions is deprecated; use 'not' instead.

meta.symbol: old style '-' for negation at beginning of strings

text: no longer shows line numbers in --verbose output, only nodeid: Stop relying on line numbers appearing in --verbose output; the output now shows only the nodeid.

meta.symbol: no longer shows line numbers in --verbose output, only nodeid

text: default --tb=auto shows only first and last entry as long, others short: The default traceback display changed to --tb=auto which only shows long tracebacks for the first and last entry; use --tb=long to restore old behaviour.

meta.symbol: default --tb=auto shows only first and last entry as long, others short

text: -v output includes full node ids of tests: The -v output now includes full node IDs of tests, which users can copy and use as positional arguments to run a single test.

meta.symbol: -v output includes full node ids of tests

text: User-provided assertion messages no longer replace py.test introspection messages; both are now shown.

meta.symbol: user-provided assertion messages replaced py.test introspection messages

text: xpass (unexpected pass) shown in red when -v is used: Stop relying on XPASS being red in verbose output; it is now yellow.

meta.symbol: xpass (unexpected pass) shown in red when -v is used

text: test classes with __init__ and callables that look like tests but are not functions were silently skipped: Stop expecting test classes with __init__ and non-function callables in test modules to be silently skipped; they now generate warnings.

meta.symbol: test classes with __init__ and callables that look like tests but are not functions were silently skipped

text: using 'py.test' as the command name: Stop using 'py.test' as the command; use 'pytest' instead.
capfd.capture() and capsys.capture() being public: Stop using capfd.capture() and capsys.capture() as they are now private and should not be called externally.

finalizers ran in some order (not lifo) on independent fixtures depending on a parametrized higher-than-function scoped fixture: Finalizers for independent fixtures depending on a parametrized higher-than-function scoped fixture now run in LIFO order instead of the previous order.

meta.symbol: finalizers ran in some order (not lifo) on independent fixtures depending on a parametrized higher-than-function scoped fixture

text: special index for parameters to only use indices for parametrized test ids: Parametrized test IDs now use a special index that only uses indices for parametrized test ids, changing how test IDs are generated.

meta.symbol: special index for parameters to only use indices for parametrized test ids

text: all finalizers run but the first failing exception is saved and re-raised: All finalizers now run, but the exception from the first failing finalizer is saved and re-raised, changing which exception propagates during teardown.

meta.symbol: all finalizers run but the first failing exception is saved and re-raised

text: The -k flag now allows filtering with simple strings that are not valid Python expressions (e.g. '-k 1.3' or '-k None'), which previously raised syntax errors.
pytest had built-in trial support code: The trial support code has been removed from pytest; trial now handles it on its own via unittest compat enhancements.

meta.symbol: pytest had built-in trial support code

text: importerror when importing a plugin was hidden/silenced: An ImportError raised when importing a plugin is now propagated instead of being hidden, which may break code that relied on the old silent behavior.

meta.symbol: importerror when importing a plugin was hidden/silenced

text: usefixtures and autouse fixtures now work for running doctest text files: usefixtures and autouse fixtures now apply to doctest text files, which may cause fixtures to run in contexts where they previously did not.

meta.symbol: usefixtures and autouse fixtures now work for running doctest text files

text: --resultlog now only relies on longrepr instead of reprcrash: The --resultlog option now only uses longrepr instead of the 'reprcrash' attribute, which may change output format or break code parsing resultlog output.

meta.symbol: --resultlog now only relies on longrepr instead of reprcrash

text: @pytest.fixture(params=iterator) did not work with iterators: @pytest.fixture(params=iterator) now accepts iterators by exploding them into a list early, which changes behavior if the iterator was stateful or lazy.
nested parametrize-value markers were not allowed: Nested parametrize-value markers are now allowed, which may change how parametrization is resolved in complex cases.

meta.symbol: nested parametrize-value markers were not allowed

text: monkeypatch.setattr with import_path had unicode issues: monkeypatch.setattr(import_path, value) now handles unicode correctly, which may change behavior for code that relied on the broken unicode handling.

meta.symbol: monkeypatch.setattr with import_path had unicode issues

text: junitxml now handles unicode correctly: junitxml output now handles unicode correctly, which may change XML output format for tests with unicode characters.
The --runxfail flag now also turns imperative pytest.xfail() calls into no-ops, not just pytest.mark.xfail markers.

meta.symbol: --runxfail only neutralized pytest.mark.xfail markers, not imperative pytest.xfail() calls

text: The AssertionRewritingHook now registers properly with pkg_resources, which may affect how pkg_resources.resource_stream works and potentially break code relying on the old behavior.
pytestconfig fixture was not session-scoped: The pytestconfig fixture is now session-scoped instead of its previous scope, meaning it is the same object during the whole test run.

@pytest.mark.some(lambda arg: ...) did not work correctly: Using a lambda as argument to @pytest.mark.some now works correctly, whereas previously pytest assumed the argument was a function to be decorated.
varnames() did not deal with classes's __init__: The internal varnames() function now handles classes's __init__ methods and has fixed caching, which may affect introspection behavior.

meta.symbol: varnames() did not deal with classes's __init__

text: importing namespace-packages with no __init__.py did not work properly: Importing namespace packages without __init__.py now works properly, which may change behavior for namespace packages that previously failed to import.

meta.symbol: importing namespace-packages with no __init__.py did not work properly

text: Stop using pytest_plugin_unregister hook; it has been removed and was never properly called.
pytest.fixture-decorated functions could be generators (use yield): Stop using yield inside @pytest.fixture functions; they no longer support generators. Use @pytest.yield_fixture instead.

meta.symbol: pytest.fixture-decorated functions could be generators (use yield)

text: xunit-style teardown methods are not called if the corresponding setup method failed: Stop relying on teardown methods running when setup failed; all xunit-style teardown methods (nose-style, pytest-style, unittest-style) will not be called if the corresponding setup method failed.

meta.symbol: xunit-style teardown methods are not called if the corresponding setup method failed

text: --genscript produced a standalone script compatible with python 2.5+: Stop expecting --genscript from Python 2.7+ to produce a Python 2.5-compatible script; it now only works on Python 2.7 or above.

meta.symbol: --genscript produced a standalone script compatible with python 2.5+

text: --collectonly (underscore-separated): Stop using underscore-separated long options as the primary form; use hyphen-separated long options (e.g. --collect-only instead of --collectonly), though old spelling remains backward-compatible.
used optparse for command-line option parsing: pytest now uses argparse instead of optparse, which adds argparse as a dependency for Python 2.6 environments or below.

meta.symbol: used optparse for command-line option parsing

text: Remove implicit distribute_setup support from setup.py.
files without any doctest items showed up in test results: Doctest examples are counted as separate test items and files without any doctest items will not show up anymore.

meta.symbol: files without any doctest items showed up in test results

text: -k/-m options only match markers/test names/keywords respectively: The -k and -m options are cleaned up to only match markers/test names/keywords respectively.

meta.symbol: -k/-m options only match markers/test names/keywords respectively

text: Yielded test functions cannot accept fixtures as funcargs; use parametrize instead.

meta.symbol: yielded test functions cannot accept fixtures as funcargs

text: -k accepted special syntax like 'testclass.test_method' to match a method in a class: The -k option now uses expression syntax like -m, so matching a method in a class requires 'TestClass and test_method' instead of 'TestClass.test_method'.

meta.symbol: -k accepted special syntax like 'testclass.test_method' to match a method in a class

text: skip markers and pytest.skip() now take precedence over xfail markers: A skip-mark or pytest.skip() now takes precedence before xfail-markers; xfail/xpass status cannot be determined in case of a skip.

meta.symbol: skip markers and pytest.skip() now take precedence over xfail markers

text: pluginmanager.register() now raises ValueError if the plugin has already been registered or the name is taken, instead of silently succeeding.
reported as xpass (unexpected pass): A failing setup of an xfail-marked test is now reported as xfail instead of xpass.
request.keywords and node.keywords are now writable, and all descendant collection nodes will see keyword values.
final test's teardown runs immediately after the test: The final test in a test node now runs its teardown directly instead of waiting for the end of the session.

meta.symbol: final test's teardown runs immediately after the test

text: hooks now receive 'nextitem' parameter which is none for the final test: The pytest_runtest_protocol and pytest_runtest_teardown hooks now have 'nextitem' available which will be None indicating the end of the test run.

meta.symbol: hooks now receive 'nextitem' parameter which is none for the final test

text: teardown functions called earlier (eager tearing down): Teardown functions are now called eagerly after each test item instead of at the end of the session.

meta.symbol: teardown functions called earlier (eager tearing down)

text: assertions for test modules on python 2.6+ are now rewritten via ast and pyc file saved before import: Assertions in test modules on Python 2.6 and above are now rewritten by AST transformation and pyc caching before import, changing assertion semantics.

meta.symbol: assertions for test modules on python 2.6+ are now rewritten via ast and pyc file saved before import

text: skipif/xfail expressions can now be strings referencing module globals: Skipif/xfail conditions now accept string expressions that can access module globals, changing how conditions are evaluated.

meta.symbol: skipif/xfail expressions can now be strings referencing module globals

text: same-conftest detection that ignored conftest.py when they appear in several subdirs: Stop relying on 'same-conftest' detection; conftest.py files in subdirectories are no longer ignored.

meta.symbol: same-conftest detection that ignored conftest.py when they appear in several subdirs

text: setting command line options from conftest.py files: Stop setting command line options directly in conftest.py; use ini-files or the pytest_cmdline_processargs(args) hook instead.

meta.symbol: setting command line options from conftest.py files

text: yielded tests were setup as with pytest 1.3.4 (setup before collection): Yielded tests now follow collection-before-running semantics; setup no longer happens as it did in pytest 1.3.4.

meta.symbol: yielded tests were setup as with pytest 1.3.4 (setup before collection)

text: Stop using environment variables and dynamic conftest.py reading for config; use ini-style files (setup.cfg, tox.ini) instead.

meta.symbol: setting config options via environment variables and dynamic conftest.py reading

text: Stop using py.test.collect.Directory; it has been removed.
Sibling conftest.py files are no longer loaded, and you cannot customize Directory collectors from a Directory's conftest.py — do it at least one level up.

meta.symbol: sibling conftest.py files were loaded and directory collectors could be customized from a directory's conftest.py

text: xpass tests do not cause exitcode != 0: Unexpected pass (xpass) tests no longer cause a non-zero exit code.

meta.symbol: xpass tests do not cause exitcode != 0

text: mark.* objects were mutable: mark.* objects are now immutable — you must create new instances instead of mutating them.

conftest files with same content could be loaded multiple times in nested directory structures: Stop relying on conftest files with duplicate content being loaded multiple times; conftest loading now deduplicates files with identical content.

meta.symbol: conftest files with same content could be loaded multiple times in nested directory structures

text: x.bestrelpath(path) where x is a file: Stop calling path.bestrelpath() when X is a file; X must be a directory and the method now returns '.' when called on a directory.

meta.symbol: x.bestrelpath(path) where x is a file

text: The recwarn funcarg now enables warnings on Python 2.7, so existing code that relied on warnings being disabled may see new warning-related test behavior.

meta.symbol: recwarn funcarg may not have enabled warnings on python 2.7

text: --pdb ignores xfailed tests, unifies tb reporting, and does not display failures again at the end: The --pdb option no longer displays failures again at the end and ignores xfailed tests, so code relying on the old output format or xfail-pdb interaction will break.

meta.symbol: --pdb ignores xfailed tests, unifies tb reporting, and does not display failures again at the end

text: xfailed tests show 'x', xpassed tests show 'x', and xfail/xpass results show up as skipped in junitxml output: xfail/xpass results now show up as skipped tests in junitxml output, changing the junitxml schema for these test outcomes.

meta.symbol: xfailed tests show 'x', xpassed tests show 'x', and xfail/xpass results show up as skipped in junitxml output

text: py.test.cmdline.main() now returns the exitstatus instead of raising SystemExit, and can be called multiple times.
The --report option is deprecated in favour of the -r option which takes a string argument of 'xfsX' characters.

setup_module/class functions were called multiple times if they raised an exception (including skipped), and teardown was not always called after failed setup: setup_module/class functions are now called only once instead of multiple times on exception, and teardown functions are always called even if the corresponding setup failed

meta.symbol: setup_module/class functions were called multiple times if they raised an exception (including skipped), and teardown was not always called after failed setup

text: looponfailing has been moved out of py.test core into the separately released pytest-xdist plugin

meta.symbol: looponfailing was part of py.test core

text: disabled attribute on test items: Stop using the 'disabled' attribute on test items; use pytestmark instead.
Stop defining Directory, Module, Class and Function nodes in conftest.py files; use pytest collect hooks instead.

meta.symbol: defining directory, module, class, function nodes in conftest.py files

text: first hook to return something prevents further hooks from being called: The first pytest_collect_directory hook to return a value now prevents any further hooks from being called.

meta.symbol: first hook to return something prevents further hooks from being called

text: figleaf plugin ran automatically: The figleaf plugin now requires the --figleaf command-line flag to be activated; it no longer runs automatically.
doctest plugin is now enabled by default with --doctest-glob option: The pytest doctest plugin is now enabled by default, which may cause doctests to run unexpectedly in existing projects.

meta.symbol: doctest plugin is now enabled by default with --doctest-glob option

text: internal py._* helper variables (except py._pydir): All internal py._* helper variables except py._pydir have been removed.
py.rest tool and internal namespace: The py.rest tool and its internal namespace have been removed.
Stop using parser.addgroup and use getgroup instead to create option groups.
Stop using py.execnet code and use the 'execnet' package directly instead.
Stop using py.compat.doctest, py.compat.subprocess, py.compat.textwrap, and py.compat.optparse as they are deprecated.
Stop using py.magic.autopath as it is deprecated and the py/magic directory has been removed.
py.magic.*: Stop using py.magic namespaces and use py.code namespaces instead for assertion handling.
Stop using conf_capture in conftest.py and use option_capture instead.
test-prefixed classes with __init__ are not collected by default: Stop relying on Test-prefixed classes that have an __init__ method being collected; they are now skipped by default.

meta.symbol: test-prefixed classes with __init__ are not collected by default

text: item.repr_failure(excinfo, outerr): Stop passing the outerr argument to item.repr_failure(); it now only accepts excinfo.
Stop using py.test.xfail; use py.test.mark.xfail instead.
Stop passing a scope argument to request.addfinalizer(); it has been removed. Use request.cached_setup for scoped setup instead.
Stop defining plugin classes; instead define hooks directly in conftest.py or global pytest_*.py files.
py.test.broken and py.test.notimplemented helpers: Stop using py.test.broken and py.test.notimplemented as they have been removed.
Stop relying on the lock that was previously required for atomic reporting issue display in py.test; it has been removed.
Stop relying on globals in execnet; they have been removed.
py.test did not exit with code 1 when there were failures: py.test now exits with code 1 when there are test failures; code that assumed a different exit code on failure will break.

meta.symbol: py.test did not exit with code 1 when there were failures

text: autouse fixtures defined inline in test modules with module/package/session scope executed once under --doctest-modules: Autouse fixtures with module, package, or session scope defined inline in Python test modules (not conftests) may execute twice when using --doctest-modules.

meta.symbol: autouse fixtures defined inline in test modules with module/package/session scope executed once under --doctest-modules

text: using non-collection iterables (generators, iterators, custom iterables) for argvalues: Stop using non-Collection iterables (generators, iterators, custom iterables) for the argvalues parameter in @pytest.mark.parametrize and metafunc.parametrize; they get exhausted and cause skipped tests.

meta.symbol: using non-collection iterables (generators, iterators, custom iterables) for argvalues

text: Stop passing baseid to FixtureDef or nodeid strings to fixture registration APIs; use the node parameter instead. For nodeid=None, pass node=session.

meta.symbol: passing baseid to fixturedef or nodeid strings to fixture registration apis

text: pytest.warns now shows 'Regex pattern did not match' instead of 'DID NOT WARN' when warnings were emitted but the match pattern did not match.
When using --setup-show, a space is now printed after the test name to separate it from the test result.

meta.symbol: a space is now printed after the test name to separate it from the test result

module-level __getattr__ returning none for pytestmark caused cryptic typeerror: got none instead of mark: When a module-level __getattr__ returns None for pytestmark, pytest now emits a PytestCollectionWarning instead of raising a TypeError, which changes error handling behaviour for code that relied on the TypeError.

text: pytest.raises(match=...) failures now suppress the mismatched exception as a cause of the resulting AssertionError, changing the exception chain.

text: Blocking a conftest.py file using the -p no: option is now disallowed and raises UsageError instead of causing an internal assertion failure.

text: When using pytest.mark.parametrize([], ids=idfunc), the idfunc will no longer be called with internal objects.

Stop using the :pull: RST role; use :pr: instead.
extlinks sphinx extension (and its :bpo: role): Stop using the :bpo: RST role; use :issue: with the GitHub issue number instead, as the extlinks extension is no longer enabled.

text: collection_path parameter: Stop using the 'path' parameter in pytest_ignore_collect hooks; use 'collection_path' instead.
file_path parameter: Stop using the 'path' parameter in pytest_collect_file hooks; use 'file_path' instead.
module_path parameter: Stop using the 'path' parameter in pytest_pycollect_makemodule hooks; use 'module_path' instead.
start_path parameter: Stop using the 'startdir' parameter in pytest_report_header hooks; use 'start_path' instead.
start_path parameter: Stop using the 'startdir' parameter in pytest_report_collectionfinish hooks; use 'start_path' instead.

text: Python 3.7 is no longer supported; upgrade to Python 3.8 or later.
PytestPluginManager.consider_conftest has a new parameter; external code calling this private API will break.

package was a subtype of pytest.module (representing __init__.py): Stop treating Package as a Module subclass; Package is now a Directory subclass and its path points to the directory, not __init__.py.

text: Package.path now returns the directory path, not the __init__.py file path.

parser.addini now returns type-specific defaults (e.g. False for bool, '' for str) when default is not passed, and honors default=None; previously it returned empty list/string inconsistently and ignored None.

text: Test functions returning a value other than None now issue pytest.PytestWarning instead of pytest.PytestRemovedIn8Warning, meaning this will remain a warning and never become an error.
silently ignored (no warning): Applying a mark to a fixture function now issues a warning; marks on fixtures never had any effect, and this will become an error in pytest 9.0.
colliding ids like a0, a1, a0: Parametrized test node IDs are now guaranteed unique; previously colliding IDs are renamed, which may break test selection references or recorded test names.

text: The third item of FixtureDef.cached_result (cached_result[2]) is now an exception instance instead of an exception triplet; any code accessing cached_result[2] expecting a tuple will break.

pytest.warns and similar functions now capture warnings even when an exception is raised inside the with block, which may cause previously-passing tests to fail if they expected no warnings on exception paths.

exceptions raised in __iter__ methods produced an unhelpful error message from assertion rewrite mechanism: Stop relying on assertion rewrite producing an error message when __iter__ raises; objects raising in __iter__ are now treated as un-iterable.

text: Stop relying on pytest depending on the py library; install py separately if you need py.* modules beyond py.error and py.path.
Stop using pytest_warning_captured hook and use pytest_warning_recorded instead.
Stop using -k -foobar syntax and use -k 'not foobar' instead.
Stop using the -k foobar: syntax entirely as it has been removed.
Stop importing from pytest.collect module and import from pytest directly instead.
Python 3.6 is no longer supported; upgrade to Python 3.7 or later.
The plugin previously named 'pythonpath' has been renamed to 'python_path' to avoid conflict with the pytest-pythonpath plugin.
pyreadline workaround for --pdb: Stop relying on pyreadline working with --pdb; the workaround has been dropped.

pytest.warns(none) used to check that code emits at least one warning of any type: Stop using pytest.warns(None); it was commonly misunderstood to mean 'no warnings' but actually checked for at least one warning of any type.

text: The path property of Code and TracebackEntry now returns a pathlib.Path object instead of py.path.local, which will break code that expects py.path.local methods.

only had 'path' parameter (py.path.local): The pytest_ignore_collect hook now receives an additional 'collection_path' (pathlib.Path) parameter alongside the existing 'path' parameter; hooks using only positional arguments may break.

only had 'path' parameter (py.path.local): The pytest_collect_file hook now receives an additional 'file_path' (pathlib.Path) parameter alongside the existing 'path' parameter; hooks using only positional arguments may break.

only had 'path' parameter (py.path.local): The pytest_pycollect_makemodule hook now receives an additional 'module_path' (pathlib.Path) parameter alongside the existing 'path' parameter; hooks using only positional arguments may break.

only had 'startdir' parameter (py.path.local): The pytest_report_header hook now receives an additional 'start_path' (pathlib.Path) parameter alongside the existing 'startdir' parameter; hooks using only positional arguments may break.

only had 'startdir' parameter (py.path.local): The pytest_report_collectionfinish hook now receives an additional 'start_path' (pathlib.Path) parameter alongside the existing 'startdir' parameter; hooks using only positional arguments may break.
node.fspath (py.path.local): Stop using Node.fspath; use Node.path (a pathlib.Path) instead.
Stop using the old 'toml' parser; switch to 'tomli' for TOML v1.0.0 support in pyproject.toml.
240 characters (no -v/-vv effect): Assert error string truncation threshold is now 240 characters by default, increased to 2400 with -v, and disabled entirely with -vv or higher.
callspec2._arg2scopenum attribute: Stop accessing the private `CallSpec2._arg2scopenum` attribute as it has been removed.

directories are created with private permissions, and reusing a preexisting directory owned by another user results in an error: Temporary directories under /tmp are now created with private permissions, and pytest will error instead of silently reusing a preexisting /tmp/pytest-of-<username> directory owned by another user.

Stop calling gethookproxy() and isinitpath() on FSCollector or Package instances; use self.session.gethookproxy() and self.session.isinitpath() instead.

builtin attributes (like __class__, __delattr__, __new__) were checked against python_classes/python_filters and passed to pytest_pycollect_makeitem hook: pytest now ignores builtin attributes (like __class__, __delattr__, __new__) during test collection without consulting python_classes/python_functions config or passing them to the pytest_pycollect_makeitem hook.

text: comparing source with a str: Stop comparing Source objects with strings; that comparison is no longer supported.
source.isparseable() and source.putaround() methods: Stop calling Source.isparseable() and Source.putaround(); these methods have been removed.
Stop using Source.compile() and _pytest._code.compile(); use plain compile() instead.
Stop using _pytest._code.source.getsource(); use Source() directly instead.

when using pytest.fixture on a function directly (pytest.fixture(func)) with autouse or params arguments, the function was ignored: Stop relying on pytest.fixture(func) silently ignoring autouse or params arguments; the function is now treated as a fixture.

accepted multidimensional inputs: BSpline methods now raise an error if inputs have ndim > 1.

parsed correctly (left associative): Python ternary if expressions (`x if y else z`) in torch.jit.script are no longer parsed as right associative, which may change behavior of nested ternary expressions

Replace the parameter name `unwrapped_params` with `nonwrapped_numel` in lambda_auto_wrap_policy, transformer_auto_wrap_policy, and size_based_auto_wrap_policy.
Replace keyword argument names `output` and `input` with `output_tensors` and `input_tensors` in the alltoall collective API.

backendpatternconfig((nn.relu, (nn.batchnorm2d, nn.conv2d))): Stop passing patterns as nested tuples to BackendPatternConfig; pass them as a flat tuple of modules in execution order (conv, bn, relu) instead.
backend_config.configs now returns a list of BackendPatternConfig objects instead of a dict keyed by pattern.

overwrite_output_observer / overwrite_output_fake_quantize in backendconfig: Stop using overwrite_output_observer and overwrite_output_fake_quantize in BackendConfig; observer constraints for fixed qparams ops are now represented through the existing DTypeWithConstraints mechanism.

logs a warning and skips quantization of the fixed qparams op: Using wrong observers for fixed qparams ops no longer raises an exception; it now only logs a warning and the fixed qparams ops will not be quantized.

text: torch::deploy in pytorch core: Stop using torch::deploy from PyTorch Core; it has been migrated to the MultiPy repository.
lazy::view view and aliasing infrastructure: Stop using lazy::View; the view and aliasing infrastructure in lazy tensor core has been deprecated in favor of functionalization.
Stop using c10::fromIntArrayRef; it has been renamed to c10::fromIntArrayRefSlow.

at::frobenius_norm(const tensor&) overload: The tensor overload at::frobenius_norm(const Tensor&) has been removed; it was not used in PyTorch bindings.
torch._six (string_classes, int_classes, inf, nan): torch._six has been removed; replace string_classes with str, int_classes with int, and import inf/nan from torch.
